name: Aladdin
description:
I am a charmer and will want fuss from most people even strangers.

I need a prospective adopter to visit me a couple of time or more to get to know me before I can go on a home trial

I cannot be placed in a property with no direct access to a private garden.

I need a home that is at ease with my kind of dog, who have a good understanding and experience of dog behaviour.

description:
I need a prospective adopter to visit me a couple of time or more to get to know me before I can go on a home trial

I need a home that is at ease with my kind of dog, who have a good understanding and experience of dog behaviour.

I have been through a lot and need a home who has dog experience and wants to offer a home to a real Underdog

I cannot be placed in a property with no direct access to a private garden.

I need a prospective adopter to visit me a couple of time or more to get to know me before I can go on a home trial

I need a home that is at ease with my kind of dog, who have a good understanding and experience of dog behaviour.

I need to be placed in a home who have another dog or other dogs.

I would like a home that does not require me to deal with heavy traffic and stimulations.

I cannot be placed in a property with no direct access to a private garden.

I need a prospective adopter to visit me a couple of time or more to get to know me before I can go on a home trial

I need a home that is at ease with my kind of dog, who have a good understanding and experience of dog behaviour.

description:
I cannot be placed in a property with no direct access to a private garden.

I need a prospective adopter to visit me a couple of time or more to get to know me before I can go on a home trial

I need a home that is at ease with my kind of dog, who have a good understanding and experience of dog behaviour.

name: Ringo
description:
Ringo has previously lived in a home environment so we can provide some information to potential adopters. Ringo is a typical terrier and is after an active home with a family that is willing to put in time to train him further. He would thrive in a home where someone was around a lot to take him on plenty of walks and provide him with lots of enrichment to keep his brain stimulated. He would be a great dog to take part in activities such as scent work or man/pet trailing. Ringo is very excitable on the lead so could do with some training to help him in this area. Experience and knowledge of the breed would be beneficial.

Ringo has never lived with children before so we think that it would be best for him to be rehomed with kids over the age of 12 years – Subject to a successful meet.

Ringo has interacted well with dogs whilst in our care, he can be a little unsure at first so could do with some socialising to improve his confidence. He could possibly be rehomed with another dog of a similar temperament – subject to a successful meet.

Ringo can’t be rehomed with cats. It’s recommended that there are no cats in nearby properties either.

name: Stevie
description:
Meet Stevie.  This lovely girl is looking for a forever home where she can enjoy all the love and attention she deserves.  She is a sweet, friendly girl with a wonderful nature who absolutely loves human company.  If you’re happy to dish out plenty of belly rubs, bum scratches and fuss, you’ll quickly become her favourite person!  She loves affection and is happiest when she’s close to her people.  She is a pleasure to take out and walks nicely on her lead, making her an enjoyable companion for leisurely strolls.  She is also well behaved at the vets, taking examinations and handling in her stride.  She does currently have Cherry Eye, this will be repaired before the adoption takes place.  She gets on well with other dogs but does prefer calmer canine companions who match her relaxed temperament.  We do feel she could live with another calm dog following a successful meet and greet.  As with all Bulldogs, an understanding of the breed and its specific needs would be a real advantage.  Her new adopters should be aware of the care and consideration Bulldogs require to help her live a happy, healthy life.  If you are looking for a loving, loyal companion with a lovely personality and lots of affection to give, this gorgeous girl could be the perfect addition to your family.

As Stevie came into the home as a stray, we have no previous history on her so would prefer any children in the home to be 12+ years.

name: Rosa
description:
Meet Rosa!  She is a beautiful and gentle soul who is searching for a calm, loving home where she can enjoy her golden years.  When you first meet Rosa, she can be timid and nervous around new people and unfamiliar surroundings.  She needs time to feel safe, so her new adopter will need to be patient and allow her to build trust at her own pace.  Once she knows you, Rosa’s sweet, friendly nature begins to shine through, and she forms lovely bonds with those who give her the time she needs.  Rosa would thrive in a quiet retirement home with a calm routine and understanding owners who appreciate that the best relationships are worth waiting for.  She will need several meet and greets before the adoption can take place to ensure she feels comfortable with her new adopter. She has socialised well with other dogs whilst in our care so we feel she could possibly live with another calm, older dog following a successful meet and greet. If you have a peaceful home, a kind heart, and the patience to let Rosa blossom in her own time, you will be rewarded with the companionship of a truly lovely girl who deserves to spend her retirement surrounded by love.

As she came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on her and as she can be a little timid around new people we would like any children in the home to be 16+ years.

name: Onion
description:
Meet Onion!  Onion is a very special boy who has come such a long way since arriving in our care.  He was extremely nervous when he first came to us and was too frightened for us to enter his kennel, often retreating to the back and growling out of fear.  With time, patience and gentle encouragement, onion has slowly started to show us the lovely dog hiding underneath.  True to his name, Onion has layers.  The more time you spend with him, the more of his wonderful personality shines through.  Once he trusts you and he gets out of his kennel, he completely transforms.  He becomes affectionate, loves climbing on your lap for cuddles, enjoys playing with his toys and is even quite vocal when he is happy.  He has also been fantastic with other dogs he has met whilst in our care.  Onion is looking for a calm, patient home with owners who understand that building trust will take time.  He will need several meet and greets to help him feel comfortable before going home, and his new family must be committed to helping him continue to overcome his fear of new people.

Patient, understanding owners willing to let Onion progress at his own pace. Watching Onion gain confidence has been incredibly rewarding, and we know that with the right family he will continue to blossom into the amazing companion we know he can be.

name: Raven
description:
Raven is a gentle, sweet-natured girl looking for a patient and understanding home where she can build her confidence at her own pace.

She has been no trouble in our care and has no issues with feeding or cleaning. When she first arrived, she was extremely nervous and wouldn’t approach for attention. By day five she had made small steps, coming over for a sniff, but she is still very worried about being stroked and is understandably wary of new people, often keeping her tail tucked between her legs.

As Raven came into our care as a stray, we don’t know what she may have experienced in the past, but it’s clear she will need kind, committed owners who are willing to give her the time, patience and reassurance she needs to flourish.

Although she has coped around other dogs in our environment, she is very unsure of them and would be happiest as the only dog in the home, where she can have her own space and settle without added pressure.

With time, patience and plenty of love, Raven has every chance of growing in confidence and becoming a wonderful, loyal companion.

name: Dusty
description:
Dusty is a calm, gentle and affectionate girl looking for a loving home. Although she can be a little nervous when first meeting new people, she quickly comes out of her shell and soon seeks out fuss, cuddles and company.

She has no issues with feeding, cleaning, handling or grooming, happily sitting still while being brushed and allowing herself to be touched all over. Dusty knows some basic commands and enjoys spending time with people more than anything else – she’s happiest simply sitting beside you and soaking up the attention.

Dusty has paired well with other dogs in our care and is happy to walk alongside them, showing lovely social manners. She can be a little fussy with dry food and prefers wet food, but otherwise she’s an easy-going, sweet-natured girl.

Dusty would be best suited to a home withchildren aged 14 years and over, who will understand that she may need a little time to build confidence when meeting new people.

If you’re looking for a gentle, loving companion who will reward your patience with endless affection, Dusty could be the perfect addition to your family. ❤️

name: Marshall
description:
This handsome boy is full of life and absolutely loves people! Friendly, affectionate, and always ready for attention, he has a big personality and an even bigger heart.

He can be very excitable and still behaves very much like a puppy, so he’ll need owners who are committed to continuing his training. He is strong on the lead and pulls when walking, but with consistency and patience he is eager to learn. Once he settles, he enjoys being stroked and is happy to be handled all over.

He is looking for an active home that can give him the time, guidance, and love he deserves to help him become the best companion he can be.

Suitable for children aged 14+due to his size, strength, and bouncy nature.

name: Prism
description:
Prism is an ex racing greyhound that has come into our care after retiring. Prism is looking for a home to call her own after always living in kennels. Prism is a sweet and gentle girl who enjoys attention and a cuddle. Prism walks lovely on the lead but can still react to certain wildlife due to her prey drive. Prism has never lived in a home before so will need a patient family that is willing to give her time to settle and adjust to her new surroundings.

Prism has interacted well with dogs whilst in our care and has had a kennel mate previously. Due to this prism can be rehomed with another dog of similar size – subject to a successful meet. Prism isn’t yet spade so any male dogs would have to be castrated.

Prism has interacted well with staff and volunteers so we believe that she can be rehomed with children who are 12 years and over – Subject to a successful meet.

name: Elias
description:
Elias is a very sweet and friendly boy. He came into our care as a stray so we don’t have any background information on him. He has always been friendly with staff and volunteers whilst here. Elias can be a little strong on the lead due to his size so some basic lead training would be beneficial for him. We are looking for an active home for Elias as he has a lot of energy and would enjoy going on lots of adventures. Elias is a calm boy in his kennel and loves to have some down time with a tasty chew.

Elias is a friendly boy but due to his size we believe that it would be best for him to be rehomed with children over 14 years and over – Subject to a successful meet.

Elias has interacted well with dogs whilst in our care, he has a submissive nature around other dogs. We believe that Elias can be rehomed with another dog with the same energy levels.

name: Jasper
description:
Jasper is an ex-racing greyhound that has spent all of his life in kennels. Jasper is now ready for a comfortable and cosy forever home to call his own. Jasper is a friendly and loveable boy who likes attention and a cuddle. Jasper has a high prey drive still so does pull when he sees ducks, birds and wildlife when out on walks. Jasper has never lived in a home before so he will need time to settle and get used to unfamiliar sounds and objects.

Jasper has interacted well with dogs and is currently paired with another greyhound so we believe that he can be rehomed with another dog of a similar size – Subject to a successful meet.

Jasper is a sweet boy so can potentially be rehomed with children who are 12 years and over – Subject to a successful meet.

name: Tilly
description:
Tilly is a super sweet girl who loves attention and a fuss. She can be a little wary of new people at first but comes around quite quickly and will then be your best friend. We are unsure of her history so we think that she is going to need some more training to improve her manners. Tilly is a strong girl on the lead so basic lead training will also be needed. Tilly loves her walks but due to her breed she isn’t going to want to go on big hikes. Tilly does love enrichment that you can use to tire out her brain in between walks.

Tilly has interacted well with dogs whilst in our care and does like to play with other dogs, due to this we believe that she could potentially be rehomed with another dog -subject to a successful meet.

We think that Tilly can be rehomed with children who are 12 years and over – subject to a successful meet.

name: Teddy
description:
Teddy isn’t a typical yorkie, he is a strong independent boy who doesn’t live up to his name or size. He isn’t a big fan of being picked up or handled too much, unless its on his terms. Teddy likes going on walks but isn’t very keen on having a lead on so could do with some training so he can become more confident with this. Teddy loves his food, treats and enrichment. Teddy is going to need to have regular grooms to keep his coat healthy, he will need some training in this area so that he becomes more confident being groomed. Teddy is going to need a calm home where he can have time to settle and adjust to his new environment.

Teddy has walked nicely with other dogs and can interact well with them whilst on walks. But we believe that it would be best for him to be the only dog in the home as he can be over the top with dogs when it comes to living with them.

Teddy needs to gain more confident around being handled so we believe it would be best that he is rehomed to an adult only home.

name: Hunter
description:
If you’re looking for a loyal best friend, this handsome boy could be the one for you. He has a heart full of love and greets everyone with a wagging tail and a face that lights up with excitement. Affectionate, gentle, and eager to please, all he really wants is to be by your side.

He walks well on the lead, enjoys being groomed, knows basic commands such as sit, wait, and down, and is happy to be handled all over. He has also shown friendly, positive interactions with other dogs in our care.

Hunter could live with children aged 12 and over who confident and respectful around dogs. This sweet boy would thrive in a home where he can finally enjoy the love, attention, and companionship he deserves. In return, he’ll give you endless affection and be a wonderful, devoted companion.

name: Drago
description:
Drago is an ex-racer that has come straight to us from the racing kennels. Due to always being in kennels Drago will need time to settle and get used to every day life in the home. With patience and love greyhounds make a perfect companion to any household.

Drago is a lively boy as he is still quite young and has a high prey drive. He would love a family that could take him out on lots of adventures to burn off his energy. Drago would benefit from some socialisation with new environments. He is a friendly boy with all of the staff and volunteers here on site and loves to give out kisses.

Drago can be rehomed with children over the age of 12 years old and can potentially be rehomed with another dog of a similar size and temperament – subject to a successful meet and greet.

Do you think you could give this handsome boy his forever home?

name: Dakota
description:
Dakota is looking for her forever home, and she has so much love to give.  She’s an incredibly sweet, friendly girl who absolutely adores people.  If you’re happy to give her plenty of fuss, she’ll be in her element!  Despite her size, she is a gentle soul with a lovely nature and enjoys spending time with her favourite humans.  She already knows some basic commands and is very eager to please so would benefit from further training.  Being a young, strong dog, she can be powerful on the lead, so her new adopter will need to be confident and committed to continue her lead training.  Because of this, she will be best suited to an active home where she can enjoy plenty of exercise, adventures and mental stimulation.  If you are looking for a loyal, affectionate companion with a heart as big as she is, this lovely girl could be the one for you.  She will need a private secure garden.

She has socialised with other dogs whilst in our care, and is very playful and can be too much for other dogs as she does get in their face so for this reason we feel she would be best rehomed as the only dog in the home where she can continue to learn manners and boundaries around other dogs will the full support of her new adopter with lots of positive reinforcement.

As Dakota came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on her so would prefer any children in the home to be 14+ years.

name: Toki
description:
This gorgeous girl is Toki! She is looking for her forever home where she can continue to flourish.  At just 18 months old, she is full of life, love and endless enthusiasm.  She’s a very sweet and friendly girl.  She can be a little unsure when meeting new people, but with a little patience, she soon comes out of her shell and is more than ready for plenty of fuss.  Like many young Staffies, she’s full of energy and absolutely loves to play.  Toys are one of her favourite things, and she’s always up for a game.  She’s a fun, giddy girl who will keep you smiling with her playful personality.  She is still learning her manners and doesn’t quite understand personal space.  She loves attention and will jump up to say hello.  She’s also discovered that food on tables is very tempting and won’t think twice about jumping on the table and helping herself if given the chance!  Her new adopter will need to be committed to continuing her training and setting clear, consistent boundaries. She can be strong on the lead, so she will benefit from ongoing lead training and an active home that can provide plenty of exercise, enrichment and positive guidance.  She will need a private secure garden.

Toki has socialised  with other dogs whilst in our care and is very playful and giddy and doesn’t really know when to stop.  We do feel she could potentially live with another dog that would tolerate her full-on behaviour following a successful meet and greet. (must be neutered).

As she came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on her so would prefer any children in the home to be 14+ years.

name: Buster
description:
Buster is a typical young bulldog – he’s full of energy and always ready to cause mischief. Buster is a super happy and playful dog. When he arrived in our care he was very confused and quiet but now he’s settled his personality is showing. Buster loves off lead time and zooming around the compound playing with toys. He is a big foodie and loves his treats and enrichment! We believe that Buster would benefit from some on going training to help him thrive. Buster’s perfect home would be with someone who is active and has free time to play with him and ensure that he has lots of adventures and mental stimulation throughout the day. Someone who has experience and knowledge of the breed would also be beneficial to help Bruce develop into the perfect companion.

Bruce can be rehomed with dog savy children who are 16 years and over and are used to boisterous dogs – subject to a successful meet.

Bruce has interacted well with dogs whilst in our care so could potentially be rehomed with another dog, that is also young and active and is willing to put up with his energy levels.

name: Sparky
description:
This little character is Sparky!  He is looking for an understanding and committed home where he can continue to learn and build his confidence.  We think he has had very little socialisation in his early life, so he can be extremely nervous when meeting new people and experiencing unfamiliar situations.  However, once he gets to know and trust you, he is a sweet, affectionate and friendly boy who enjoys lots of fuss.  He is a lively young lad with plenty of energy and loves to play fetch.  One of his favourite things is carrying his toys around with him, and will proudly come over to show what he has got.  As he lacks basic training, he does not currently know how to walk on a lead and will require patient, positive reinforcement training to help him learn.  He can be quite barky at times, especially when unsure of situations, and he does jump up a lot when he gets excited.  When over stimulated, he can become a bit nippy.  He is also know to nip at ankles when the staff leave his kennel, so any potential adopter must be prepared to work on his impulse control and training.  Due to these behaviours we would like any children in the home to be 12+ years.  This lovely boy came into us after being found confined in a crate with 2 other dogs, so it looks like he has missed out on many of the normal experiences and training opportunities that young dogs should have.  With time, patience, consistency and kindness, this loveable youngster has the potential to become a wonderful companion.  He would benefit from a private secure garden.  He will need an experienced owner.

He has socialised with other dogs whilst in our care but he does prefer his own space so we feel he would be best rehomed as the only dog in the home.

name: Scout
description:
This handsome boy is Scout!  He has had a difficult start in life after being found dumped in a cage with 2 other dogs.  Despite everything he has been through, he is beginning to show what a lovely little character he really is.  When meeting new people, he can be very nervous and tends to be quite vocal.  He needs time to build trust and feel safe in unfamiliar situations.  However, once he has got to know you, his sweet and affectionate nature shines through.  Like many young dogs that have missed out on important early experiences, he can become a little over excited and can be nippy when jumping up for attention, ongoing training and clear boundaries will help him learn more appropriate ways to interact.  Initially he was very scared of the lead, but with gentle encouragement and patience he is making good progress when out on walks. He will need a home committed to continuing his confidence-building journey and helping him learn that the world isn’t such a scary place. He is looking for a calm, patient home that understands nervous dogs and is willing to give him the time to settle in.  In return, you will gain a loyal, affectionate companion who simply wants to feel safe, loved and part of the family.  He would benefit from a private secure garden so he can safely have some off lead time.

He has socialised well with other dogs whilst in our care, so we feel, following a successful meet and greet he could possibly live with another small dog.

As Scout can be very nervous and unsure we would like any children in the home to be 14+ years.

name: Samuel
description:
This handsome boy is Samuel!  He is a fun-loving, affectionate lad who is looking for an active home. He is an incredibly friendly and sweet-natured boy who loves being around people and enjoys plenty of fuss and attention.  As a typical young dog, he has lots of energy and enthusiasm, so he’ll thrive with owners who can give him plenty of exercise, playtime, mental stimulation and continued training.  He loves nothing more than a game of fetch and will happily spend time chasing his favourite toys.  He’s always ready for an adventure and would make a fantastic companion for someone with an active lifestyle.  Being a young dog, he can become overexcited and will jump up to greet people.  He can also be a little nippy during these excited moments, so he’ll need patient owners who are committed to helping him learn calmer manners through positive reinforcement.  He is a strong boy on the lead and would benefit from further lead training.  In his kennel, he does chase the hose and brushes – it’s all part of his playful, cheeky personality.  This lovely lad is looking for a home that understands young, energetic dogs and is willing to invest the time and training he will need.  He will need a private secure garden.

He has socialised with other dogs whilst in our care and can be a little nervous and unsure at first, so we would like him to be the only dog in the home where he can slowly build up his confidence around other dogs with the full support of his new adopter.

As he came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on him and as he can be a little nippy when he is overexcited we would like any children in the home to be 14+ years.

name: Bruce
description:
This handsome boy is Bruce!  He is a sweet-natured lad with plenty of love to give.  Full of character and enthusiasm, he absolutely loves playtime and can often be found carrying his favourite toy around or trying to persuade you into a game of fetch.  When he is excited, he can become quite giddy and mouthy, so he would benefit from adopters who understand his playful nature and can continue to guide him with calm, consistent boundaries.  Despite his excitable side, he already knows his basic commands and enjoys learning and engaging with people. He thrives on companionship and would make a wonderful addition to an active home that can provide him with lots of walks, adventures, enrichment and affection.  He generally prefers the company of female dogs and can sometimes be a little dominant around male dogs, so for this reason we feel he could be rehomed with a female dog following a successful meet and greet.

This loveable boy has so much to offer and is ready to find a family who will appreciate his fun-loving personality and loyal nature.  If you’re looking for a playful, affectionate companion with plenty of zest for life, he could be the one for you.

As Bruce came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on him so would prefer any children in the home to be 14+ years.

name: Coco
description:
Coco came into our care as a stray so we don’t have any background information on her. She is a sweet but shy girl at times. She does get excitable once she gets to know you. With time and patience she will be a great companion for any home. She loves her walks and going outside for plenty of sniffs. As she came in as a stray, she would benefit from some basic training to help her flourish.

Coco can be rehomed with children 12+ – subject to a successful meet.

Coco has interacted well with dogs whilst in our care but can be a little nervous at first. We believe that she could potentially be rehomed with another dog of a similar size and with a calm temperament.

name: Pink
description:
This lovely girl is Pink! She is a friendly, affectionate girl who is sure to seal your heart.  She absolutely loves human company and enjoys plenty of fuss and attention, with bum scratches being one of her favourite things in the world! She has a playful side and loves chasing around with her toys, especially if it squeaks.  She’s always ready for a game and enjoys interacting with people.  Water is another of her passions, and unfortunately does chase the hose whilst the staff are trying to clean, but once the cleaning is done she loves to splash about in our paddling pool.  She can be quite vocal in her kennel, especially when she is looking for attention, but once out she does settle down.  She does know her basic commands and walks nicely on the lead, enjoying her outings making her a wonderful companion.  This sweet, playful girl is ready to find a home where she can enjoy plenty of affection, playtime and adventures.

Pink has socialised with other dogs whilst in our care, although she does bark at first, once she has had a sniff she soon loses interest.  For this reason, we think she would best to be rehomed as the only dog in the home where she can get 100% of the attention.

As Pink came into the home as a stray, we have no previous history on her so would prefer any children in the home to be 16+ years.

name: Jax
description:
This stunning boy is Jax! we are looking for an experienced home for this fantastic lad.  He is a friendly and affectionate boy who absolutely loves attention and enjoys a good fuss.  He has a wonderful nature and is always happy to spend time with his people. This clever lad is exceptionally well behaved at the vets, taking examinations and handling in his stride, which is a testament to his temperament and trainability.  Like many dogs, he has his own little quirks – one of his favourites is enthusiastically ragging his blankets and rearranging them to his liking!  As a typical Belgian Malinois, he is highly intelligent, energetic and thrives on both physical exercise and mental stimulation.  He loves charging around our compound and would be happiest in a home where he has plenty of opportunities to stay active and engaged.  Because of his breed traits, he will need an owner who has previous experience with Belgian Malinois and understands their needs.  He is not a dog that will be content with a short walk around the block, he requires ongoing training, enrichment and a lifestyle that allows him to use both his body and brain.  He will need a private secure garden.

He has socialised well with other dogs whilst in our care, so we feel he could possibly be rehomed with another dog (similar size) following a successful meet and greet.

As Jax came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on him so would prefer any children in the home to be 16+ years.

name: Sunny
description:
Sunny is a strong happy boy who loves attention and a cuddle off all of the staff and volunteers he meets. He has a lot of energy so can be boisterous at times so his new family will need to do some basic training with him so that he can learn some manners. He loves his walks, toys and treats! He’s a happy go lucky guy who is looking for his new forever home and a comfy sofa to sleep on. Sunny would benefit from an enclosed garden that he can have off lead time in to burn off some energy in between walks.

Due to his boisterous behaviour we believe that Sunny would be best suited to be rehomed with children who are 16 years and over – subject to a successful  meet.

Sunny has interacted well with dogs whilst in our care but can be full on for some dogs. We think that if the other dog is a similar size or over and can match his energy levels he could potentially be rehomed with another dog – subject to a successful meet.

name: Loki
description:
Meet Loki! This handsome young boy is just 10 months old and is looking for a patient and understanding home to help him continue growing in confidence.  He can be timid and nervous when meeting new people, but with a little time, kindness and reassurance he soon comes around and starts to show his true personality.  His adopters will need to continue building his confidence in new situations through positive reinforcement and gentle encouragement.  Once he feels comfortable, he is a fun-loving, giddy youngster who is full of energy and enthusiasm.  He loves to play, enjoys interaction, and can get quite excitable.  He is known to jump up for attention, so ongoing training and consistency will help him learn good manners.  As you would expect from a young dog, he is still learning.  He can be vocal in his kennel and is currently very strong on the lead, so he will benefit from further lead training and guidance from his new adopter.  With the right home willing to invest time in his training and confidence building, this sweet young lad has all the makings of a fantastic companion. He would benefit from a private secure garden with an experienced owner.

Loki is very under-socialised around other dogs and is quite unsure around them so we feel he would be best rehomed as the only dog in the home where his new adopter can slowly introduce him to other dogs in a controlled environment with lots of positive reinforcement.  Puppy training classes would be great for him.

As Loki came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on him and as he can be a little unsure around new people we would prefer any children in the home to be 16+ years.

name: Buddy
description:
Meet our little gentleman Buddy!  Unfortunately Buddy is Blind.  As he came into us as a stray we are unsure if he was born like this or his eyes have been surgically removed.  He lives his life to the fullest despite this.  He is a very friendly boy who minds his own business.  He is incredibly smart and a quick learner and picks up his environment very quickly.  He loves going for walks and interacts very well with other dogs but can be quite humpy with them.  He is a lovely affectionate boy who has adapted remarkably well to life without his sight and approaches the world with confidence.  He may be able to live with another dog that would tolerate his humpy behaviour whilst his new adopter put training and management in place following a successful meet and greet.  Prospective owners should understand the commitment involved in caring for a completely blind dog, previous experience would be preferred.  In return you will gain a wonderful companion with a fantastic outlook on life.  He would benefit from a private secure garden where he can safely have some off lead time.

As Buddy came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on him so would prefer any children in the home to be 14+ years.

name: Rogue
description:
Meet the gorgeous Rogue!  She has settled into our care very well, with no issues during processing, feeding, cleaning or her first vet appointment for her vaccination.  Initially, she was a little nervous and understandably cautious in her new surroundings, but after a few days she began to build trust and her lovely personality started to shine through.  She is a sweet and affectionate girl who enjoys attention and treats, quickly learning where her favourite snacks are kept!  Once she feels comfortable, she will happily approach you for fuss and interaction.  We would recommend that her potential adopters have a few meet and greets before the adoption takes place to help her settle and build her confidence with them.  However, she is easily won over with patience, kindness and a few tasty treats.  She is sure to become a wonderful companion and much loved member of the family.  She would benefit from a private secure garden.  Experience with large breed dogs would be an advantage.

Rogue has socialised well with other dogs whilst in our care so we feel following a successful meet and greet could possibly live with another calm dog of similar size (must be neutered).

As Rogue came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on her and as she can be a little timid at first we would prefer any children in the home to be 14+ years.

name: Daisy
description:
Daisy was previously a racing greyhound who has been brought up in kennels. She is a smaller girl in comparison to a typical greyhound but still does have quite a high prey drive. She makes up for her size with her big personality, she is a very sweet and loving girl but can be a little cheeky at times. As like most greyhounds, Daisy enjoys a good walk and sniff in the woods, with the occasional time off lead in an enclosed area to burn off energy but will equally spend lots time relaxing on a comfy cosy bed. Daisy is after someone that is looking to give a dog lots of love and companionship, which she hasn’t previously had in her life. She in return will give you unconditional love.

Daisy can be a little nippy at times so we feel that it would be best for her to be rehomed with children who are 14 years or older – subject to a successful meet.

Daisy is currently paired and used to living with another greyhound in kennels. She has also interacted well with other dogs whilst on walks so we believe that she could be rehomed with another dog of a similar size and temperament – again subject to a successful meet.

Due to Daisy being an ex-racer it wouldn’t be suitable for him to be rehomed with cats or small furries.

name: Treats
description:
Treats is a retired racing greyhound who has spent all of his life in kennels and is now ready to find his forever home. Treats is an affectionate boy who is after someone who wants to give him the life that he deserves full of adventures and love. Treats is an energetic boy who loves going for his walks and having off lead time zooming around the enclosed compound area. Once he has burnt off his energy he is ready to snooze away the day on his comfy bed.

With his out going personality and loving heart, he would fit into many different households that can offer him kindness, exercise, and plenty of cosy spots to curl up in. In return, this gorgeous soul will bring warmth, devotion, and endless companionship.

We believe that Treats can be rehomed with children who are 12 years and over – subject to a successful meet.

Treats is currently paired and is used to living with other greyhounds in kennels, he has also walked and interacted well with other dogs during his time as the home so could possibly be rehomed with another dog of similar size and temperament – again subject to a successful meet.

Due to treats being an ex-racer it wouldn’t be suitable for him to be rehomed with cats or small furries.

name: Ranger
description:
This handsome boy is Ranger!  He is guaranteed to bring fun, energy and plenty of personality into his new home.  Affectionately known by the staff as their ‘Social butterfly’ he loves meeting new people and is always ready to make new friends wherever he goes.  He is a friendly, giddy and playful boy who approaches life with enthusiasm.  His excitement can sometimes get the better of him, and he can be quite boisterous, often jumping up to greet people.  Ongoing training and consistent boundaries will help him learn more appropriate ways to say hello.  Like many hounds, he is very vocal in his kennel and isn’t shy about letting everyone know he’s there! He thrives on interaction and enjoys being involved in the action, so will need a very active home.  Potential adopters should be aware that he may display typical hound traits like unreliable recall and great at escaping so he will need a private secure garden and careful management when outside of the house.  He is also very strong on the lead and will benefit from continued lead training and handlers who are confident managing a powerful dog.  This lad is full of energy and would suit an active home that can provide plenty of exercise, mental stimulation and opportunities for adventure.  He’ll make a fantastic companion for someone who enjoys an outdoor lifestyle and is prepared to continue his training.  In return, you’ll gain a fun-loving affectionate character with a huge zest for life and enough enthusiasm to keep a smile on your face every day.

Ranger has socialised well with other dogs whilst in our care although he can be very full on with them and extremely playful.  We feel he would be best rehomed as the only dog in the home where he can get 100% of the training he needs.

As Ranger came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on him so would prefer any children in the home to be 12+ years.

name: Csilla
description:
This gorgeous girl is Csilla.  Bursting with energy and enthusiasm she is looking for an active home that can keep up with her fun-loving personality.  One of her greatest passions is her toys!  She absolutely loves to play and will happily entertain herself – and you – for hours.  She knows exactly what toy she wants, will proudly bring it over, and drop it at your feet ready for another game.  If you’re looking for a dog who enjoys interactive play, she’s your perfect match.  She also enjoys long walks and exploring the world around her.  Due to her strength and excitement, she can be strong on the lead at times, so her new adopter should be prepared to continue with her lead training and provide her with plenty of physical and mental stimulation.

When it comes to other dogs, she prefers to take things at her own pace.  She can be nervous and avoidant around other dogs and values her personal space.  She is happy to take part in social walks with other dogs, provided they respect her boundaries and don’t pressure her to interact.  She would benefit from continued positive socialisation.  For this reason we feel she would be best rehomed as the only dog in the home with an experienced owner that will bear in mind she prefers her own space when out on walks.

This affectionate, playful girl is searching for a home that understands her needs, respects her boundaries, and can provide the active lifestyle she craves.  She will need a private secure garden.

As Csilla came into the home as a stray, we have no previous history on her so would prefer any children in the home to be 16+ years.

name: Hermione
description:
Hermione is a beautiful and sensitive girl that is looking for a very specific home.  She is an extremely timid and nervous girl who currently finds the world quite overwhelming and will often avoid people altogether.  While she is slowly beginning to build trust with familiar staff, she still has a long way to go and will need a patient, understanding adopter who is willing to let her progress at her own pace.  Hermione isn’t the kind of dog who will settle in a few weeks, she will need time, consistency and a calm environment to truly feel safe.  One thing that does bring her comfort is the presence of other dogs.  She is noticeably more confident around them, so a home with a calm, friendly resident dog (must be neutered) could really help her continue to grow in confidence.  Hermione is looking for a quiet, adult only home with someone experienced with supporting extremely nervous dogs.  She will need a secure environment, gentle encouragement and lots of kindness, but in the right home, she has the potential to blossom into a truly rewarding companion.  She will need a private secure garden.

The potential adopter will need to commit to multiple regular meet and greets for Hermione to get to know them before the adoption can take place.

Please only apply if you have the experience and commitment to work with Hermione.

name: Brandy
description:
This handsome lad is Brandy.  He is a lovely boy who is searching for an understanding home where he can continue to grow in confidence and enjoy his life to the fullest.  He can be slightly nervous when meeting new people, but once he gets to know you, he thoroughly enjoys human interaction and loves a fuss.  He forms good bonds with people and has plenty of affection to give.  In kennels, he can be very vocal and has shown some kennel guarding behaviours, but once he is out he is more settled.  He does have a tendency to jump up when excited and does forget how big he is, so he would benefit from continued training and consistent boundaries in his new home.  He is a bright dog who responds well to positive guidance.  Around other dogs, he prefers to have his own space and generally avoids close interactions.  However, he is happy to participate in social walks alongside other dogs and walks calmy in their company.  So we feel he would be best rehomed as the only dog in the home.  Due to his breed traits and prey drive he cannot live with cats of small furries.  This sweet-natured boy is looking for patient adopters who can provide ongoing training, understanding and plenty of love.  He will need a private secure garden.

As Brandy came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on him so would prefer any children in the home to be 14+ years.

name: Gandalf
description:
Meet Gandalf!  A true gentle giant with a big heart to match his size.  This affectionate boy loves human company and enjoys plenty of fuss and attention.  He thrives on interaction and will happily spend time playing with his toys. Caucasian Shepherds are highly intelligent and naturally independent thinkers, bred to work without direction, making them confident and strong-willed dogs that require experienced handling. He would thrive in a home where he has space and structure.  Gandalf will need continued training and socialisation. A breed experienced home is essential. Due to his size and strength he needs to be the only dog in the home.  A private secure garden is a must for this gorgeous boy.

As Gandalf came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on him so would prefer any children/visiting children in the home to be 16+ years.

Please only apply if you have previous experience with this breed and are fully committed to fulfilling all his needs and requirements.

name: Scooby
description:
Meet our handsome boy Scooby, who is slowly learning that the world isn’t such a scary place after all.  When he first arrived with us, he was extremely timid and unsure around people. Since, then he has made lovely progress in the care of our kennel staff.  He will now happily take treats from familiar people and is beginning to enjoy gentle strokes and attention once he feels comfortable.  One thing that brings out his personality is playtime – he absolutely loves his toys.  Because of his nervous nature, he will need patient adopters who are willing to give him the time he needs to build trust.  Multiple meet and greets will be required before any adoption can take place so he can get to know his new family at his own pace.  He gains a lot of confidence from other dogs, so we feel he would thrive in a home with a calm and friendly resident dog who can help show him the ropes and continue building his confidence. He will need an experienced owner that has previously had very timid dogs.  With time, understanding and patience, this sweet boy has so much potential to blossom into a loyal and loving companion.  Scooby will need a private secure garden.

As Scooby came into the home as a stray, we have no previous history on him and as he is extremely timid we would prefer any children/visiting children in the home to be 16+ years.

name: Ivan
description:
This handsome lad is Ivan!  He’s a friendly, affectionate boy with a big personality and an even bigger heart.  Full of energy and enthusiasm, he’s always ready for fun and loves nothing more than chasing after tennis balls.  He’s a very excitable and giddy dog who doesn’t always realise his own size, so he can jump up when greeting people or when he’s excited.  He is also quite vocal at times, especially when he wants your attention.   He knows his basic commands and is eager to interact with people, but he will need continued training and guidance, particularly when out walking as he is very strong on the lead.  Due to his size and strength, he would benefit from an experienced and active owner who can continue his training and provide him with plenty of exercise and mental stimulation.  Ivan would benefit from a private secure garden where he can safely have off lead time to play fetch with his tennis balls.

Ivan has interacted well with other dogs whilst in our care so we feel he potentially could be rehomed with another dog of similar size and energy levels following a successful meet and greet.

As Ivan came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on him so would prefer any children in the home to be 16+ years.

name: Buddy
description:
This loveable boy is Buddy! He is the perfect mix of playful energy and affectionate companionship. He’s a friendly, easy-going lad who absolutely adores people and loves being the centre of attention.  Full of character, he’s excitable, giddy and always ready to make you smile.  Whether it’s cuddles on the sofa or a game in the garden, he just wants to be involved in everything you do.  He is bursting with energy and would thrive in an active home where he can enjoy plenty of exercise, playtime and mental stimulation.  Fetch is one of his favourite games, and he will happily keep you entertained for hours chasing after a ball.  Buddy is incredibly affectionate and loves a fuss, he will make a loyal, loving companion.  One thing to note : He is quite the escape artist, so he will need a private secure garden and owners who are mindful of doors, gates and fencing.  When Buddy came into us he had an Entropian (an eye condition) which has now been repaired and he is healing nicely.

Buddy has interacted well with other dogs whilst in our care so we feel he could potentially live with another high energy dog following a successful meet and greet.

As Buddy came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on him so would prefer any children in the home to be 14+ years.

name: Buster
description:
At 12 years young, this handsome boy is proving that age is just a number! He is still full of life, loves getting out and about and really enjoys his walks.  He is always ready to explore and be part of the action.  Don’t let his age fool you, this boy can still be quite bouncy and excitable, especially when he’s happy or knows something fun is about to happen.  He has a playful spirit and plenty of personality, bringing lots of joy and energy wherever he goes.  He is looking for a home that will embrace both his golden years and his lively nature, somewhere he can enjoy regular walks, plenty of attention and a comfy spot on the sofa to relax after a busy day.  Buster is a lovely boy and has so much love left to give and would make a wonderful companion for someone willing to open their heart to an older dog. Buster can be quite strong on the lead so will need continued lead training.  Buster has now been in kennels for 94 days and is now desperately looking for his quiet retirement home.

Buster has socialised with other dogs whilst in our care but we feel he would be best rehomed as the only dog in the home where he can get 100% of the love and attention.

As Buster came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on him so would prefer any children in the home to be 14+ years.

name: Mercy
description:
Mercy came into our care as a stray, very shut down and scared. With time and patience staff managed to win her round and she slowly came out of her shell. Little by little we are seeing her lovely personality shine through. With those she trusts, Mercy has shown to be very affectionate and sweet lady.

She is still a little unsure, especially around men. However, this is something we are currently helping her with, and her new family must be willing to continue this.

With Mercy being nervous and unsure she is looking for a very patient and understanding adult only home, where she can develop and settle in at her own pace. Her new family must have the time to be able to come and meet her multiple times so she can get to know you before she goes home.

Mercy is currently also having socialisation training around dogs to build up her confidence. She is still unsure of other dogs so needs to be the only dog in the home.

name: Cali
description:
Cali was a very nervous girl when she first arrived at the home, she came in as a stray so we don’t know much about her history. It has taken a lot of time and patience for Cali to show us her excitable, bubbly personality. Due to this we are after an experienced home that has the time to get to know Cali here before she goes home. Once Cali gets to know you she loves a fuss and attention.  She loves going out for woodland walks and gets very giddy for her lead, she pulls a little on the lead, due to her size, so would benefit from some on going training. Cali enjoys an offlead run in the enclosed compound where she can chase after a tennis ball.

Cali will need to live in an Adult only home with an enclosed garden where she has space to roam about at her leisure.  She used to be a little nervous and unsure around certain dogs, we have been working with her on this and introducing her to dogs which is helping her social interactions. She is now a friendly girl with most of the dogs on site and can say a lovely face to face hello and even tries to play with others. She will need continued training to help with her socialisation. We still feel that Cali will need to be the only dog in the house.

If you think you can give this gorgeous girl her forever home please apply!

name: Rue
description:
Meet Rue!  Rue was a little timid when she first came into us but it didn’t take her long to come around and show us her crazy and fun loving side.  Rue absolutely loves playing with toys especially her plastic ring, she will chase around the compound with it all day if she could.  She is a real character and will come over for some fuss and then she’s off again with her ring. When she’s not zooming around the compound you will find her going on long walks exploring and having a sniff about.  She is strong on the lead so will need continued lead training.  We are looking for an active home for Rue that have previous experience with larger boisterous dogs.  Rue would benefit from a private secure garden so she can safely have off lead time with her toys. Continued training and socialisation is a must for this gorgeous girl.

Rue has socialised with other dogs whilst in our care and although is very playful with them she is very boisterous and has no boundaries so for this reason we think she would be best rehomed as the only dog in the home and the adopter to continue her socialisation around other dogs so she can learn how to behave respectfully around them.

As Rue came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on her so would prefer any children in the home to be 14+ years.

name: Tails
description:
This stunning girl is Tails.  We are looking for a committed and knowledgeable home for her where she can truly thrive.  As a typical Husky she is full of energy and very intelligent so will need an owner who understands the unique needs of this incredible breed. She is very high energy and an independent thinker with a typical Husky stubborn streak. She will need a home that has an active lifestyle where she can have plenty of exercise, enrichment and ongoing training.  Tails has so much potential and will make a loyal, fun and rewarding companion in the right home.  We are looking for someone patient, understanding and ready to invest time into building a strong bond with her.

Tails has socialised well with other dogs whilst in our care so can potentially be rehomed with another dog of a similar temperament – subject to a successful meet.

As Tails came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on her, so we think it would be best for her to go to an adult only home.

name: Pearl
description:
This talkative girl is Pearl.  She is a very affectionate, confident and friendly girl that is always up for lots of fuss and strokes.  She can be very vocal when she wants your attention so probably not suitable for an apartment/flat.  She enjoys her walks everyday having a sniff about and saying hello to everyone she meets.  She is very strong on the lead so will need an experienced owner that is used to powerful dogs. Pearl would benefit from a private secure garden.

Pearl has socialised with other dogs whilst in our care, she can be a little unsure at first but does come around after a bit, so we feel she may be best suited as the only dog in the home where she can take her time getting used to other dogs with lots of positive reinforcement from her new adopter.

As Pearl came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on her so would prefer any children in the home to be 12+ years.

name: Shadow
description:
Shadow is a very cheeky boy has been super friendly to our staff and volunteers since he came in to our care. With him being an ex racer, he does have a high prey drive so can try to chase squirrels whilst out for his walks! He was paired in his kennel prior to her going home and will walk well with other greyhounds. He does seem unsure around other breeds of dogs, especially smaller breeds and will be quite vocal towards them. The staff and volunteers have been doing socialisation training with him here and he is improving slowly, his new family would need to continue this to help with improve further.

Shadow will have spent most of his life in kennels with his racing background, so he may feel a little nervous going in to a home environment. We also do not know if he is house trained or toilet trained due to this. He loves to lie in his comfy beds after his walks – we sometimes catch him upside down showing his belly off!

We think Shadow would prefer to be the only dog in the home, with him being a little unsure around other dogs at moment. However, he could potentially live with another greyhound if a meet and greet with him went well and depending on the other dogs temperament. He could also potentially live with children 12 years and older – subject to a successful meet.

Shadow would love a home where he could relax and enjoy his retirement years and rest on a comfy bed (or couch) of his own!

name: Ginger
description:
When Ginger first came into our care she was very nervous, but with patience, kindness, and support from our staff, she has made lovely progress and is slowly learning that the world isn’t such a scary place after all. New people, places and noises can make her a little unsure so we feel she is in need of a quieter and less busy household with someone who is willing to take that extra time to get to know her and give her the time that she needs to feel safe. Ginger is especially nervous of men. Because of this, Ginger will need several meets with her potential adopters to help her feel comfortable and build a trusting bond.

Once she feels comfortable it’s like watching a completely different dog, her true personality really shines, she’s playful, super silly, full of energy and LOVES a kiss and a cuddle!Ginger is a real foodie and will do most things for a treat (especially a carrot!) she has also mastered the puppy eyes which she will use against you with every snack or meal you eat!She a really curious girl on her walks, she’s loves exploring what nature has to offer and stopping to sniff every new smell she can!

Ginger has interacted really well with other dogs whilst being with us and she loves to make new furry friends, however we do think because of her initial nervousness and her need for attention/reassurance she would benefit from being the only dog in a home so she can get all the love, cuddles and treats all to herself!!

Ginger has previously been in a foster home for 3 weeks, during this time we have found out that she is good with stairs, loves getting comfortable on the sofa and is pretty much toilet trained – with the odd couple of accidents. She likes to make her presence known and will bark when people knock on the door or she hears other dogs barking. She can be left alone for short periods of time without issues but does prefer people to be around. She flinches and gets scared of sudden movements or noises, we think this is due to her previous mistreatment. Gingers love language is definitely physical touch so she is after someone who loves cuddles! Ginger lived with a 14 year old during her foster stay and was really good with them. She is calm and gentle and loved the attention. Due to this we feel that Ginger would be able to be rehomed with children over 14 years old that are very dog savvy and have grown up around dogs.

With the right home, time, and understanding, Ginger will make a wonderfully loving and loyal companion. Could you be the one to give this special girl the patience and love she deserves?

name: Willow
description:
This stunning girl is Willow.  Willow was very nervous and unsure for the first few weeks she was with us.  She was very vocal when the staff went in her kennel and would hide in the back.  The staff have worked hard with her to gain her trust and she has come out of her shell now and is showing her playful friendly side.  We are looking for an experienced owner for Willow that have had nervous large breed dogs previously and will work with her on her confidence, doing this at Willow’s pace and not too rushed.  She is a very independent girl who will come to you when she wants some attention, her toys are her number 1 priority in life.  Willow is very food orientated and will sit nicely for her treats.  This said she is a little bit chunky at the moment so is on weight management food to help her loose a few pounds, and well done to Willow and the staff as she has managed to lose 3kg up to now!  She will need continued training and socialisation. Willow will benefit from a private secure garden where she can have off lead time to relax and have a sniff about.

Willow has socialised ok with other dogs whilst in our care but we feel she would be best rehomed as the only dog in the home as she does prefer her own space.

As Willow came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on her so would prefer an adult only household.

name: Bruce
description:
Bruce is a very active and playful boy who absolutely loves chasing around our compound with his toys.  He can be quite boisterous and bouncy when he is excited so would definitely benefit from continued training and socialisation. He can be quite a strong boy on his lead too.

He will need an active home where he can get all the exercise and mental stimulation he will need every day. Bruce would benefit from a private secure garden where he can have off lead time with his toys in between his walks.

Bruce has interacted well with most dogs whilst in our care but can be very dominant with them so we feel he would be best suited to be the only dog in the home.  The new adopter will need to continue his socialisation with other dogs to improve his interactions with them.

As Bruce can get nervous and overwhelmed meeting new people, especially men, we feel that it would be best for him to go to an adult only home. He will need time to get to know you before going home. His new family will need to be able to commit to multiple meets.

Bruce is a wonderful, vibrant boy with lots of love to give to his new family! He has spent a while in kennels now so would love to find his forever home and someone to be the person to throw his ball endlessly! Do you think this could be you?

name: Clover
description:
This gorgeous girl is Clover!  We are looking for an experienced owner for her.  Once Clover has bonded with you she shows off her loving side, it just takes some time for her to trust new people. Once she has got to know you she doesn’t leave your side.  Clover is a very boisterous and bouncy girl who does forget just how big she is when she is jumping up at you to say hello. She would definitely benefit from puppy training classes.  She can be very silly at times but in the nicest possible way and will definitely keep you smiling.  Her new adopter must give her all the support she will need whilst she gets used to new people and places and will have to commit to regular meet and greets before the adoption could take place.  Clover will need a private secure garden.

Please only apply for this gorgeous girl if you have the experience and can commit to the training and socialisation she will need.

Clover has interacted well with other dogs whilst in our care but we do feel she would be best rehomed as the only dog so she can get 100% of the training and socialisation she will need.

As Clover came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on her so would prefer any children/visiting children to be 14+ years.

name: Barney
description: Barney is a 9-Month-Old poodle X schnauzer who is full of life! Barney is looking for a family who are home most of the time as he really struggles being left alone. He is a playful boy who loves nothing more than playing with his toys! Barney can get slightly overwhelmed in certain situations so is looking for a family who have the time and patience to build up a bond with him and show him how the world works. Barney can live with other dogs however he is best suited to a dog who is no smaller than him, and who is confident and tolerant of boisterous young dogs. Barney walks good on lead and would benefit from a family who have had experience around worried dogs. AWE licence number 13090 by West Lothian Council.

name: Pepper
description:
Pepper is a 7 year old gorgeous Dalmatian who is a total sweetheart! Pepper is a friendly and inquisitive girl, but can be worried in new situations, so a patient and understanding family would be perfect for her. Pepper does prefer having her own space, so she'd like to be the only pet in the home at the moment, whilst she works on her social skills. Any children in the home should be around secondary school age.

Perfect girl Pepper loves exploring and going for adventures in the car. She's a total foodie who loves learning or figuring out puzzles to get some tasty treats. This can lead to her scavenging for things on her walks, but she wears a muzzle to prevent her from eating anything she shouldn't! Animal Welfare licence number is AWE02, as licenced by Glasgow City Council.

name: Olive
description:
Gorgeous Olive is a 3 year old German Shepherd who is dinky in size for a Shepherd, but her personality is larger than life! She is a sensitive soul, who can be reactive to other dogs, so would prefer a quieter environment, so she wouldn't see too many dogs on her daily walks. Olive arrived in our care after being found as a stray, with part of her left ear missing. Due to her unknown background, we can only speculate as to how this happened.

Olive is such a cuddly girl who loves to coorie in for more scratches! Olive is looking to have access into a secure and private garden, to have as a safe happy place to be off-lead, as she should be kept on lead and muzzle in public spaces. She is sweet, strong and smart and she would love to continue her training in her new home. Olive will require multiple meets at the centre prior to adoption. Animal Welfare licence number is AWE02, as licenced by Glasgow City Council.

name: Snoop
description:
Snoop would be best suited to a home with secondary school aged children and above who are confident around bigger dogs who can mouth although he would be looking for adults to walk him. His ideal home would have experience with German Shepherds as he is very typical of the breed and ideally would have minimal leaving hours as he struggles to be left. Snoop can be reactive around dogs and would be happiest with no dogs or cats in the home with any small furies kept separately. His ideal adopters are patient and consistent with his training and will give him time to settle into his new home at his own pace.

Meet Snoop our gorgeous 1 Year Old German Shepherd. Snoop really enjoys human company and loves spending time with people whether that’s playing, having a nice walk or a good fuss. Snoop travels really well in the car and is always ready to head out on adventures. He is really well behaved at the vets and groomers also. Snoop is really food motivated so is always keen for his training sessions. Snoop finds kennel life stressful and is looking forward to finding his forever home to relax his paws him.

name: Indiana Bones
description:
Indiana Bones is a gorgeous 1 year old Lurcher with a real zest for life. He could live with another dog, pending a successful meet at our centre, as well as secondary school aged children who are comfortable with larger dogs as he still has that bouncy puppy energy. He is looking for a rural, active home who are able to give him all the exercise he needs to stretch those long legs. Indiana may never have lived in a home before, so his new family will need to be able to build up his leaving hours very slowly at first.

Indiana Bones is such a sweet boy who loves his doggy friends and getting neck scratches from his favourite people. He is bound to make you smile with his silly personality and is an expert sofa cuddler. He is a massive foodie so would benefit from enrichment and is sure to love tricks and any training that will help guide him through his adolescence. This goofy boy has such potential and we can't wait for him to find his forever home.

name: Elsa
description:
Elsa is a lovely girl looking for a very special home where she can truly thrive. She forms strong bonds with the people she loves and is happiest when she has company, so she is searching for a home where she will have very minimal to no time left alone. A household with multiple people would be ideal, ensuring there's usually someone around to keep her company.

Elsa could live with another calm and well-mannered dog, subject to successful introductions at the centre. She would be best suited to a canine companion with a relaxed temperament who will respect her space.

She can happily live with children of primary school age and older who understand how to interact respectfully with dogs.

Elsa does have an ongoing medical condition which requires medication, so she is looking for adopters who are happy to continue her treatment and provide any ongoing veterinary care she may need. Our team will be on hand to discuss her medical history in full and answer any questions.

In return, Elsa is looking for a committed family who can offer her the time, companionship, and understanding she deserves.

Elsa is a fun, friendly little girl. You would never know she is 10 years old. Always wants to say hello to new people and loves a fuss and cuddle. Elsa mixes well with most dogs but can be worried by other worried dogs. Elsa loves to play ball when she’s allowed and is always eager to get out and explore new places with her favourite people.

name: Levi
description:
Levi is ready to find his forever home with a family who can provide him with the company that he adores and the activities that he loves. He’d love for his family to be passionate about dog training as he’s keen to learn and further his skills. Toy time and mind activity games will also be a good outlet for this clever, energetic lad, and a garden would be a great area for these. Walking routes without the hustle and bustle of lots of traffic where Levi can explore and seek out all the sniffs at ease will be ideal. Levi has been good travelling in the car too. Levi may live with another dog in the home or would love some canine pals to meet up with out on walks. He could potentially live with older teens. All members of the family will need to meet him a couple of times before starting the process of a home visit before adoption.

Levi gets sore ears from time to time and will require life-long management for this. We will discuss this further if your application is shortlisted. If this is a barrier to application, we would still like to hear from you.

Looking for an active, affectionate, adorable new best friend? Then meet Levi! The lovely Levi is a fabulous lad and is going to be an amazing, fun dog to welcome into the family! He’s a very clever pup and has bags of potential. He’s mastered a few basic cues already and happy to demonstrate, especially for a tasty reward. Levi is enthusiastic when it comes to mealtimes and doesn’t mind helping himself if there are freebies to be had. He can be a little bouncy on greetings as he’s so excited to say hello and soak up that extra attention. Think Levi could be your next best friend? Get in touch now!

name: Emily
description:
Emily is looking for a home where any children are of secondary ages and above as she is a lively girl. She would like to be the only dog in the home, as she is undersocialised around them so would benefit from a family willing to work on this with her. Emily would not be suited to live with cats, however could live with small furries if they are kept separate. She would love a private secure garden where she can explore freely and burn off some of her endless energy. Emily can struggle to settle in a home environment so would suit adopters that are dedicated to her ongoing training, providing a patient and stable environment for her. As she struggles to be left on her own, Emily is looking for a home with more than one person where someone is around all of the time initially, building up her time spent alone slowly as she settles in. She would also like a home where any car travel is not essential as she will need to continue her confidence training around this before heading on adventures. Emily will require home trials to assist in her settling into her home, therefore her new family will need to reside within one hour of the rehoming centre so we can facilitate this.

Meet Emily, our bouncy and a little bit bonkers 7 year old Labrador that is seeking a forever family that can match her enthusiasm for all things life! She loves nothing more than zooming around secure areas with her favourite toy in her mouth, proving just how clever she is by happily offering sits and downs in exchange for tasty rewards! Out on walks, she enjoys every scent the world has to offer. Though she can find it hard to fully relax indoors and prefers the comfort of staying close to her people, Emily’s food‑motivation, love of interaction, and eagerness to learn means she’ll thrive with patient adopters who can support her as she continues to grow in confidence and settle into home life.

name: Zebra
description:
Enthusiastic bundle of fun Zebra is a 3 year old Border Collie who likes to explore the world around her. She loves to play with her toys, she will happily entertain herself with them, but if you pick up a tennis ball be prepared to play fetch for quite a while, as this is her favourite game. In between playing with her ball Zebra will approach for a gentle fuss before heading off again, she likes a gentle bum/back scratch and will push into your hand for more if she fancies it. This sweet girl is ready to find her new home.

Zebra is looking for a home with loving and active adopters who can take her on sniffy adventures, who will take the time to help her settle in and provide her with fun and stimulating enrichment to keep her clever brain busy. She is quite an independent girl, who can be uncomfortable when meeting people for the first time, so could live with older secondary school children who approach at her pace, who will play with her nicely and will give her space to rest when needed. Zebra likes all of the attention to herself, so is looking to be the only pet in the home, she would benefit from having polite and calm walking buddies who will give her space and help her to build confidence around socialising with other dogs. Zebra is a clever girl, she is eager to engage in training in short, sweet sessions as long as a tasty treat or a toy is on offer. She would like a home with a private, secure garden for her to potter about in at her leisure. Zebra can settle in the home, so could be left for up to 4 hours, any leaving time would need to be built up slowly once she has had time to settle into her new home. Zebra will need to meet her adopters multiple times prior to going home so please bear all of this in mind when applying.

name: Pooky
description:
Pooky is a sweet 1 year old Frenchie X Dachshund who loves people and enjoys fuss, greeting you with her trademark bouncy wiggle. Pooky can find other dogs unsettling so would prefer to be the only pet in the home, but can live with older primary school children who are confident with her excitable play style. The Dachshund side of her means she can be vocal when she's left alone for too long, so a family who is able to be around a lot of the time would be ideal for her at first so she can build up those leaving hours slowly. Pooky needs her own private garden where she can chill and enjoy her alone time during the day before cuddling up on the sofa in the evening. A quiet or reasonably busy house would be ideal for her.

Pooky is sure to bring a joyful, fun-loving energy to your home. She isn't massively fussed on toys but loves rough housing and playing chase with her favourite people. She is a very clever girl who has several tricks in her wheelhouse that she loves to do with her humans for a tasty treat or two (ham is her favourite). Pooky walks well on lead and prefers a quiet, sniffy walk where she won't be bothered by any other dogs, as she finds them quite worrying. Because of this, she will need owners who are able to advocate for her and give her space from other dogs when out in public. After a long day of playing and sniffing, she loves nothing more than a fuss and a cuddle with her favourite people. This sweet girl is full of personality and we can't wait for her to find her forever home.

name: Eddie
description:
Eddie is looking for a home where any children are of upper secondary school ages. Eddie would be best suited to a home where he is the only dog, he is friendly towards them out and about but can be over enthusiastic and lacking in social manners. He would love a family that are keen to continue to work on his training around this. Eddie requires to be the only pet in the home as he has been known to have a chase drive towards smaller animals. He is looking for a family that don't mind keeping him on his lead when out and about, making use of secure fields for some off lead time. Eddie doesn't need his own garden but would not be suitable for a communal garden space. Eddie would like for his forever family to have the ability to build on his time left alone slowly as he settles in.

Introducing Eddie, he's our larger than life 3 Year old Mastiff crossbreed that is sure to bring all the fun to his forever home. This fella is full of character, being super friendly towards all the people he meets, bouncing around our compounds with his toys and getting stuck into new experiences with enthusiasm. Eddie can be a strong boy on the lead so would suit an adopter that is comfortable with this! He is keen to jump into the car for an adventure so wouldn't mind a family that enjoy being out and about exploring. Eddie is a clever boy too, being more than happy to show off his tricks with promise of a treat of course. We are sure that Eddie will make a wonderful addition to a home searching for a big, goofy dude to brighten each day.

name: Buzz
description:
Buzz can become mouthy when overexcited or overwhelmed so is looking for a home with just adults who are comfortable giving him the time to settle. We are looking for adopters that have the ability to separate him in the home due to this. He is happy to say hello and can make friends with dogs out and about but at times can get a little too over the top off so is best suited to be the only dog in the home. He cannot live with cats but could live with small pets if they are kept separately from him. Buzz is looking for a home who are happy to keep him on lead out and about.
