He can struggle to settle during car journeys so a home where it isn't essential for him to travel frequently is best. Buzz will require home trials to help him settle into his forever home, therefore we are looking for adopters who live within 30 minutes of the Manchester centre to facilitate this.

Buzz is our handsome 7-year-old Mastiff crossbreed looking for his forever home, he is enthusiastic for life and eager for his next adventure. Buzz hasn’t had the best of luck during his time with us but has a lot of love to give to his next home! This boy is the biggest lap dog you’ll ever meet; he is super food orientated too so this is great for his training, which he finds a lot of fun! In his previous home, Buzz could be left for up to 6 hours so could suit a working home. He enjoys spending his time here making new friends (people & dogs) and going for walking adventures with his pals.

name: Blaidd
description:
Blaidd is a handsome 1-year-8-month-old Siberian Husky with a fun-loving personality and an enthusiasm for life. He loves being out and about, exploring the world on his walks and meeting new people along the way. Blaidd is a very friendly boy who happily comes over for a fuss and can become quite excited when greeting people. He would thrive with an active family who can provide plenty of exercise, enrichment, and opportunities for adventure. Blaidd enjoys playing with a variety of toys and has bundles of energy to burn, making him an ideal companion for owners who enjoy an outdoor lifestyle. He could live with secondary school-aged children, and potentially younger children who are confident and comfortable around large, excitable dogs.

Blaidd is a social and playful dog who gets on very well with other dogs and would benefit from living with a similarly sized, playful canine companion or having regular walking buddies. He does find being left alone difficult, so he is looking for a home where someone is around for much of the day and where any time spent alone can be introduced gradually and positively. With patience, consistency, and continued training, Blaidd will gain confidence and learn that being left for short periods is nothing to worry about. Affectionate, energetic, and full of character, Blaidd is a wonderful young dog who is ready to become a much-loved member of an active and committed family.

name: Lexi (and Bella)
description:
Meet Lexi and Bella! This gorgeous duo are looking for their new home together.

This pair love to go for walks and enjoy spending the time having a sniff and an explore. They are looking for a multi-carer household to share all their love

They are not looking for much when meeting dogs out and about, and would much rather say 'hi' and 'bye', and continue their stroll in the sun.

Lexi and Bella travel beautifully, so would enjoy walks further afield.

Lexi and Bella would be happy to live with any age children who can give them the space when they need it and all the cuddles they could want as well!

They would be looking to be the only pets in the home so they can be the centre of all the attention.

This dynamic duo just pull at your heart strings with how loving and affectionate they are. These two are the perfect dogs for someone looking for companionship as they will give you just that.

name: Bella (and Lexi)
description:
Meet Lexi and Bella! This gorgeous duo are looking for their new home together.

This pair love to go for walks and enjoy spending the time having a sniff and an explore. They are looking for a multi-carer household to share all their love

They are not looking for much when meeting dogs out and about, and would much rather say 'hi' and 'bye', and continue their stroll in the sun.

Lexi and Bella travel beautifully, so would enjoy walks further afield.

Lexi and Bella would be happy to live with any age children who can give them the space when they need it and all the cuddles they could want as well!

They would be looking to be the only pets in the home so they can be the centre of all the attention.

This dynamic duo just pull at your heart strings with how loving and affectionate they are. These two are the perfect dogs for someone looking for companionship as they will give you just that.

name: Kenny (and Barnaby)
description:
Meet Barnaby and Kenny!

This adorable duo are looking for their new home together. Barnaby loves to play and enjoys a good game of tug whilst Kenny prefers human affection. Both dogs love a calm home environment and have a lot of love to offer.

Since being together from puppies, these boys have a strong bond and can often be found resting near each other. They would benefit from being the only dogs in the home but could have social doggy friends to spend time or go on walks with. They would like a home without cats due to their desires to chase.

They are looking for a quieter home with plenty of space for them to play, explore and rest. Barnaby can be sensitive to loud noises and unpredictable sounds. He will need a calm steady home environment. They would like a home with older teenage children or an adult only home for the time being.

Due to his size Barnaby will need to be walked by someone physically capable of managing him. Flat faced (brachycephalic) dogs suffer from additional health problems, which may impact their quality of life. We always recommend anyone thinking about adopting or buying a flat faced (brachycephalic) dog to thoroughly research the breed beforehand. Our expert team will be able to give you tips on exercise and weight management and advice on any support Barnaby may need.

name: Barnaby (& Kenny)
description:
Meet Barnaby and Kenny!

This adorable duo are looking for their new home together. Barnaby loves to play and enjoys a good game of tug whilst Kenny prefers human affection. Both dogs love a calm home environment and have a lot of love to offer.

Since being together from puppies, these boys have a strong bond and can often be found resting near each other. They would benefit from being the only dogs in the home but could have social doggy friends to spend time or go on walks with. They would like a home without cats due to their desires to chase.

They are looking for a quieter home with plenty of space for them to play, explore and rest. Barnaby can be sensitive to loud noises and unpredictable sounds. He will need a calm steady home environment. They would like a home with older teenage children or an adult only home for the time being.

Due to his size Barnaby will need to be walked by someone physically capable of managing him. Flat faced (brachycephalic) dogs suffer from additional health problems, which may impact their quality of life. We always recommend anyone thinking about adopting or buying a flat faced (brachycephalic) dog to thoroughly research the breed beforehand. Our expert team will be able to give you tips on exercise and weight management and advice on any support Barnaby may need.

name: Kiki (& Gypsy)
description:
Introducing our loveable girls, Kiki and Gypsy!

This adorable pair of sisters share the sweetest bond and are looking for a forever home where they can stay side by side. Kiki and Gypsy adore curling up together on the sofa, soaking up affection, and demanding belly rubs from their favourite humans!

They’re wonderfully loyal to their people and to each other, always checking in and staying close. They also love a bit of playtime, happily entertaining themselves with their favourite toys before settling back in for more cuddles.

Out on walks, Kiki turns into a little adventurer, eager to explore new paths and sniff out every exciting corner, while Gypsy loves the thrill of stretching her legs and having a joyful run.

They’re incredibly affectionate with their humans and with the people who come into their home, always greeting them with a wiggly bottom!

They can be left for short periods, and with steady, positive routines, this can be built up further over time. They will need two adults in the home and are unable to live with cats.

This lovely pair are ready to steal your hearts — could you offer them their forever home?

Flat faced (brachycephalic) dogs suffer from additional health problems, which may impact their quality of life. We always recommend anyone thinking about adopting or buying a flat faced (brachycephalic) dog to thoroughly research the breed beforehand. Our expert team will be able to give you tips on exercise and weight management and advice on any support Kiki and Gypsy may need.

name: Gypsy (& Kiki)
description:
Introducing our loveable girls, Kiki and Gypsy!

This adorable pair of sisters share the sweetest bond and are looking for a forever home where they can stay side by side. Kiki and Gypsy adore curling up together on the sofa, soaking up affection, and demanding belly rubs from their favourite humans!

They’re wonderfully loyal to their people and to each other, always checking in and staying close. They also love a bit of playtime, happily entertaining themselves with their favourite toys before settling back in for more cuddles.

Out on walks, Kiki turns into a little adventurer, eager to explore new paths and sniff out every exciting corner, while Gypsy loves the thrill of stretching her legs and having a joyful run.

They’re incredibly affectionate with their humans and with the people who come into their home, always greeting them with a wiggly bottom!

They can be left for short periods, and with steady, positive routines, this can be built up further over time. They will need two adults in the home and are unable to live with cats.

This lovely pair are ready to steal your hearts — could you offer them their forever home?

Flat faced (brachycephalic) dogs suffer from additional health problems, which may impact their quality of life. We always recommend anyone thinking about adopting or buying a flat faced (brachycephalic) dog to thoroughly research the breed beforehand. Our expert team will be able to give you tips on exercise and weight management and advice on any support Kiki and Gypsy may need.

name: Padme
description:
This bouncy, energetic girl is always ready for fun and never says no to a game with her toys. She loves nothing more than charging around the run, proudly parading her prized possessions, especially her beloved soft, fluffy toys. Her playful spirit and joyful energy make her an absolute delight to be around.

Padme is looking for an owner who can help her transition into home life with a little patience and reassurance. With the right support, she’s sure to blossom into a wonderful and devoted housemate.

Out on walks, Padme is a dream on the lead. She strolls beautifully by your side, often staying close as if to share every special moment with you. She’s also a social butterfly, always happy to greet new people, leaning in gently to soak up as much affection as she can.

And her coat - what a bonus! Unlike the typical greyhound coat, Padme’s is irresistibly soft, almost like velvet, making her even more special.

With some continued training, lots of love, and a caring home, Padme will make an incredible companion, one who will bring laughter, warmth, and a smile to your face every single day with her bright and bubbly personality.

name: Gizmo
description:
This sensitive chap is Gizmo.

We’re looking for understanding and supportive owners that ideally have experience with rescue dogs as Gizmo is going to need a little help to settle back into pet life.

The biggest thing that Gizmo struggles with is being left alone, so there will need to be multiple people in his new home to keep him company whilst he settles and help him to learn how to be home alone – or better yet never have to experience being on his own! He needs confidence building when out on walks, however inside, he is a bouncy, playful and cuddly boy who loves to be in your arms having fuss.

We’ve found that Gizmo loves nothing more than routine as this helps him feel more confident, se we take him for walks, feed him, play with him, and spend time with him at the same time each day. The team here at the centre can advise on the best ways to set Gizmo and his new owners up for success.

When out and about Gizmo prefers to avoid other dogs, however with time, patience and suitable calm dogs he can build up to making friends as he has done here at the centre. In his new home though, he will need to be the only pet as he will likely chase cats and isn’t suitable to live with other dogs as he will need to be the main focus for his owners. He would be best suited to live with adults and teenagers who can appreciate that underneath this worried exterior there is a super loving and cuddly dog just waiting to come out.

If Gizmo sounds like the dog you’ve been searching for, please get in touch today!

name: Elsa
description:
Elsa is a beautiful white German Shepherd with a big personality and lots of energy. She loves being active, playing with toys, and getting out and about, so she’d suit an active home with space to run, ideally with a secure area for off-lead time. After which she’s more than happy to relax with a chew and unwind.

She can be unsure in new situations, so she needs a calm, patient owner who can help build her confidence. Elsa isn’t instantly affectionate but forms strong, rewarding bonds once she gets to know you, and she may need a few visits before going home.

Elsa can live with older secondary school aged children who are confident around energetic  dogs. She isn't able to share her new home with other pets.

Elsa is searching for her Anna, a calm, understanding companion who knows that good things take time. As Olaf would say, some things are worth waiting for!

If you think you could be the perfect match for Elsa, please fill in an application form.

name: Tiggy Wiggy
description:
Tiggy Wiggy is a young, fun-loving girl with a zest for life and a heart full of affection. Bursting with energy and enthusiasm, she loves getting out and about for adventures before curling up for a well earned snooze!

Tiggy loves to have a paddle in the stream and have off lead time in a secure run. She enjoys playing with her toys and getting a tasty chewy to settle down with.

Tiggy could live with teenage children who have been used to living with bouncy dogs before. She could potentially live with another large dog in her new home, that has lived with dogs before.

Tiggy wouldn't be able to live with cats or small animals due to her desire to chase!

If you have room in your life for a girl who Is full of character, then Tiggy Wiggy could be the perfect addition to your home.

name: Kayde
description:
Meet Kayde, a large dog with an equally large zest for life!

She is a gentle giant who's full of fun and as she gets to know you will shower you with love.

Her favourite thing to do is walk in nature and is very responsive with training... especially when you provide food.

Kayde does like interacting with other dogs but continued work and socialisation would benefit her going forwards. Cats are just way too much fun and she is likely to chase them so she is looking for a home away from cats.

Her new family should be aware that Kayde can sometimes be enthusiastic in meeting people or dogs, and she can jump up. She just needs more training but is incredibly responsive and will get the hang of things in no time at all.

She hasn't lived with children before, but could live with children that are used to large and sometimes enthusiastic dogs. An ideal home would be one that understands her intentions when saying hello to people and dogs, and can respect her size and strength even when on lead.

Kayde enjoys travelling, so once she is all settled into her new home she would be open to car journeys somewhere nice.

If you have the space in your heart and home to take on a sweet girl like Kayde, please apply!

name: Chops
description:
Chops is a young bulldog cross with a big personality and an even bigger heart. This gorgeous boy is full of enthusiasm and loves being involved in whatever his people are doing.

He is a friendly chap who is eager to please, he is quick to learn and will need owners who are willing to continue his basic training. Chops is still growing and is already at 32kg, so his new owners will need to be comfortable handling a strong dog on lead.

Chops can live with older teenage children who have had experience of large breed dogs.

Chops would love to live with a doggy companion who has lived with dogs before - preferably one of similar size who he can play with! He wouldn't be able to share his home with cats or small animals.

If you think you could offer our Cheeky Chops a new home then we look forward to hearing from you.

name: Mojo
description:
Miss Mojo is a vibrant, joyful girl with an infectious zest for life that shines through in everything she does. She thrives in an active home where her energy and enthusiasm can truly blossom, so if you love walks, outdoor adventures, and fun games, she may be your perfect match. Mojo is a clever, eager learner who is always ready to try something new, especially when tasty treats are involved.

While in our care, she has enjoyed making dog friends and walking alongside them, and she could happily share her home with another dog who matches her playful temperament. She could also live with a family, either with or without older children, however she will not be able to live with cats. Mojo may need a little support when learning to be left alone in a new home, but with patience and guidance, she will flourish into a loyal and loving companion. A home with a secure garden and high fencing would support Mojo with her off lead time as she will need to be exercised in public on lead.

Full of personality, she brings laughter, affection, and a touch of cheeky charm wherever she goes. She will suit adopters who are ready to invest time in her continued training, for example her lead walking - she can be quite strong sometimes so this is something an adopter needs to consider. With the right home, Miss Mojo will be a devoted best friend and an exciting partner for all of life’s adventures.

name: Alba
description:
This lovely lady is looking for a very specific home, so please ensure you read her profile before submitting an application.

Although Alba is young, she can be underconfident and find new environments and novel things a little worrying.

She is looking for a quiet home that can give her consistency, and with new owners who are happy to continue her training around building confidence, providing access to quiet walking areas, where she can continue to learn about the world. Any children in the home would need to be 13+ and be happy to be involved in her training.

Alba is incredibly tactile and enjoys the company of her friends. Alba enjoys exploring the great outdoors so would make a great companion for those looking to keep their steps up.  She could live with a calm canine roll model who can help her learn the ropes.

Alba is learning about the joys of toy play , so this can be a great way to build a bond with her. She is looking for a secure garden to explore and play, as this is her favourite thing to do! Once she's tired himself out, he enjoys nothing more than a cuddle on your lap as she is a very tactile lady.

New owners will need to be around for most of the day to help her settle in and learn how to spend time alone at her own pace. Alba is unable to live with cats due to her desire to chase them.

She will need help with house training, and support exploring things within a home.

Flat faced (brachycephalic) dogs suffer from additional health problems, which may impact their quality of life. We always recommend anyone thinking about adopting or buying a flat faced (brachycephalic) dog to thoroughly research the breed beforehand. Our expert team will be able to give you tips on exercise and weight management and advice on any support Alba may need”.

If Alba sounds like the companion for you, please apply today.

name: Frank
description:
This lovely boy is a retired greyhound, a gentle soul who is looking forward to discovering all the joys life has to bring.

He can be a little unsure at first, so would suit a patient family with children who understand the importance of giving him time and space to settle at his own pace.

As he builds confidence, his true personality shines through, He’s an affectionate boy who adores fuss and will happily soak up all the love you can give.

He could potentially share his home with another calm dog of a similar size, but he isn’t able to live with cats or small pets.

A home with a secure garden would be perfect for him to enjoy the occasional run around, and he loves a spot of solo play, followed by a well-earned snooze in a shady spot.

This sweet boy has so much love to give and is ready to become a cherished companion in his forever home.

name: Smudge
description:
Smudge is a sensitive little boy who is looking for a calm and understanding home where he can truly come into his own. Having lived a very sheltered life, Smudge is still learning about the world around him and can find new experiences a little overwhelming. Because of this, he will need patient, supportive and dedicated owners who are committed to building his confidence slowly and at his pace.

Smudge may be shy to begin with, but beneath his cautious exterior is a charming and playful character just waiting to shine. He thrives on positive interactions and, being very food-motivated, responds brilliantly to reward-based encouragement. Enrichment activities and puzzle feeders are some of his favourite things and are a fantastic way to keep his mind engaged whilst helping him grow in confidence.

Once he feels comfortable and trusts you, Smudge shows his fun-loving side—he adores his toys and enjoys games like tug and chase, making him a rewarding companion as his personality blossoms.

Smudge is looking for a quiet, settled home environment where he can adjust gradually:

•	A low-visitor household to help him feel safe while he settles

•	A secure private garden for exercise, as he is not ready for walks during his initial settling period

•	A designated safe space indoors where he can retreat and decompress

•	Patient owners who can support his confidence-building journey

Smudge is currently learning house training and is making good progress, but he will likely need continued support and understanding in his new home.

Due to his sensitivity to sudden noises and unpredictable movement, Smudge is not suitable to live with children at this time. A calm adult-only home would suit him best, where routines are gentle and predictable.

Smudge will thrive with kind, committed adopters who are happy to take things slowly and celebrate the small wins along the way. In return, they’ll gain a loyal and affectionate companion who, once settled, will bring plenty of joy, playfulness, and love to their home.

If you’re looking for a special dog to nurture and help grow in confidence, Smudge could be the perfect addition to your life

name: Kaiya
description:
This beautiful lady is on the lookout for a new home. She is a cool girl with bags of personality and stamina!

Kaiya is an active girl, loves adventures, sniffing and playing with her favourite toys.

She will benefit from a home with owners who have some experience with large breed dogs or have researched and have time to spend with her. Kaiya will also benefit from mental stimulation such as snuffle mats to keep her occupied.

Kaiya could live with a dog in her new home, we would look for a confident dog used to living with dogs and one of similar nature to her. This will all be dependant on meets here at the centre first.

Sadly due a high prey drive she is unable to live with cats and small pets.

We feel Kaiya could live with children in her new home, children used to large dogs and who are happy to give her space when she is settled or resting.

Ideally she needs to have someone around with her whilst she is settling into her new home.

If you can offer this gentle lady a home, please ask about her today!

name: Timmy
description:
Timmy is a handsome, gentle giant weighing in at almost 35kg, but don't let his size fool you, he truly has a heart of gold.

This steady, easy going boy is a pleasure to walk and enjoys strolling nicely by your side on the lead. Timmy is friendly and welcoming when meeting new people, always happy to say hello. If you're lucky, you may even be treated to the classic Greyhound lean, a sure sign that he's taken a shine to you and is enjoying your company!

Timmy has also been spending time in the office with the team, where he has shown just how well-mannered he can be. He's learned to settle beautifully on his bed and is content to relax while the world goes on around him.

When it comes to his forever home, Timmy isn't asking for much. He's looking for a family who can offer companionship, gentle walks, plenty of affection, and, of course, a very large comfy bed to stretch out on after a busy day of being adorable.

If you're searching for a calm, affectionate companion who will happily be by your side and reward you with endless love, Timmy could be the perfect addition to your family.

name: Derek
description:
Derek is a lovable little chap with a big personality and an even bigger heart. He loves meeting and greeting everyone he comes across and is happiest when he's surrounded by his people. However, he can struggle with handling when this is not on his terms, and may growl if he is overwhelmed.

This handsome boy is a real foodie and will happily show off his tricks in exchange for a tasty treat! He's intelligent, eager to please, and always ready for a snack.

Derek absolutely loves playing fetch with his toys and would thrive with an active family who enjoy getting out and about on adventures. Whether it's exploring new places, going for walks, or enjoying playtime together, Derek will be your loyal companion every step of the way.

After a fun-filled day, Derek is more than happy to curl up beside you on the sofa for a well-earned snooze.

Derek could potentially live with a calm, confident dog following the correct introductions and a careful settling-in period.

He is looking for a loving new home where he can be part of the family and enjoy plenty of attention, affection and quality time together. Derek can struggle if he is overwhelmed, and needs a patient and calm approach and an awareness of his body language.

If you're looking for a fun-loving companion, Derek could be the perfect match for you.

name: Woody
description:
Woody is a gentle and loving companion who is ready to find his forever sofa.

Woody’s fosterers describe him as a wonderfully calm and affectionate boy whose absolute favourite thing in the world is people and company. Whether he is lying next to them on the sofa or relaxing on a cozy spot of his own, Woody is happiest when he is close to his human friends.  Food and walkies come close though!  He is house-trained, and sleeps soundly through the night in the lounge.

In his foster home, Woody enjoys spending time out in the garden playing with his toys. He has a keen eye for garden wildlife too!

Woody is a relaxed passenger in the car who travels calmly and quietly, making him the perfect partner for both daily routines and weekend adventures!

Woody could potentially share his home with another calm dog of a similar size, but he isn’t able to live with cats or small pets.

This sweet boy has so much love to give and is ready to become a cherished companion in his forever home.

name: Bailey
description:
Meet brilliant Bailey, a handsome older boy who is ready to find a calm and loving home to call his own.

Bailey is a friendly and affectionate boy who enjoys spending time with his favourite people. He has settled beautifully into his foster home, where he is the only dog, and is showing just how much he enjoys a quieter routine with space to relax. Once Bailey has made himself at home, he enjoys being close to his people, settling nearby and making the most of life’s simple pleasures.

Bailey may be older, but he still very much enjoys getting out and about. He loves his walks, exploring new places and especially enjoys swimming! He can be a clever, curious little chap and would enjoy owners who can offer him enrichment, gentle training and plenty of positive encouragement.

Bailey is generally neutral around dogs when out and about, but he has previously found living with other dogs difficult. For this reason, he is looking to be the only pet in his new home, where he can enjoy having his own space and one-to-one attention.

Bailey could live with secondary school aged children who understand that he may appreciate a quieter space when things get busy. He would prefer a home that is not too hectic, with owners who can offer him a predictable routine and help him settle at his own pace.

This lovely lad has so much to offer and is thriving now he has found a calmer environment. If Bailey sounds like the companion for you, please apply today.

name: Jet
description:
Jet may be an older Staffy girl, but she is definitely young at heart! Full of enthusiasm and personality, she still loves making the most of every day and is looking for a family who can match her zest for life.

One of Jet's favourite things is getting out and about on her walks, where she can enjoy plenty of sniffing, exploring, and taking in the world around her. She's an active girl who loves having adventures with her people.

Jet is also a big foodie and never says no to a tasty treat! In fact, she'll happily show off her tricks in exchange for a reward, making her a fun and engaging companion to spend time with.

When she's not out exploring, Jet loves playing with her toys and will often bring them over to encourage you to join in the fun. Whether it's a game of fetch or simply some interactive playtime, she enjoys spending quality time with her favourite humans.

Jet is looking for an experienced home with an active family who can provide her with the exercise, enrichment, and companionship she enjoys. While she likes to socialise with other dogs when out on her walks, she prefers having her own space at home and would therefore need to be the only dog in the household.

If you're looking for a loving, playful, and loyal companion with plenty of life left to live, Jet could be the perfect match for you.

name: Luna
description:
Luna has absolutely perfected the art of work-life balance. She enjoys a good walk, loves a game when the mood strikes, but firmly believes the best part of the day is stretching out on the sofa for a cuddle afterwards.

In fact, if you're wondering whether your bed is big enough for Luna... don't worry, she's already decided that you'll make room.

She's a wonderfully gentle, sweet-natured girl who wins people over without even trying. Calm, affectionate and easy-going, Luna is just as happy pottering around sniffing every blade of grass as she is leaning into fuss from her favourite people. She doesn't demand constant attention, but somehow always manages to appear exactly where the cuddles are happening.

When it comes to other dogs, Luna enjoys making friends, although she can be a little bouncy when she's excited to say hello. She's a big girl with an even bigger heart, so we're looking for confident owners who can help manage her enthusiasm while she remembers that not everyone appreciates being greeted with such enthusiasm.

Luna is looking for a home where someone will be around for most of the day to begin with, as she'll need a little help building her confidence with being left on her own. With patience, reassurance and plenty of positive experiences, we're confident she'll continue to flourish.

If your ideal weekend involves a nice walk, a good meal, followed by a lengthy afternoon snooze, Luna could be exactly the girl you've been looking for.

name: Bentley
description:
Bentley is a friendly, fun-loving boy who loves being part of family life. He enjoys spending time with his favourite people, whether that's heading out for a walk, playing tug with his toys or simply relaxing nearby after a busy day.

Full of character, Bentley is always keen to see what everyone is up to and likes being included in the action. He loves meeting new people, greeting them with plenty of enthusiasm, which is likely his way of seeking reassurance when he's feeling a little uncertain. Given calm introductions and a little time, he quickly settles and enjoys being close to his people.

Bentley enjoys his walks and loves getting out and about. He can be strong on lead when he's eager to get going, but he's equally happy spending time sniffing and exploring. He is looking for a family who can help him continue growing his confidence and social skills around other dogs. Bentley is very interested in making canine friends but can be a little enthusiastic and direct when saying hello. Bentley would benefit from building friendships with a few familiar dogs and gradually developing his confidence and social skills.

Bentley is looking for a calm but active home with secondary school aged children. He would prefer a home where he can be the only pet and have all the space he needs to settle in and thrive. A secure garden for playtime, training and relaxing in the sunshine would be a bonus.

Bentley is a loving, loyal companion who will bring plenty of fun, affection and personality to his future home.

name: Paddy
description:
Meet Paddy, a truly gorgeous greyhound with the gentlest of souls. This handsome lad has completely stolen our hearts at Thirsk, and it’s impossible not to fall in love with him once you meet him.

Sadly, black greyhounds can sometimes be overlooked, but Paddy is proof that some of the most wonderful dogs are hiding in plain sight. He is brilliant in every way and has so much love to give. One of his favourite things is leaning gently into his people for a fuss, soaking up all the affection he can get. He has a calm, soothing presence that makes him a joy to spend time with and the perfect companion for anyone looking for a loving best friend.

Paddy walks beautifully on his lead and enjoys getting out and about, taking the world in at his own relaxed pace. While in our care, he has also made some lovely doggy friends and could potentially live with another friendly, social dog.

Life in a home will be completely new to Paddy, so he is looking for patient adopters who are happy to support him as he learns all about home comforts and settles into family life. He could also live with children who understand he may need a little time and encouragement as he adjusts.

Paddy is a very special boy, and we know his perfect family is out there waiting. We simply adore him at Thirsk. With his kind heart, gentle nature and love of affection, Paddy deserves a family who will show him just how wonderful home life can be

name: Julia
description:
Ready to race her way straight into your heart, beautiful Julia is an ex-racer with the gentlest soul and the most calming presence. She's now looking for a loving family to call her own.

Julia takes great comfort from her people and looks to them for reassurance as she navigates the big wide world. Life in a home will be a whole new adventure for this sweet girl, so she's hoping to find a patient family who can help her discover all the joys of home life at her own pace. Having her favourite humans around for much of the day would be a huge help as she settles into this exciting new chapter.

One thing Julia already knows she loves? Walkies! She is an absolute dream on the lead and enjoys exploring the world by your side. She also gains lots of confidence from other dogs and could happily share her home with a friendly, social canine companion who can show her the ropes.

Julia could live with older children who understand that sometimes a princess needs a little time and space to adjust.

Sweet, graceful, and endlessly lovable, Julia has completely stolen the hearts of Team Thirsk. Now all she needs is a family of her own to love her just as much as we do.

name: Sophie
description:
She is a lovely girl who enjoys nothing more than spending time with her favourite people. She has a gentle nature and settles very well when indoors, happily relaxing alongside her human companions.

Sophie is looking for a special home where she won't need to encounter other dogs. She would thrive in a home with a secure garden where she can enjoy most of her exercise, alongside short, quiet potters around the block. Her ideal family will be happy to help her avoid busy dog-walking areas and continue supporting her confidence.

She can live with sensible teenage children who understand when she would like some space to relax. Sophie is looking to be the only pet in her new home, allowing her to enjoy all the attention for herself.

Sophie is currently working on building good connections with her handlers, and her new family should be committed to continuing this work using positive, reward-based training. With patience, understanding, and plenty of companionship, Sophie is sure to make a loyal and loving best friend.

Could you be the special person Sophie is waiting for? She would love a peaceful home where she can enjoy life's simple pleasures by your side.

name: Skye
description:
Skye is a beautiful Husky with a fun-loving personality and a real passion for making friends. She can be very enthusiastic when she first spots another dog, but this excitement quickly settles into lovely social skills. Once she reaches them, she's polite, friendly and often greets with a sniff or a gentle face lick before happily carrying on her way. She would love a home that will continue to give her positive opportunities to socialise with other dogs on walks.

Skye is incredibly people-savvy and does a great job of reading body language. She knows when someone wants to say hello and when they'd rather keep their distance, making her a lovely companion to have out and about.

As a typical clever Husky, Skye enjoys exploring and would love a secure garden where she can safely enjoy the outdoors. She will need secure 6ft fencing and owners who are mindful of anything near the fence that could help her investigate beyond the garden boundaries!

Skye will need to be the only pet in her new home, but she's more than happy to enjoy canine friendships out on her walks. Her adopters should simply be aware that she prefers her food and treats to herself and manage these situations thoughtfully around other dogs.

She can live with sensible secondary school-aged children and would enjoy an active family who can provide plenty of enrichment, adventures and companionship. Skye is also looking for owners who can gradually build up her time left alone, helping her settle confidently into the home.

Affectionate, intelligent and full of character, Skye is ready to find a family to share her next adventure with.

name: Whisky
description:
Whisky is a truly fabulous girl with so much love, fun and personality to offer the right family. Having lived a rather sheltered life, she is now looking for an understanding and patient home that will help her discover the world at her own pace while building her confidence.

One of Whisky’s favourite things is getting out and about on adventures. She absolutely loves using her nose while out on walks and would thrive with an active family who enjoy spending time outdoors exploring together. Whether it’s woodland trails, country walks or new places to investigate, Whisky is always keen to join in.

At home, Whisky is affectionate, playful and enjoys being close to her people. She would love a family where someone can be around to support her as she settles into home life and help her adjust to new experiences. While she may be a little reserved when meeting new people initially, it doesn’t take long for her warm and friendly nature to shine through, and soon she’ll be your best friend.

Whisky can enjoy the company of other dogs and may even be able to live with a dog-savvy cat. She would prefer a quieter home with minimal hustle and bustle, allowing her to feel safe and secure while she settles in.

Fun, loving and full of potential, Whisky is ready to find her perfect match.

name: Murphy
description:
This lovely, friendly boy is searching for a home with an experienced owner who understands the breed and can provide the guidance, exercise, and enrichment he needs to thrive.

He has a fun-loving personality and enjoys playing with his toys, with a game of tug being one of his favourite ways to spend time. He's always ready for a play session and loves interacting with his favourite people.

He can be quite strong on the lead, so he will need a confident owner who is able to manage his strength when out on walks. He would be best walked in quieter areas with fewer off-lead dogs around and, if possible, have access to a secure enclosed dog field for him to enjoy some off-lead exercise.

He has so much potential and will make a loyal, affectionate, and rewarding companion. If Murphy sounds like the right dog for you, apply online today.

name: Mars
description:
Mars is a wonderful greyhound who offers the perfect balance between playful energy and calm relaxation. He has a charming, quirky nature and an easy-going temperament that makes him a pleasure to be around.

Mars loves his toys and is always enthusiastic about mealtimes, often showing his excitement with tappy feet and chattering teeth. Out on walks, he is a delight to handle as he explores his surroundings through careful sniffing. He is also keen to greet other dogs and has been making excellent progress in his training to offer calm, polite hellos.

After his adventures, Mars is quick to settle down and enjoy a well-earned rest in his bed. With his affectionate personality, gentle manners, and endearing quirks, Mars is sure to make a wonderful companion in his forever home.

Mars could live with older children and another dog. He couldn’t live with small animals or cats, as they would be too fun to chase!

name: River
description:
Please meet River, she is looking for a very specific home. She will be a big project for any new owner.

Her new owners will need to be patient, supportive, and understanding as this little lady has been through a lot and has been in our care for over 200 days! She finds the home life and the world overwhelming at times, so whilst she settles in at home River will need to have a puppy pen to settle into which the team can talk more about.

She is making progress with housetraining, but it is likely that she’ll have an accident or two whilst she gets used to her new home. River could live with children aged 12 and over who are able to understand that she might take some time to make friends with them.

She needs to be the only pet in her new home as she is vocal and underconfident with dogs and doesn’t desire to have any interactions with them, and she may chase cats. So her new owners will need to continue to help her avoid dogs in the future and have low expectations of her meeting dogs.

River is looking for new owners who are around for the majority of the day to help her learn how to spend time on her own after she has settled in.

If you can commit to helping River learn how fun being a pet is, please get in touch today.

name: Finny
description:
Finny is a friendly little dog who enjoys the company of people and is always happy to meet someone new. He has a cheerful nature and likes being involved in whatever is going on around him.

He loves his walks and particularly enjoys stopping to investigate all the interesting scents he comes across. Exploring the world through his nose is one of Finny's favourite pastimes.

Finny is looking for a loving home where he can enjoy plenty of attention, companionship and opportunities to get out and about. He's a sweet boy with lots of love to give and is ready to find a family of his own.

As a flat-faced (brachycephalic) dog, Finny may be more prone to certain health issues that can affect his wellbeing. We encourage anyone considering offering him a home to take some time to research the needs of brachycephalic breeds. Our experienced team will be happy to provide guidance on exercise, weight management, and any additional care or support Finny may require.

If you think you have the perfect home for Finny, please complete an application for him today.

name: Yuki
description:
This handsome lad can't wait to meet his new family! Yuki is a calm, loyal and independent boy who spends a lot of his time chilling and observing the world. Yuki has a lot of love to give and greets his favourite people with a wagging tail. If he's extra excited to see you he will bring you one of his soft toys as well!

Yuki is looking for a new family that have some understanding of Akita traits or who are willing to learn and understand the subtleties in his body language. Yuki will flourish in the right environment and will make a loyal, engaging companion.

Yuki loves getting out on his walks and can enjoy seeing other dogs, but his breed typical body language can come across quite aloof. Therefore, he is looking for a family that can read dog behaviour to navigate these interactions calmly and confidently and are happy to keep up with his ongoing training out on his walks and in the home.

Yuki could live with older kids that are used to larger bouncy breeds, he would be best suited to being the only dog in the home. He couldn't live with cats but could live with smalls if kept separate.

If you feel Yuki would be a great addition to your family, please fill in an application.

name: Mabel
description:
Meet the lovely Mabel!

Mabel is a loving girl, once she feels comfortable and knows you, her affectionate and sweet nature shines through. Mabel is looking for a calm, quiet home where she can grow in confidence and become part of the family with lots of fuss on the sofa.

Mabel loves to get out on her quiet walks and would benefit from her new family helping her engage in her environment and enjoy all the sniffs.

Mabel could live older children who are comfortable at reading her body language and giving her space when she needs it. Mabel would benefit from being the only dog in the home to have all the fuss and attention. She wouldn’t be able to live with cats as they’d be too fun for her to chase.

Flat faced (brachycephalic) dogs suffer from additional health problems, which may impact their quality of life. We always recommend anyone thinking about adopting or buying a flat faced dog to thoroughly research the breed beforehand. Our expert team will be able to give you tips on exercise and weight management and advice on any support that may be needed.

If you feel like you could give the lovely Mabel a home, please fill in an application today!

name: Benji
description:
He is a sweet older chap who enjoys fuss on his own terms and is looking for a new home who can offer him a calm lifestyle. Think of him as a cat, he'll come to you if he wants fuss and his day is organised by him, you're just lucky to tag along!

Benji would be best suited to living with adults or teenagers who can understand when this lovely little chap will need time to do important dog business like lounging in the sun, pondering interesting topics, watching the world go by.

In his new home, whilst Benji doesn't want much from other dogs out and about on walks, he has lived with a dog before who shared his relaxed personality and was content to share space rather than be attached at the hip! This funny and loving sweetheart has enough personality to go around, and he loves to go for walks and is quite speedy when he gets going. He isn't able to live with cats.

Benji has shown himself to be housetrained here at the centre and loves to settle on his  dog bed.

If this lovely little Man is the canine companion you’ve been looking for, please get in touch today!

Flat faced (brachycephalic) dogs suffer from additional health problems, which may impact their quality of life. We always recommend anyone thinking about adopting or buying a flat faced (brachycephalic) dog to thoroughly research the breed beforehand. Our expert team will be able to give you tips on exercise and weight management and advice on any support Benji may need.

name: Lulu
description:
Lulu is a sweet and affectionate girl who is searching for a loving forever home. She has a gentle nature and enjoys being around people, often forming strong bonds with those she trusts.

Whether it’s heading out for a short stroll or curling up for some quiet time, Lulu is happiest when she’s sharing her day with her humans. She has a friendly personality with a lovely mix of playfulness and calmness. Lulu doesn’t need a large amount of exercise, making her well suited to a more relaxed lifestyle, but she still loves to have fun.

She really enjoys playing with her soft, squeaky toys and will happily engage in games with her people. Lulu also has her playful, silly moments where she gets the zoomies, racing around with excitement and showing off her joyful, cheeky side before settling down again.

Lulu could live with older school-aged children who can understand and respect her boundaries. She would prefer to be the only dog in the home so she can enjoy all the attention, though she may be able to live with a calm, dog-savvy cat following sensible introductions.

Lulu would thrive in a home where she can have a consistent routine and plenty of companionship. In the right home, she will blossom into a loyal and loving companion who will bring plenty of fun, affection, and those happy zoomie moments into everyday life.

name: Willow
description:
Meet wonderful Willow!

Willow is a fun-loving, young Bulldog Cross who is bursting with personality and energy! She’s a lively lass who enjoys playtime and exploring, and she’ll thrive in a home that can keep up with her active and enthusiastic nature.

She’s a sociable girl who could happily share her home with another dog, especially one that enjoys a good play and matches her confident, friendly style.

She takes comfort from companionship and would benefit from having company for much of the day, as she can find being left alone a little challenging. Because of this, she’s looking for committed adopters who are around for most of the day and can help her build confidence gradually.

She is able to live with children, but this will be dependent on successful meets at the centre to ensure everyone is a good match. Her playful energy means she’ll be best suited to a family who understand his needs and can provide guidance, routine, and plenty of positive outlets for his energy.

In return, Willow will  bring lots of fun, affection, and character into her new home.

If you’re looking for a loyal companion who will keep you smiling and love being part of the action, Willow could be the perfect fit!

name: Flash
description:
*Please read before applying for Flash.*

Flash is a very handsome boy, as you can see by his pictures. He is very worried and under-socialised so is on the lookout for a very specific home.

He is looking for new owners who have got time, patience, and dedication to spend with him helping him get used to the big wide world.

Ideally owners who are used to high energy breeds, and nervous dogs so that you will have an understanding of his needs. With this in mind, Flash is also a clever boy so would be eager to learn and with time he will flourish.

Flash is best as the only pet for now, potentially in the future he could live with a dog but for now all the attention needs to be on Flash. Continued socialisation with dogs will be key for this young pup.

Ideally he can live with children, children who won't pester him and will have an understanding of his needs. Allow him time to settle and readjust.

If you feel you could offer this sweet soul a home, then please do apply today. With time, love and understanding this boy will become your faithful friend.

name: Coco
description:
Have you ever been so excited to see your friend that you zoom around in circles? Coco has!

Have you ever been so happy to see a dog that you try to get them to chase you? Coco has!

Do you ever just wish someone would just throw a ball or something? Coco does!

Sweet and social with both people and dogs, Coco is ready to make some new friends! She can be a little shy initially, but it doesn’t take long for her to make you her bestie. She could live with children who were able to understand that she needs to take her time getting to know people.

Coco has a big brain so is eager to learn, but she's no fool, she's not doing it for free - make sure you have some treats ready for her. Her favourite treats are: all of them.

Coco has spent time in a home living with dogs before for short periods, so we think with the right match she would be happy to do so more permanently. She is keen to make dog friends out on walks but can come across over-eager so needs some help with boundaries. Coco does have a history of chasing cats so may struggle with one in her new home.

Finally, and possibly most importantly, Coco loves a caravan holiday! So if you are looking for a happy-go-lucky girl who you can treat to a staycation, then please apply for Coco!

name: Amethyst And Friends
description:
We will be expecting that potential adopters will be able to meet as soon as possible. We will arrange this with you if you are contacted. The puppies will be ready to go home on Thursday 23rd July and need to go within 2 days of this date.

These seven beautiful puppies came into our care at just two weeks old and have been lovingly reared by their mum and the team, giving them a wonderful start in life with lots of human interaction and care. Each puppy already shows their own little personality, but they all share a playful, affectionate, and curious nature.

Being Akita cross puppies, they are expected to grow into large (25kg+), strong, and intelligent dogs. Their future families should be prepared for a dog that will need both physical exercise and mental stimulation to thrive and would benefit from researching the breed.

We are looking for committed, loving homes that understand the needs of large-breed puppies. These pups will grow quickly and require consistent training, structure, and socialisation.

•	Families willing to continue training and socialisation

•	Homes with large dog experience (preferred, but not essential if willing to learn)

•	Owners prepared for long-term commitment

•	Puppies require constant supervision

•	Regular feeding schedules (3–4 meals daily initially)

•	Toilet training (including nighttime wake-ups)

•	Daily training and bonding time

Training and Socialisation

•	Positive reinforcement training is essential

•	Early socialisation with people, environments, and other dogs

•	Continued training into adolescence (large breeds can be strong and independent)

•	Basic obedience is a must (recall, lead walking, etc.)

•	As they grow, these puppies will need plenty of exercise

•	Mental enrichment (toys, puzzles, training) is equally important

•	Balance exercise appropriately while growing (protect joints)

•	Secure garden required for a large dog

•	Enough space for a growing dog

•	Safe, puppy-proofed environment

name: Lassie
description:
Lassie is a sweet, easy-going girl who would love a calm, relaxed home where she can enjoy life at her own pace. She walks beautifully on a loose lead and is happiest when she can potter around the garden, taking in all the interesting scents before settling down for a cosy nap.

Although affectionate, Lassie likes to take her time getting to know new people but with patience and kindness, her loving nature soon shines through. She would thrive with adopters who understand that she doesn't need constant activity or attention and are happy to let her settle into home life gently and gradually.

A predictable routine and a quieter household would suit Lassie best, particularly one without a steady stream of visitors. She would also love access to a secure garden where she can enjoy the outdoors and relax whenever she chooses.

As a typical sighthound, Lassie has a strong prey drive and will need a home without cats or small pets. She has limited experience living with other dogs and would be happiest as the only dog in the home, where she can enjoy all the attention to herself.

Lassie could live with secondary school-aged children who understand the importance of giving her space and interacting with her gently and respectfully.

In return, this lovely girl promises to be a loyal and devoted companion, perfect for someone looking to share life's quieter, simpler moments with a calm and affectionate friend.

name: Junie
description:
Junie is a gentle, sensitive girl who forms incredibly strong bonds with the people she knows and trusts. Once she's settled and feels safe, she'll absolutely adore you and happily become your little shadow, always wanting to be close by.

Because Junie is such a sensitive soul, she's looking for a calm, quiet, and predictable home where she can feel secure and supported. She isn't asking for much - just some steady companionship, a loving family, and a garden or peaceful walking route where she can enjoy gentle adventures at her own pace.

Having lived a rather sheltered life, Junie can find new experiences a little overwhelming. She'll thrive with a patient and understanding owner who is happy to let her build her confidence at her own pace. With kindness, reassurance, and plenty of love, Junie will truly blossom into the loyal and affectionate companion she's waiting to become.

If you're looking for a devoted best friend who will reward your patience with unwavering love and companionship, Junie could be the perfect match for you.

name: Moon
description:
Moon is a quiet, sensitive lurcher who has had a difficult time recently, and it shows. He’s feeling very sad and unsure of the world right now, but beneath that sadness is a soft, trusting soul who just needs the right people to help him feel safe again.

Moon is a gentle, thoughtful dog who watches the world carefully before deciding how he feels about it. He isn’t the type to rush into new situations — he needs time, kindness, and consistency. Once he begins to trust, you see the real Moon

He will need new owners who understand that trust isn’t instant. It’s earned slowly, with patience and soft voices. If you enjoy building relationships at a steady pace, Moon will reward you with loyalty and quiet affection

Moon does have some ongoing medical issues. These will be discussed fully with potential adopters so they can make an informed and confident decision. He is still very capable of enjoying life — he just needs owners who are willing to support him as he ages.

Moon isn’t a dog who will bounce into your arms on day one. He’s a dog who will choose you slowly, and that choice will mean everything. In the right home, with the right people, Moon has every chance of becoming a much happier boy — relaxed, secure, and finally able to enjoy his twilight years with dignity and love.

name: Dexter
description:
Dexter loves nothing more than spending time with people and heading out on daily adventures. Friendly, affectionate and full of character, he enjoys fuss, cuddles and accompanying his favourite humans wherever he can.

He is an energetic, enthusiastic companion who enjoys his walks, playtime with his toys and exploring new places. In foster, Dexter has settled in brilliantly, enjoying games of fetch and showing everyone just how much zest he has for life. He would benefit from a home that can offer a lots of human companionship alongside regular opportunities for exercise and fun.

Dexter has always been sociable with people and greets visitors warmly before settling down to relax nearby. He could share his home with teenagers and may be able to live with a calm, friendly dog of a similar size and temperament. Dexter is so friendly, he even loves going to the vets!

Dexter is looking for a home where he can enjoy regular walks and human companionship. He is not a fan of cats and has been known to bark at or chase them, so his new home will need to be cat-free.

His foster carers describe him as ‘a lovely, spritely companion with plenty of love and character who will love sharing adventures with his new family’.

name: Ted
description:
Ted is an affectionate, friendly dog who loves spending time with people. He enjoys a fuss, settles well in the home and will happily relax by your side after a gentle walk.

After spending much of the last five years without regular walks due to his previous owner's ill health, Ted is now enjoying exploring the world again. As many everyday experiences are still new to him, he's looking for a calm and patient family who can help him build his confidence at a pace that suits him.

Ted enjoys seeing other dogs and is keen to say hello, although he can become a little overexcited on lead. To help him settle into his new routine and focus on his training, he isn't suited to share his home with other pets.

Ted has previously lived with a child, but we think he'd be happiest in a quieter home where he can continue to grow in confidence with plenty of reassurance and consistency.

In return, Ted will reward his new family with endless affection, loyal companionship and the joy of watching him flourish.

name: Tony
description:
Tony is a fun-loving, energetic chap who's always ready for an adventure! Full of character and enthusiasm, he's sure to keep his new family smiling. Alongside his playful side, Tony also has a sensitive nature and will need patient, understanding owners who can help him settle into his new home and build his confidence.

To give Tony the very best start, he'd benefit from a few visits with his new family before heading home. Once he gets to know you, he forms incredibly strong bonds and becomes a loyal, affectionate companion.

Tony would be happiest exploring quieter walking routes without hustle and bustle where he can enjoy sniffing to his heart's content without the worry of unfamiliar off-lead dogs rushing up to him. A calm environment will help him feel relaxed and allow him to focus on having fun with his favourite people.

This clever boy is eager to learn and loves training sessions - especially when tasty hot dog treats are involved! With some reassurance and encouragement, Tony is sure to thrive in his new home.

Above all, Tony adores his people. He's an affectionate, devoted dog who is looking for a family willing to invest a little time and patience in return for a lifetime of love, loyalty, and companionship.

Could Tony be the perfect addition to your family?

name: Marley (& Belle)
description:
Meet Marley and Belle,

Marley and Belle are a social, bonded brother-and-sister pair seeking a quiet home where they can bring their perfect blend of outdoor energy and indoor calmness. Used to a quiet home, they are ideally suited for a gentle household without cats or small animals, though they could happily live with older, calm children.

Marley is a confident, natural greeter who warmly welcomes people and spends a lot of his indoor time contentedly lounging on his bed. Belle is generally a little more cautious, looking to her brother for reassurance, but she is a true cuddler at heart who nudges for fuss and who would happily be a lap dog!

Both dogs possess beautiful coats that require regular brushing – luckily they absolutely love to be groomed!

On adventures, both dogs travel wonderfully in cars, love to run, and share a massive passion for chasing tennis balls. They are used to long walks of over an hour and would need lots of opportunities for exercise in their new home.  Because they are very used to running off-lead, their new family will benefit from doing structured training on a lead to build up their walking manners.   Belle also has a keen interest in agility, which is perfect for keeping her active and mentally stimulated.

This lovely pair are ready to steal your hearts — could you offer them their forever home?

name: Belle (& Marley)
description:
Meet Marley and Belle,

Marley and Belle are a social, bonded brother-and-sister pair seeking a quiet home where they can bring their perfect blend of outdoor energy and indoor calmness. Used to a quiet home, they are ideally suited for a gentle household without cats or small animals, though they could happily live with older, calm children.

Marley is a confident, natural greeter who warmly welcomes people and spends a lot of his indoor time contentedly lounging on his bed. Belle is generally a little more cautious, looking to her brother for reassurance, but she is a true cuddler at heart who nudges for fuss and who would happily be a lap dog!

Both dogs possess beautiful coats that require regular brushing – luckily they absolutely love to be groomed!

On adventures, both dogs travel wonderfully in cars, love to run, and share a massive passion for chasing tennis balls. They are used to long walks of over an hour and would need lots of opportunities for exercise in their new home.  Because they are very used to running off-lead, their new family will benefit from doing structured training on a lead to build up their walking manners.   Belle also has a keen interest in agility, which is perfect for keeping her active and mentally stimulated.

This lovely pair are ready to steal your hearts — could you offer them their forever home?

name: Percy
description:
Percy is a bright and busy boy who loves nothing more than getting his nose to the ground and exploring the world around him. Having arrived in our care as a stray, he has embraced new experiences and has shown himself to be a clever, fun loving companion who enjoys playtime and investigating the world around him.

Percy is looking for a family that can continue helping him build his confidence. He would be looking for a home with patient owners who could build up any leaving time slowly and where he can be gradually introduced to car journeys.

This energetic boy would love owners who enjoy getting outdoors and can provide plenty of opportunities for sniffing, enrichment and exploration. Mental stimulation is just as important to Percy as physical exercise and he is always keen to learn when treats are involved.

Percy could live with children who are respectful of his space and understand when he would like some quiet time. He may also be able to share his home with a dog or cat that has lived with dogs before, provided introductions are carried out at a pace that helps everyone feel comfortable.

With patience, understanding and lots of opportunities to use his brilliant nose, Percy is sure to make a delightful companion for the right home.

name: George
description:
George is a handsome, lovable chap who's searching for a patient and understanding home where he can truly thrive. With the right support, reassurance, and guidance, George has all the potential to blossom into the most loyal companion.

Once George gets to know you, you'll have a friend for life. He absolutely adores his people and loves nothing more than staying close by their side. As with many sensitive dogs, he can be a little wary when meeting new people, so he'll appreciate owners who can gently support and guide him through new situations, helping him build his confidence at his own pace.

George is a big fan of his daily walks, especially when they're nice and relaxed. Rather than rushing from A to B, he enjoys taking his time to sniff and explore the world around him - after all, every walk is an adventure when there are so many interesting scents to discover! He's happy to greet other dogs while out and about, but he'd be happiest as the only dog in his new home, where he can enjoy all the love and attention for himself.

If you're looking for a devoted best friend and have the patience, understanding, and enthusiasm to continue George's training, you'll be rewarded with a wonderful companion. By providing him with the reassurance, consistency, and stability he needs, you'll help him grow in confidence and become the happy, secure dog he's meant to be.

Could you be the special person George has been waiting for?

name: Dobby
description:
Dobby is a bright, affectionate dog who loves being with his people. He's a quick learner who enjoys training, food enrichment and using his nose to explore the world around him. Once he gets to know you, Dobby forms close bonds and has a playful, loving nature that makes him a rewarding companion.

Dobby can be a little unsure in new situations, so he's looking for patient adopters who will help him continue building his confidence. Calm introductions, positive reinforcement and gradually introducing him to new places will help him settle and thrive. He's already made great progress in foster care and we're confident he'll continue to flourish in the right home.

One of Dobby's favourite activities is a quiet sniffy walk, where he can explore at his own pace. He walks nicely on lead and enjoys learning about the world through his nose. He'll also enjoy enrichment activities and short training sessions that keep his clever mind busy.

Dobby wouldn't be suited to share his home with children, as he'll benefit from a calm environment while his confidence continues to grow. He may be able to live with a calm, confident dog who can be a positive role model.

If you're looking for a fun, affectionate companion and would enjoy helping a dog grow in confidence, Dobby has so much to offer. We think he's going to make a fantastic addition to the right home and can't wait to see him enjoying life with his new family.

name: Nyla
description:
Meet Nyla, our sweet little lady with the most beautiful nature. While she came into our care as a stray and we sadly don’t know what her life looked like before she arrived, it didn’t take long for her lovely personality to shine through and capture our hearts.

Nyla is looking for a calm and understanding home where she can settle into her new life at her own pace. A quieter environment, without too much coming and going, would suit her best and help her build her confidence as she learns that the world can be a safe and happy place.

She enjoys her little walks and loves taking in her surroundings, making every outing a gentle adventure. Nyla would thrive with adopters who are patient and willing to let her find her feet in her own time, celebrating all the small milestones along the way.

Although she has the potential to have doggy friends out and about, Nyla would prefer to be the only dog in her home so she can enjoy all the love and attention for herself. She is also looking for a cat-free home. Older children who understand the importance of giving her space and time to settle could live with her.

Once Nyla knows and trusts you, she transforms into the ultimate lap dog. She adores being close to her people and will happily snuggle up for cuddles, proving just how much love this special girl has to give. We can't wait to see Nyla find the loving home she so deserves.

Flat faced (brachycephalic) dogs suffer from additional health problems, which may impact their quality of life. We always recommend anyone thinking about adopting or buying a flat faced (brachycephalic) dog to thoroughly research the breed beforehand. Our expert team will be able to give you tips on exercise and weight management and advice on any support Nyla may need.

name: Lola
description:
Please give a warm welcome to gorgeous Lola!

This lovely lady is looking for a quiet new home that is able to provide structure and consistency while she finds her feet back into home life.

Lola would be best suited to an adult only home or with older teenagers who are confident in understanding Lola's body language but also happy to enjoy playtime and get involved with the fun that comes with sharing your home with Lola.

Ideally Lola's new home will have people around for a large portion of the day to help her adjust from kennels to home life.

She has made some lovely canine companions here at the centre, we feel she could share her home with a well matched canine friend pending meets here at the centre.

Dachshunds can suffer from breed related health problems. Dogs with long backs and short legs may also be pre-disposed to certain skeletal conditions. We always recommend anyone thinking about adopting or buying a dachshund to thoroughly research the breed beforehand. Our expert team will be able to give you advice on any support Lola's needs.

If you think you can give Lola the home she is looking for then please fill out her application today....

name: Sally
description:
I need a calm and quiet home where I can settle in at my own pace.

I need a larger, resident male dog to help me settle in and I have met cats fine.

I would like a secure garden to spend time in.

I am learning to walk on a lead and harness, but it's still very new for me.

I have never lived in a home so I will need help to learn house training.

Sweet little Sally has come to us from a breeder to begin her search for a loving forever home. When she first arrived, she was understandably feeling a little worried to find herself here, but she has been trying so hard to be brave and has really started to come out of her shell.

Sally is growing into the funniest and cutest little dog, and for such a small girl she certainly has a big personality that is beginning to shine through. Her confidence continues to grow, and she now happily enjoys receiving fuss and attention from people she trusts. Sally has even started her harness training and is although it's still very new to her, she is enjoying going on walks and exploring.

Sally is a super little dog who is just waiting for someone to give her the time, patience, and love she needs to continue building her confidence. With the right home and lots of kindness, we are sure she will blossom into a wonderful companion.

She will be looking for a calm, kind and understanding home with adopters who are around most of the time and who will allow her to settle in at her own pace. She has never lived in a home before, so everything in a home environment will be new to Sally, so she will need gentle guidance as she continues learning about everyday life and learns new skills such as house training.

Sally absolutely loves other dogs and gains a lot of comfort from having canine friends around her. She will need another dog in her new home to help her feel confident, but she would prefer the company of a smaller male dog. Sally can be a little bossy with female dogs, so she is best suited to living with a male companion. She has met a cat at the rescue fine (please see the video in her gallery!) and so maybe possibly be able to be homed with a dog savvy cat.

This sweet girl just needs someone to give her the chance she deserves, and we are sure she will repay your love tenfold.

Sally has a lick granuloma on her left front leg, which is healing, and an old scar on her left hip. She has had an umbilical hernia repair at the time of her spay and is being treated for bilateral conjunctivitis.

Sally has been given the Gift of Life by Carolina Olsen

Super little Sally is doing so well. I absolutely adore this lovely lady. She grows in confidence every day, and it really hasn’t taken her all that long to come out of her shell.

Sally still needs a little time with new people to feel safe, but she tries so hard, and once she feels comfortable with you, her funny personality shines through, from showing you her full-body rolls, to running over for gentle fusses, or scrambling all over you for foodie treats. I’m very proud of how hard she tries each day to be brave.

Today, Sally enjoyed another little walk around the rescue with her new kennel companion. Sally is by no means confident on the lead yet and will need gentle encouragement and patience to continue her learning journey and start to relax on her walks.

Sally looks to her kennel buddies for support, and her perfect home will already have a friendly (male) resident dog to help show her the way. Sally is best suited to a quiet, adult-only home, ideally with knowledge and/or experience of ex-breeding dogs.

Sally is just the growing into the funniest, cutest of little dogs. For a small being she has a big personality and it’s really starting to shine through. She grows ever confident, and happily takes fusses from me and today successfully begun harness training.

Sally is a super little dog just waiting for a loving home to help continue bringing her out of her shell. Sally will need a friend in her new home but prefers the company of smaller male dogs, she can be a bit bossy with female dogs and is best suited to an adult only home.

Sweet little Sally has come to us from a breeder to begin her search for a loving forever home. She is a very gentle girl who is understandably feeling a little worried to find herself here, but she is trying so hard to be brave.

Sally will gently approach you for a little sniff and is already showing what a sweet nature she has, she just needs time, patience and reassurance to help her confidence grow. With the right home and lots of kindness, we are sure she will blossom into a lovely companion.

She will be looking for a calm, kind and understanding home with adopters who are around most of the time and who will allow her to settle in at her own pace. Everything in a home environment will be completely new to Sally, so she will need gentle guidance as she learns about everyday life and new skills such as house training and how to walk on a lead.

Sally absolutely loves other dogs and gains a lot of comfort from them, so she will need at least one other kind and gentle resident dog in her new home to be her friend and show her the way. Sally will need a larger, male dog in her home and cannot live with females or smaller dogs.

This sweet girl just needs someone to give her the chance she deserves and she will repay your love tenfold.

Sally has a lick granuloma on her left front leg, which is healing and an old scar on her left hip. She has had an umbilical hernia repair at the time of her spay and is being treated for bilateral conjunctivitis.

Through the generosity of a Gift of Life sponsor, this dog was able to leave behind an uncertain future in Ireland and find safety and care at Many Tears. Every sponsorship helps us rescue and save more dogs in desperate need. If you would like to become part of a rescue story, please consider donating to our Gift of Life fund.

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Kelly
description:
I would like a calm and quiet home where I can settle in.

I need a resident dog to help me settle in and I have never met cats.

I will need a secure garden while I get used to my new home.

I can walk on a lead and harness and love to explore quiet areas.

I am in a foster home now so am learning all about home life and house training.

I have a very overshot jaw, and I have been spayed.

Kelly is a lovely girl who has come to us from a breeder to find her forever home. When she first arrived, she was quite worried but she soon started to settle in and come out of her shell. She has since moved to a wonderful foster home where she is settling in well and experiencing home life for the first time.
