Swanny has cataracts in both eyes, which do not appear to bother her at all. She has had an umbilical hernia repaired at the time of her spay, along with two teeth extractions. She also has one fractured pre-molar, which our vets have advised leaving for now, though it may need monitoring in the future. Swanny also has a travel document in place.

Swanny is an absolute delight to care for here at the rescue, and we truly believe that whoever opens their heart and home to her will be gaining a very special little dog who has so much love to give.

Over the last couple of months, we’ve introduced Swanny to many larger male dogs at the rescue in attempt to find her an ideal playmate. Swanny is an absolutely comical girl, and no matter the size of the male dog, she will not hold back!!

Swanny’s main buddy is our Standard Poodle Flash, and every single day they have play dates. She is incredibly tolerant with his exuberant and playful nature, but also gives it as good as she gets. They have such a good balance between them, and once Swanny has had enough she will just take herself away from playing and have a good sniff around our big play yard, or steal some fusses off the first human in sight!

She has also had a play date with Charlie (a Great Dane X Mastiff) and his size did not phase her one bit, he was probably 3-4x bigger than her, but Swanny’s playfulness definitely trumped his size!!!

Swanny is not only an absolute delight to look after, but just being in her presence would make your entire day. She is as sweet as anything, she is kind, gentle, and loving. She is not built for kennels, she is build for sofa snuggles, big adventures, and endless love.

How Swanny has not been adopted yet is a mystery to me .

Perhaps it's her demeanor when meeting new people? Swanny can be a little shy at first but soon makes a connection and there's nothing wrong with being cautious.

Swanny walks beautifully on a lead and loves to sniff and smell the grass.

As Swanny does have enough confidence, she can live as the only dog in the house or equally she would be happy living with a larger male dog.

I would say, to see the true Swanny, you need to meet her, walk her and give her a little time to show affection. It really is rewarding when it happens.

Beagles are really pretty dogs and Swanny is no exception, so if you would like a beautiful beagle in your life, Swanny is ready to be the one.

Swanny is the sweetest angel, and it truly is a privilege to care for her five days a week. She has the most gentle and loving nature.

Swanny can be a little nervous when meeting new people, which is completely understandable, but with a bit of patience and kindness, she soon begins to show her true self.

Once you’ve built that special bond with Swanny, she becomes the most loyal companion you could ever ask for. She loves to  be close to you and will happily follow you around like you’re shadow!

Like a true beagle, Swanny absolutely loves her walks. The moment she’s outside, her nose is straight to the ground, exploring every scent along her way. Watching her take in the world through her nose is such a lovely reminder of how much joy the simple things can bring.

Swanny really is the most perfect dog in her own unique way. With the right family who can give her patience, reassurance, and lots of love, she will truly flourish.

She has so much to offer and would make the most wonderful addition to a caring and understanding home.

Swanny is such a sweet girl who is blossoming in confidence every single day!

Swanny has made so much progress over the last few weeks, I now believe she can be an only dog in the right circumstances.

Swanny does get on with larger male dogs absolutely fine, but prefers dogs who aren’t in the her face and give her the space she needs.

During her time here so far swanny has had a few disagreements with the male dogs she has shared kennels with when they get to much in her space.

Swanny is now walking beautifully on lead, and enjoys spending time going out for walks, or running freely down our big yard!

Swanny also tries to keep her kennel clean during the days, so house training should be a breeze!

Swanny is honestly a delight to look after here at the rescue, whoever welcomes her into their family are going to be very lucky people!

Swanny is a fabulous little lady who has come to us from a breeder. She’s an inquisitive girl with the unmistakable nose of a Beagle, always eager to explore and see what’s going on around her. Swanny can be confident when she chooses, but she still has a shy side, and with the gentle encouragement of her kennel friends, she’s starting to show her sweet, sociable nature.

She will need a confident, larger male resident dog in her new home to help her settle, guide her, and teach her about life in a home. Once she’s comfortable, Swanny would absolutely love an active family who can take her on plenty of adventures and keep her mind and body engaged.

Swanny is an absolute gem and, as she has never lived in a home before, will need patient adopters to help her learn house training, walking on a lead, and all the routines of home life. With kindness, consistency, and love, we believe she will settle quickly and become a devoted, loving companion.

Swanny has come to us with cataracts in both eyes, that does not seem to bother her. She has had an umbilical hernia repaired at the time of her spay as well as 2 teeth extracted. She also has one fractured pre-molar which our vets have advised to leave, but this may need to be looked at in the future.

These wonderful souls may be older, have medical needs, or come with a few unique quirks—but they have endless love to give. Full of personality and deserving of a second chance, they come with a reduced adoption fee to help them find the perfect home. Could you be the one to give them the love they’ve been waiting for?

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: River
description:
I need an experienced, adult only home with no visiting children.

I would prefer to be an only dog at the moment. I can't live with cats.

I would like a secure garden in a quiet area.

I can walk well on a lead and would like an active home.

I need experienced adopters to work on my reactivity with me.

I am spayed and ready to find my forever home.

River originally came into our care in 2020 from a breeder and was adopted into a loving home. Sadly, her family later found they were unable to continue meeting her needs, and River has now returned to us.

River is a highly intelligent, affectionate girl who forms very strong bonds with her people. According to her previous home, once she feels safe she is extremely loving, enjoys close contact and seeks reassurance from those she trusts. She loves cuddles, gentle scratches, and being near her humans, and is very in tune with their emotions, often offering comfort when someone is feeling down.

River is a clever and enthusiastic learner who already knows a wide range of cues and enjoys training, enrichment and mental stimulation. She loves her toys, especially balls and tug toys, enjoys scent games such as “find it”, and is very food-motivated, peanut butter is her absolute favourite! She settles well at home, enjoys routine and structure, and is clean and non-destructive.

River enjoys her walks and would suit an active home with access to quieter walking routes. She walks nicely on a halti and enjoys being out and about, but can find busy environments overwhelming and may be reactive to dogs, vans or motorbikes when anxious. She will need confident handling and continued training, and should remain on lead unless in a secure area.

Although affectionate and loving, River is a sensitive dog and can struggle with unfamiliar people, visitors and loud or sudden noises such as fireworks. When frightened she may bark or retreat. For this reason, River is looking for acalm, quiet, adult-only homewithvery few visitors, ideally in arural or semi-rural setting. Her adopters must beexperienced with reactive dogs, patient, and willing to work at her pace while respecting her boundaries.

We are advised that River can occasionally display guarding behaviours towards her trusted humans, such as growling if another person enters the room while she is settled. This will need calm, confident and experienced management.

River is currently looking for a home as anonly dog, although she has previously lived with another small dog and may be able to live with a calm companion following careful introductions.

River may not be suited to everyone, but for the right home she will be an incredibly loyal, affectionate and rewarding companion who has so much love to give.

A bit about River from her previous home:

Quiver, Trevor, Clever Trevor, Trev, Piggy, Pig, Squirrel, Egg Nog Dog

Riverwill need more than one meet and greet with her prospective adopters to allow her time to build a bond and feel comfortable with them before being adopted.

Just wanted to share quick update for our wonderful River! Her kennel mum has been working on showing River that visiting the vets isn’t always a scary ordeal. Today, I popped in with her to see how it was going. I was SO impressed!

Although she was hesitant to come in, once in the vets she settled enough to have the biggest cuddles (River gives the BEST cuddles). We spent 5 minutes or so telling her how beautiful and amazing she is and made sure to head out before she got too worried.

River is honestly one of those special, once in a lifetime dogs. Once she bonds with you, she has this incredible ability to make you feel like the most important person in the world. She gives you her whole heart. Whoever adopts her will be so lucky to have her in their life!

Our gorgeous girl River is still on the search for a new home and family and hopefully her forever one. River is continuing to do very well here at the rescue and has progressed so much since being back with us.

Having lived in a home River knows a lot of commands and has been very well taught. She's clean in her kennel and isn't destructive at all. Although, she has lived in a home before she does still struggle at times with busy environments and people she doesn't know. When shes greeted by someone she doesn't know she will bark out of uncertainty but if you offer her a treat she tends to make friends with you pretty quickly. There were only a couple of people who could work with River initially when she came back to us due to her being so overwhelmed and worried but now half the rescue can work with her so shes made massive progress and I couldn't be any prouder of her.

She walks lovely on the lead and absolute loves going out for walks or down the big yard where she also loves to play fetch. We have been training River to understand that going to the vets isn't all that scary. We have been desensitising her by walking her up the vet steps and just sitting there for a moment praising her. Before, she wouldn't even go up the steps so for her to do this and sit there for a moment is massive for her. Our next step will be to open the vets door and go from there. River is such a special girl and when she trusts you she loves you with her whole heart and she genuinely does make you feel so loved. Being Rivers Kennel Mum is honestly the best and having all that love and affection from her is just utterly amazing.

River is continuing to do incredibly well here and is trusting more and more people she meets each day. It’s been lovely to see her confidence grow as she builds good relationships with other people. As part of her ongoing desensitisation, we’ve been walking River around the front of the rescue and gradually up towards the vets. This helps her learn that these environments are safe and nothing to worry about. Throughout this training we give her plenty of praise and tasty treats, and she is responding really well.

Considering everything she’s working through, she’s doing brilliantly. River is muzzle and Halti trained, as she can be reactive to certain dogs and can be strong when she first heads out on walks. We are working on her reactivity daily, and she is improving.

Her future adopters will need to continue with this training to help her keep progressing and feeling secure. River is an absolute darling. She has such a loving nature and truly makes you feel so loved when you spend time with her. With the right support and understanding, she would make a wonderful companion and a very special addition to the right home

River is a gentle girl who has previously lived in a home and is already kennel clean, giving her a great foundation for her next chapter. She can be a little cautious with new people at first, but with time and patience, she soon shows her sweet, affectionate side. Once she trusts you, River forms a lovely bond and just wants to be close to her people. She’s looking for a kind, understanding home that will give her the reassurance and love she deserves.

These wonderful souls may be older, have medical needs, or come with a few unique quirks—but they have endless love to give. Full of personality and deserving of a second chance, they come with a reduced adoption fee to help them find the perfect home. Could you be the one to give them the love they’ve been waiting for?

name: Tinkerbell
description:
I need an adult-only home with ex-breeder experience that can show me love.

I need a resident dog to help me feel safe but I haven’t met cats yet so I'm not sure

I would like a secure garden to spend time in.

I’ve never been on walks before and will need a gentle introduction.

Please be patient with me, I'm nervous and learning everything from scratch.

I have been spayed and am ready for my forever home.

Tinkerbell has come to us from a breeder and has never lived in a home before, so she is understandably unsure and very sensitive to touch. She can go into panic mode if handled too quickly and may try to nip out of fear as she is simply terrified and trying to protect herself. The initial handling is what scares her most, so patience and slow, careful introductions are essential.

Since being with us, Tinkerbell has begun to settle into a routine and is starting to show very encouraging signs of progress. While she is still too worried to accept touch and may move away if approached, she is becoming more comfortable being around people she knows. She will now approach to sniff fingers, gently take treats, and has even started to offer little kisses, which is a lovely sign that she is beginning to build trust. She is also happy to relax nearby, often choosing to lie close by, which shows she is starting to feel a little safer in our presence.

Tinkerbell has made particularly good progress with being picked up. This used to be extremely stressful for her, but she is now becoming more tolerant of this with calm and gentle handling, which is a huge step forward for her.

She will still need a very experienced, adult-only home with people who have handled very scared or sensitive dogs before. Tinkerbell will also need a kind, confident resident dog to guide her and help her feel more secure. Her adopters will need to understand that she may never be a typical dog who seeks constant cuddles or fuss, but with patience, love, and gentle guidance, she can form a very special bond.

As she has never experienced home life, Tinkerbell will need continued support with house training and learning to walk on a lead. Starting with calm, gentle garden time will help her confidence as she gradually explores her new environment.

Tinkerbell was recently groomed and found the experience very stressful, so it will be important for her adopters to gently and patiently help her build positive associations through gradual desensitisation. She will need regular grooming to keep her comfortable, and her new family should be prepared to work with an experienced, understanding groomer or may need to look at having her groomed under sedation until she becomes more comfortable with this. She has been making regular trips to our grooming room to have a brush and the fur around her eyes trimmed and is getting better with this each time.

Tinkerbell is a very special girl who needs an equally special home willing to invest time, TLC, and understanding. With consistency, patience, and love, she has the potential to form a unique bond and thrive in a safe, nurturing environment.

This week at the rescue we’ve had a big heatwave which resulted in a lot of our dogs needing to be shaved, including my beautiful Tinkerbell as she started to struggle having quite a thick coat. She has the most beautiful fur and she keeps herself so clean and dry, but it had to come off for her comfort. She was taken into the vets to be sedated again, but only because the weather has been so warm.. it would have been too hot to put her through the stress of shaving her all off with no sedation. I have been taking her into the grooming room regularly to brush her and trim the hair around her eyes, and she does definitely get better and better each time, so this is a good sign!! The next time she’s due a shave, I will try again without taking her into the vets to see how she does.

Even though Tinks is still a nervous girl, especially around people she doesn’t know, she is still progressing slowly and steadily. She’s still very reliant on other dogs and tends to copy them a lot of the time, so this has been good for her learning to trust me. She’s watched so many other dogs come up to me for a fuss, take treats from me, and just be comfortable in general being around me, and she is following suit.. just at her own pace. And that’s okay!

She is SUCH a sweet girl who has clearly been through a lot, and it’s not her fault she has such a strong guard up. I absolutely adore working with her and I understand and accept her for who she is. Seeing her get excited over toys and treats, and greeting me with a waggy tail in the mornings really makes me smile, and it gives me hope for her future. She deserves adopters who will love and cherish her the way she always should have been, regardless of if the feelings are mutual.. but I do think she is capable of loving and trusting.. she just needs to find herself with the right people.

Over the last few weeks, our lovely Tinks has reached a huge milestone. After seeing how well she has been progressing with her handling, we felt it was time to take the next step — harness training.

For a dog who finds handling so worrying, we knew there was a strong chance she might not accept wearing a harness. But unless we try, we’ll never know.

Tinks’ other kennel mummy and I took her into the grooming room, where we carefully introduced and fitted her harness. As expected, she was a little unsure and understandably a bit overwhelmed, but she made us both incredibly proud with how she coped. The next step was returning her to her kennel to see how she would manage wearing it throughout the day. Many of our more worried dogs will freeze or refuse to move in this situation, but not our Tinks. She carried on with her day as normal, moving around comfortably with the harness on.

To some, this might not seem like a huge achievement, but for Tinkerbell, it is a massive step in the right direction. Over the coming weeks, we will continue to build on this by practicing daily, with the hope that we can eventually begin introducing a lead in the yard.

With a dog like Tinkerbell, progress takes time, patience, and understanding. Thankfully, she has all of that in abundance, and we will never give up on her.

Tinkerbell has settled into my routine at the rescue beautifully now, so I’m finally starting to see glimpses of the real her. Her guard is definitely still up, but it has dropped slightly and I can tell she’s a lot more comfortable being around me than she used to be. She still won’t allow me to touch her and will bolt away if I go towards her, but she’s approaching me to sniff my fingers, sometimes give me kisses, and taking treats from me every single day now. She also feels content enough to lay near me when I’m sat in the yard eating my lunch, often with her back towards me which I guess is a sign that she does trust me!

One thing she has improved massively with is being picked up. I mentioned in my last update I still had to use a thick blanket and she would still sometimes try to bite (just not as often), but that’s even better again now. This week I was able to pick her up with no blanket, which is HUGE for Tinks!!

Because of how much better she had gotten, I thought I would attempt a full groom - going into it with absolutely no expectations. She allowed me to shave her back, but that’s as far as we could get. She started to get more and more stressed and I really didn’t want to push her and ruin the work we’ve done, so I took her into the vets so she could be sedated to complete the groom. So, she’s now shaved (minus her INCREDIBLE eyelashes), and I think she looks absolutely beautiful. Now that we’ve started fresh with her coat, I’m going to start taking her into the grooming room just once a week or so, and gently brush her etc to get her more used to it all.

Tinkerbell has made slow and steady progress since she’s been on my section and I’m so proud of how brave she’s trying to be. I can tell she wants to come out of her shell a bit more and join in with the rest of the dogs, but she’s not there just quite yet. Something big did happen last week though!! During dinner time I was hand feeding one of her friends, and Tinks came over and started licking the food off my hand for the first time!!!! I was absolutely over the moon and so happy that she felt comfortable enough to do that. Since then she’s done it nearly every day and has also started to take treats from me too if they’re yummy enough. I know this probably won’t sound like a lot to most people, but for a dog like Tinberbell it’s huge. For me, taking food from my hand is usually the first step in earning trust with my scared dogs, so I think we’re heading in the right direction.

We have also made a small amount of progress with Tinkerbell’s fear of being picked up. She still doesn’t like it - at all - but she’s not panicking as much any more and definitely doesn’t try to bite me as much as she did before. I still have to use a thick blanket and be very slow and calm with her, but I think she is starting to slowly realise that I’m not going to hurt her. I trimmed her fringe again a few days ago as her hair seems to grow quite quickly, so I took this opportunity to stroke her head gently for a little while once I was done. She actually started to close her eyes after a minute or so and I think she may have been enjoying it, or at least I hope she was.I adore this little lady and I’m going to do everything I can to show her what love and kindness is. I just hope a special someone will come along and feel the same way about her soon.

Tinkerbell has recently been moved to my section, as she struggles a great deal with being touched and handled by us humans. At present, she goes into complete panic mode whenever I go to pick her up, and she will try to bite to protect herself. This doesn’t mean she’s a nasty dog at all; she’s literally just so scared she will do anything she can to stop it happening. Once I have her comfortably in my arms, she will actually settle really well and will allow me to do things such as give her a gentle fuss and trim the hair on her face. I think the initial picking up is what scares her the most so this is what we’ll work on slowly together.

Tinkerbell is going to need a very experienced home with people who have had terrified dogs before. She’s going to need a lot of work and TLC put into her, and also a lot of understanding. Her adopters will need to accept the fact that she may never be a normal dog, and she may never be a dog who wants fusses or cuddles, but love her anyway. She’s a very special girl, and she needs a home equally as special to help her and give her what she needs.

I’m going to work as closely as I can with her over the next couple of weeks, and hopefully I can earn her trust and prove to her that she doesn’t need to be afraid of me. I’ll keep you all updated!

Tinkerbell has come to us from a breeder and can be a little unsure at first. She is touch-sensitive and may need time to feel comfortable, but once she calms down, she begins to show her gentle, sweet side. Tinkerbell will need an adult-only home that can handle her sassy personality and provide the patience she needs to settle in at her own pace.

She will also need a kind and confident resident dog to guide her and take her under their wing as she adjusts. Tinkerbell is looking for someone to teach her about trust, companionship, and the joys of home life.

As she has never lived in a home before, her adopters will need to be gentle and understanding while she learns about house training and walking on a lead. Starting with a few gentle garden sniffs will be perfect for her confidence as she begins to explore her new world.

Tinkerbell is a little gem who, with time and love, will grow into a devoted and affectionate companion.

Through the generosity of a Gift of Life sponsor, this dog was able to leave behind an uncertain future in Ireland and find safety and care at Many Tears. Every sponsorship helps us rescue and save more dogs in desperate need. If you would like to become part of a rescue story, please consider donating to our Gift of Life fund.

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Pushkin
description:
I need a calm and quiet, adult-only home where I can settle in at my own pace.

I will need a kind resident dog to take me under their wing. I have never met cats.

I would like a secure garden to spend time in.

I’ve never been on walks before and will need a gentle introduction.

I have never lived in a home so I will need help to learn house training.

I will be spayed before finding my forever home.

18-06-26 Pushkin has come to us from a breeder and is now searching for a loving forever home. She is initially quite nervous and unsure, but once you spend some time with her she starts to come out of her shell and even begins to enjoy a fuss.

Pushkin is a sweet girl who simply needs understanding and the chance to build her confidence at her own pace. She will be looking for a calm and quiet home where she can settle in gradually and continue to blossom. She takes a great deal of comfort from the company of other dogs and becomes quite worried and stressed when left on her own. Because of this, she will need at least one kind and confident resident dog in her new home to take her under their wing and show her that the world isn't such a frightening place.

Pushkin has never experienced life in a home before, so her new family will need to be kind, patient and understanding as she learns all about home life and new skills like house training. She will also need to learn all about walking on a lead and harness. She will need an adult-only home that has ex-breeding experience and can allow her to settle in at her own pace. She will need a garden fence of at least 6ft to ensue her safety while she settles into her home.

With plenty of love, reassurance and the support of a canine friend, Pushkin will continue to grow in confidence and become a wonderful companion. She is a lovely girl with so much potential, and we can't wait to see her enjoying the happy life she truly deserves.

Pushkin has been given the Gift of Life by Cherry Lewis

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Nora
description:
I need a calm and quiet home where I can settle in at my own pace.

I need a resident dog to help me settle in and I have never met cats.

I will need a secure garden while I get used to my new home.

I’ve never been on walks before and will need a gentle introduction.

I have never lived in a home so I will need help to learn house training.

Nora has come to us from a breeder and is now looking for a very special forever home. She is a scared girl who is understandably feeling overwhelmed to find herself here and is desperately in need of kindness, patience and TLC as she begins to settle and learn that the world can be a safe place.

When Nora first arrived with us, she was showing some neurological symptoms and ataxia, and her back legs would occasionally slide from underneath her, causing her to stumble. Following investigations with our vets, it was found that her proprioception was delayed in all four limbs. She was trialled on a course of medication alongside crate rest and has made wonderful progress. Her proprioception is now normal, she is able to get around and play happily, and she enjoys life much more comfortably. She does still have a slight amount of ataxia and can occasionally limp still. Our vet feels that she may have suffered an acute injury at some point in the past which has largely resolved itself, though she will likely never present like a normal dog.

Nora will be looking for adopters who will love and cherish her just as she is. Due to her mobility history, she will need a home without slippery flooring, or one where plenty of rugs and runners are in place to help her move around confidently and safely.

Since arriving with us, Nora has slowly begun to show glimpses of the gentle and affectionate dog she has the potential to become. Like many nervous dogs, she can be vocal when she feels unsure, and her deep, defensive howl is very much in keeping with her Beagle heritage. However, with patience and a calm approach, she soon settles and begins to reveal her softer side.

Although Nora is still learning to feel comfortable around people, she is making steady progress. She is beginning to choose to settle near her carers and, once she has had time to relax, becomes much more accepting of gentle interaction. In these quieter moments she has allowed us to stroke her and handle her gently, which is a hugely positive step for a dog who finds the world so overwhelming.

It is clear that Nora's worries stem not from a dislike of people, but from uncertainty. Having had such limited life experiences, she simply does not yet understand who we are or what to expect from us. As her confidence grows, she becomes increasingly willing to be approached and reassured, and each small step forward is a testament to her resilience.

Nora would be best suited to a calm and quiet home with adopters who have experience of nervous ex-breeding dogs and understand her worries. She will need time to settle at her own pace and will thrive with patient adopters who are happy to let her progress in her own time. We believe that what Nora needs most now is the stability, security and reassurance that only a loving home can provide.

She takes great comfort from the company of other dogs and will need at least one other kind and confident resident dog in her new home to show her the way and help her build confidence. While Nora has successfully shared her kennel with smaller dogs, we feel she would be happiest living alongside a dog of a similar size or larger.

Nora has never experienced life in a home before so her new family will need to be understanding as she learns all about home life and new skills like house training. She does not yet walk on a lead and harness, and while she remains with us we will be working on introducing her to a harness and helping her gain the confidence she needs to begin exploring the outside world safely. Initially, she will need to practise wearing her harness and exploring safely within the home and garden before she is ready to venture further afield.

Although Nora is still very worried and unsure of the world around her, beneath her fears is a sweet and deserving little girl who simply needs someone to believe in her. Watching a scared dog slowly learn to trust is one of the most rewarding experiences, and with love, patience and understanding, Nora will blossom. The day she greets you with a wagging tail and a sparkle in her eyes will make every moment of that journey worthwhile.

Nora has been given the Gift of Life by Judie Fisher

Nora is so gentle and if you ignore the deep defensive howl (she is a beagle after all!) eventually she quietens down and she shows her soft side.Nora is still trying to get used to enjoying human company. She is not there yet but is slowly beginning to show signs of trusting people and does settle near you with a little patience.

It appears to me that it's not that Nora dislikes people, it's more a matter of how she is unsure of who we are and how we do things.

Once she  settles and it does take a little time, although still afraid, is much more willing to be approached by people.

In this calmer state, I was able to smooth her coat all over and gently stroke her without her worrying too much which is a positive step for her.

Nora will need this gentle approach for some time but the reward will be endless love and loyalty which is priceless.

I believe all Nora needs right now is the stability and security that a loving home can offer.Nora does much prefer the company of dogs  at the moment so it is a must that there is a resident dog already in the home for her to be with.

Nora has kennelled with small dogs but we do feel she would be better placed with a dog of a similar size or larger.

Our next step whilst Nora is with us will be to train her to walk on a harness so she can start to discover and enjoy the outside world.

When Nora first arrived with us, she was showing some neurological symptoms and ataxia, and her back legs would occasionally slide from underneath her, causing her to stumble. Following investigations with our vets, it was found that her proprioception was delayed in all four limbs. She was trialed on a course of medication alongside crate rest and has made wonderful progress. Her proprioception is now normal, she is able to get around and play happily, and she enjoys life much more comfortably. She does still have a slight amount of ataxia and can occasionally limp still. Our vet feels that she may have suffered an acute injury at some point in the past which has largely resolved itself, though she will likely never present like a normal dog.

She would be best suited to a calm and quiet home with adopters who have experience of nervous ex-breeding dogs and understand her worries. Nora will need time to settle at her own pace and will thrive with patient adopters who are happy to let her progress in her own time. She takes great comfort from the company of other dogs and will need at least one other kind and confident resident dog in her new home to show her the way and help her build confidence.

Nora has never experienced life in a home before so her new family will need to be understanding as she learns all about home life and new skills like house training. She doesn't yet walk on a lead and harness and initially she will need to practise wearing her harness and exploring safely within the home and garden before she is ready to venture further afield.

Through the generosity of a Gift of Life sponsor, this dog was able to leave behind an uncertain future in Ireland and find safety and care at Many Tears. Every sponsorship helps us rescue and save more dogs in desperate need. If you would like to become part of a rescue story, please consider donating to our Gift of Life fund.

These wonderful souls may be older, have medical needs, or come with a few unique quirks—but they have endless love to give. Full of personality and deserving of a second chance, they come with a reduced adoption fee to help them find the perfect home. Could you be the one to give them the love they’ve been waiting for?

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Flash
description:
I need a calm and quiet, adult only home with ex-breeding experience to understand me

I would like to be the only animal in my new home.

I would like a good sized, secure garden to explore.

I can walk on a lead but find this very overwhelming at the moment.

I have entropion in both eyes, which have been repaired by the vets.

Flash is a truly striking Standard Poodle with a big personality, a sensitive soul, and an oversized puppy spirit. He first came to us after his previous owners could no longer give him the time and exercise he needed. Since then, we’ve watched him grow from a worried boy into a much more confident and affectionate companion, and he continues to make us proud every single day.

Flash is still very much a character, goofy, playful, and full of energy but beneath his bouncy exterior lies a gentle heart that just wants to love and be loved. He can be sensitive in new situations and still finds the world a little overwhelming at times, but with people he trusts, his sweet and loyal nature shines through. Once Flash bonds with you, he is yours for life.

Recently, he’s been showing us more of his loving side. On rainy days when we huddle together out of the downpour, he rolls over for belly rubs, brings his toys for attention, and happily drifts off to sleep during a cuddle. It’s in these quiet moments that his true personality comes out, affectionate, trusting, and eager to please.

Flash’s training is going from strength to strength. He’s mastered “touch” (booping his nose to your hand) and is now learning “place.” He still gets a little mouthy when he’s overexcited, but he’s come a long way since his arrival and continues to improve with patience and consistency. He’ll need his future family to have ex-breeding experience and to continue this gentle guidance in the home so he can keep building on all the progress he’s made.

Being an active breed, Flash loves his walks and enjoys exploring the world at his own pace. He’s happiest in calm, quiet areas where he can relax and take everything in without feeling rushed. He will need to be the only pet in the home, as he prefers human companionship over other animals, and he’s not a fan of pigeons or seagulls either!

Flash has had successful surgery for entropion in both eyes, and though he still needs a little care for mild dermatitis around them. He is currently being treated for sore and sensitive ears.

What Flash truly needs now is a calm, understanding, adult-only home with ex-breeding experience and with someone who’s around most of the time, someone who can offer him patience, structure, and all the love he deserves. With time and trust, Flash will repay his person a hundred times over with loyalty, affection, and that special spark that makes him so unforgettable.

Please don’t overlook this beautiful boy. Flash has come so far and is ready to start the next chapter of his life he’s just waiting for that one person to believe in him and bring him home.

Flash will need more than one meet and greet with his prospective adopters to allow him time to build a bond and feel comfortable with them before being adopted.

Flash has officially been waiting for a home for a whole year. After a first few months of (a lot of) patience and training, it's then blossomed into the most incredible remaining months where we’ve really seen Flash’s true heart and soul. This dog is phenomenal in more ways than one, and it’s so sad he’s now become part of the MT furniture, and that the rescue has now become his home.

So for Flash’s 1 year anniversary, I took him off site to the nearby country park for a lovely stroll, followed by a visit to McD’s for some chicken nuggets.

Flash was suuuuuuch a good boy on his walk. He took in all the new smells, and took the new environment in his stride. He was even approached by 3 off lead small dogs, and didn’t react at all. He trotted along the park, with the world at his feet and it was the most amazing thing to see.

He is an absolute darling in the car, so we made a swift visit to McD’s for a box of 20 nuggets to eat together in our on site flat.

It was so nice to see Flash in a home like environment, he jumped straight on the sofa, and wandered around inspecting every room. We sat on the floor together eating our nuggets, and he eventually rested on the raised bed and had a little snooze.

It was heartbreaking putting him back in his kennel, when he seemed so at peace and settled in the flat. I just wish there was someone out there who could see him for everything he is and more!!!

Our beautiful, bonkers Flash has learnt a new trick and we thought we would share.

Flash has also been enjoying the sunshine with lots of walks and plays in the yards this week.

Flash continues to work hard on growing his calmness around new people and he is doing ok. He has now been 10 months here at the rescue and has an increasing fan base amongst the staff but he of course longs for a home and people of his own.

When I first started working with him, I was apprehensive. He was a much larger dog than I was used to working with, and he would mouth you til his hearts content. Not because he is a nasty dog, or means any harm whatsoever, but simply because he has come from a home (just out of puppyhood) and he has become extremely anxious and unsettled being in a kennel environment. On top of that, he has been taught 0 manners which definitely didn’t help with the matter!!! I struggled to bond with him, I struggled to understand why he wouldn’t listen when being told, and why all he wanted to do was treat my arm like a toy…

However I found myself thinking, what has Flash been through in his life that was ever HIS fault? He didn’t ask to spend so much of his life in kennels. He never asked to be put in situations where his anxiety gets the better of him, it was never his decision to not be trained to behave normally. So I decided to buck up my ideas and realise he’s not as scary as he seems, and he’s probably just as anxious being around me as I was him. And from that moment on, our relationship has blossomed…

This boy is truly a superstar in every sense of the word. He is SUCH a goofball, and can have you in fits of laughter within seconds of being with him. His personality is infectious, and as soon as he is comfortable with you and realises you are no threat to him, he shines like the star he is. It’s gutting that Flash doesn’t allow every staff member to see all sides of him. The side that shows his softness, his sweetness, his kindness. But that just makes me so much more grateful that he feels comfortable enough around me to do so.

You can see from his pictures, Flash is muzzle trained. Not because he is an aggressive dog, not because he is a violent dog, it’s simply due to his anxiousness. On one hand, Flash is a super intelligent Poodle but on the other hand, he doesn’t really show it. His anxiety gets the better of him, and he puts himself in uncomfortable positions with new people & then panics whilst he’s in it. It is SUPER important that whoever adopts Flash’s main priority is keeping him safe, and keeping him from making decisions that he feels uncertain about. He wants to make friends with everyone, but also decides halfway through some interactions that he’s not to keen on the thought too!

Among (of course) all of the other precious angels I look after the rescue, Flash is one of the reasons I get excited to go to work. You shouldn’t really have favourites, but Flash does take the cake. I cherish every single moment I get with him. Our lovely strolls up the country lanes, our snuggles in the back of his kennel after a long day, our zoomies down our big play yard. Every single moment & memory I look forward to recreating every single day. Nothing tops being around this dog, nothing else matters when you’re with Flash.

If I was in a position to take him home & work with him through his quirks, I absolutely would, in heartbeat in fact. There is just something so special about him, and I truly envy whoever is fortunate to adopt this incredible young man. And for as long as Flash remains in kennels, I will advocate for him, make him feel safe, comfortable and most of all loved.

Flash has completely changed my mind about Poodles. I am absolutely in love with him. He might not be the sharpest boy in the bunch, but he more than makes up for it with his goofy charm and endless silliness.We are still working on his mouthing when he becomes overexcited. Progress is steady, and every small step forward feels like a real achievement. Flash loves his freedom. We take daily trips to the big yard where he runs around like the happiest whirlwind you have ever seen. He would really benefit from visits to private dog fields where he can safely zoom and explore to his heart’s content.Meeting new people can still be a little tricky for him, but we have a careful plan in place to help him do so safely and comfortably. For those of us who know Flash well, he shows a side many would not expect. Beneath his anxiousness and the need for a muzzle at times is the sweetest, softest boy who adores snuggles and head tickles.He loves to stay close, gently resting his head in your lap until he drifts off to sleep. At the end of each day, we share a quiet moment together, curled up side by side until his eyes close and he is peacefully dreaming.Flash truly is a special boy who deserves a very special home. He needs someone who can offer love, patience, and understanding. In return, he will give unwavering loyalty and companionship for life.

Flash is an absolute character, this boy is full of life and once he knows you he adores you but his introductions to new people always need to be slow as this sensitive boy can be easily overwhelmed.

Flash works well within a routine, he has an abundance of energy and a super inquisitive nature which can sometimes lead to him becoming quite lively, he will often revert to ‘mouthing’ and jumping up when he is uncomfortable or unsure.

Flash has a super inquisitive nature and loves to observe what’s going on at a distance. This boy can also be an absolute cuddle monster and adores a snuggle with his favourite people, and will fall asleep next to us when we have the opportunity to sit quietly with him. One of flash’s unique traits is his love of a pamper session! I have never known a dog who enjoys a bath and hairdryer the way he does and he becomes quite docile under the hair dryer whilst having a gentle brush.

Flash’s ideal home would be a quiet an experienced dog owning home (adults only) with humans that have the patience and time to help grow his confidence and who also understand the need to grow calmness in him. Flash has had a few dog friends at the rescue but his high spirted nature can be a bit much for some dogs and he does not seem to like male dogs, for this reason a home where he is the only pooch would suit him best.

Flash walks well on lead but can become very excited when he sees other dogs, walks in quiet areas would be perfect for him, he loves to be outdoors and often just enjoys sitting in the yard with me watching birds, but he will equally enjoy time spent just hanging out with his humans at home. Flash really is a wonderfully, lovable, giant puppy, looking for kind and understanding humans to provide him with the security and love he deserves.

Flash has had a brilliant few weeks. He celebrated his second Birthday and was treated with his first snow day, which he loved frolicking about in. Unfortunately, he did have to spend his birthday in kennels, but I made sure it was still a very special day for him. We hopped in the car, and I took him for a little walk before heading to the pet shop to pick out some birthday treats. He chose a dinosaur toy and a knuckle bone, which he thoroughly enjoyed. I also bought him some special birthday biscuits to keep him going through the week.

After that, I truly spoiled him, and we decided to go to Burger King for a burger! Flash was quite confused at first, but once he had a taste, he couldn’t get enough.

Flash truly is a special boy and will need a special home to match. He can be very wary of strangers and doesn’t always show clear signs if he’s uncomfortable. For this reason, he is muzzled when out on walks, which I make sure to do whenever we go out.

Flash will need an adult-only home with no visiting children. He cannot live with cats or any small animals

The stunning Flash had a little pamper today, which he absolutely loved. He was as good as gold whilst in the bath and sat very contentedly on the grooming table looking out the window enjoying the warmth of the blowers. I have never known a dog to enjoy bath time as much as he does, he becomes quite sleepy and cuddley, it’s the cutest thing.

After his wash and blow dry he got to open some Christmas treats out in the yard sent in from the lovely folks out there that support Many Tears, Flash particular enjoyed the food presents.

Flash really is the most wonderful boy once you get to know him, and we see improvement every day in his sometimes lively and skittish nature. This fantastic boy will make the most awesome and loyal of friend with the person or couple that are prepared to put the time and love into him he so deserves.

Today, Flash and I went on a little adventure to the local pet shop, finishing off with a much-needed pupcup! He was an absolute angel in the car—he hopped right in, buckled up, and sat calmly for the whole journey. I watched him in the mirror as he gazed out at the world passing by. When we arrived, we popped his muzzle on as a precaution since he can sometimes be wary of strangers. Flash wandered around the shop, taking in all the sights and smells, until he discovered the pick-and-mix treats—which of course we had to get. After choosing his goodies, he rummaged through the plushies and picked out a squeaky monkey to take home.

As we headed toward the till, another dog suddenly rushed around a corner and startled Flash, causing him to react. I managed to whisk him away with a distraction, but he became very on edge and tucked himself into me for comfort. A lovely lady then came around the opposite corner and, despite having seen his reaction, kindly stopped to say hello. She happened to be a previous Many Tears adopter, and her own pup is doing wonderfully. She chose to ignore Flash at first to give him space, and once he settled, he allowed her to gently stroke his head. After wishing us well, we carried on with our day.

We sat outside Costa in the sunshine, and Flash absolutely loved his pupcup, so much so that he managed to get it all over his nose.

I’m incredibly proud of Flash today. It was his first outing since arriving in kennels, and I could really see how scared he can be underneath it all, and how much he relies on his human for reassurance. Flash needs someone devoted, patient, and willing to put time into training and working through his reactivity toward other dogs. He truly is a special boy.

Another bleak day at the rescue with the rain pouring down non-stop, but it meant I got to spend some more quality time with the dogsI’ve never been one for poodles but Flash really has stolen my heartToday I really did see his loving and caring side as we cuddled away from the downpour. He rolled around, brought me his toys and chews before receiving belly rubs and head scratches until he fell asleep

If you’ve been to the rescue I’m usually walking Flash around the reception area in order for him to build his relationship with humans and other dogs but it was nice to just have some one on one time with him today, once you’ve created a bond with this boy he is yours for life.Flash’s’ training is still continuing and has managed to learn ‘touch’ where he boops his nose to the designated hand and is in the midst of learning ‘place’.

In a home will still need some training in order for him to thrive.

Flash is still a little mouthy when he gets super excited but is doing much better to when he first arrived with us.

It's been almost 4 months since Flash arrived here and I feel he has been very much overlooked. Deep down he’s a big cuddle bug and thrives on attention.

I tried spending most of my time with him today as kennel life is very stressful for him. We visited the big yard multiple times, went for a stroll and even met 2 dogs on a walk and greeted them with such kindness. Might I say he walks beautifully alongside you!

We met some new people in the reception area, which can sometimes make Flash a little uneasy but he did fantastically and even accepted some treats. For this he is muzzle trained as he can be mouthy when he’s a little too excited!

The last task of the day was to be groomed. I never thought I would see this boy sit still for more than a few seconds, but he amazed me and hopped up onto the grooming table ready to be pampered! And he now looks so good! Flash really doesn’t mind being groomed which will be helpful for the future.

I absolutely adore this beautiful boy. Flash is best described as an oversized puppy at heart. He has a playful, goofy nature but sometimes forgets his size, which can make him a little overexcited and mouthy, especially if he’s unsure or feeling frustrated.

Flash can be a sensitive soul who is often scared of his own shadow and needs plenty of reassurance and gentle confidence-building when faced with new experiences (like the umbrella he discovered today!). Because of this, kennel life is very tough for him, and I truly believe he will thrive so much more in a loving home.

Once Flash trusts you, he shows his affectionate side. He adores snuggling in for a cuddle and loves being pampered.

Flash’s ideal home would be an adult-only household where he can be the only dog. He isn’t aggressive with other dogs but can become boisterous and overwhelming. A quiet location with a secure, good-sized garden would be perfect, ideally with adopters who enjoy long walks and hikes.

He walks beautifully on the lead, enjoys all weathers, and has the stamina to go for hours. While he may sometimes react to new situations or get excitable around other dogs, his confidence and manners are improving every day. Flash will need adopters who are home most of the time or able to include him in their daily lives, as he finds being left alone difficult and may struggle with separation anxiety.

Flash is a truly striking Standard Poodle who has come to us from a home as his owners no longer had time for him and couldn't offer him the exercise he requires. Although he has lived in a home before, Flash is a worried boy who finds new experiences and people quite daunting. Because of this, Flash is looking for an experienced, adult only home with adopters who have plenty of time, patience, and love to give. He needs adopters who won’t give up on him and who will allow him to settle in at his own pace.Initially, Flash can be wary of new people and unsure of their intentions, but once you’ve gained his trust, he begins to show his affectionate and loyal nature. He forms strong bonds with those he feels safe around and shows a deep need for companionship. He does not cope well with being left alone and has shown signs of separation anxiety, getting very worried and anxious when left on his own. He will need an adopter who is able to be with him all of the time and who will work on this with him, slowly building up any time he is left. We are told he was crate trained in his previous home, which may help with this process.Flash is particularly sensitive to loud or sudden noises, which can cause him to panic. He feels safest in quiet, familiar surroundings and finds walks outside of the rescue grounds very difficult at the moment. When taken beyond his comfort zone, he becomes overwhelmed and spins in circles in an attempt to return to safety. While he can walk on a lead, his adopters will need to help him grow in confidence very gradually and may need to begin with simply enjoying time in the garden or short walks in peaceful areas.Flash would be best suited to being homed as an only dog as he can be grumbly when meeting other dogs. This is something his adopters will need to work on with him with patience and positive reinforcement. Because of this, he must only be allowed off lead in secure areas with no other dogs present. He also cannot live with cats or small animals and we are told he isn't a fan of pigeons or seagulls.Flash will be looking for a calm and quiet home, where he can settle in at his own pace and where his adopters will help him settle into a consistent routine so he can feel safe and secure. We are told that he is house trained but he may need some reminders of this in his new homeAlthough he is currently worried about going for walks, he is an active breed who will need to be kept both mentally and physically stimulated. We have been told that he has previously done some agility and scent work, so once his confidence grows this is something he may like to explore again in the future. His previous owners also said he enjoyed being groomed and bathed, though we haven’t yet introduced this at the rescue.This beautiful boy has so much potential in the right hands. If you have the time, experience, and understanding to give Flash the security and stability he needs, he will reward you with trust, loyalty, and love. Please don’t overlook him, he’s waiting for that one special person to believe in him.Flash has an entropion in both eyes, which has been repaired by our vets but does have some dermatitis around his eyes.

These wonderful souls may be older, have medical needs, or come with a few unique quirks—but they have endless love to give. Full of personality and deserving of a second chance, they come with a reduced adoption fee to help them find the perfect home. Could you be the one to give them the love they’ve been waiting for?

name: Lassie
description:
I need an adult-only home where I can feel safe and loved.

I need a resident dog to help me settle in and I cannot live with cats.

I would like a secure garden to spend time in.

I can walk well on a lead and would love an active home.

I have never lived in a home so I will need help to learn house training.

I have been spayed and am ready for my forever home.

Lassie has come to us from a breeder to find her forever home. When she first arrived she was a very nervous girl as she had not had much experience with people, so everything felt very new and overwhelming to her. She could be quite skittish when approached and was worried about meeting new people and being handled. Since then her confidence has started to really grow. Once she gets to know you, she is the most loyal, loving and affectionate girl and it's so wonderful to see.

Lassie will be looking for a calm and quiet, adult only home where she can settle in at her own pace. She would benefit from adopters who have breed experience and experience with worried dogs.

She will be looking for a home where there was at least one other kind and playful resident dog to be her friend and guide. She gets on brilliantly with all dogs here out and about and socialises well with a variety of breeds and sizes. However, we have found that she is most comfortable sharing her home with other Collies and will therefore need a home with at least one resident Collie. She cannot be homed with cats or small furries. She doesn't like to share her food with other dogs, so her adopters would need to ensure she was fed separately and would need to separate when feeding any long last treats or chews too.

Lassie now walks well on a lead and would love to find an active home where she can go on lots of walks and adventures with her new family and where her clever collie brain can be kept both mentally and physically stimulated. She is only used to walking in quiet areas, so would need gentle introduction to any busier places. Lassie has never lived in a home before so her adopters will need to be kind and understanding as she settles into the home and learns all about home life and new skills like house training.

With time, patience, and lots of love, Lassie will blossom. Once she has settled and gained confidence, she will become a cracking addition to her new family and will enjoy all the adventures life has to offer.

Lassie will need more than one meet and greet with her prospective adopters to allow her time to build a bond and feel comfortable with them before being adopted.

Lassie spent some time in the office today and coped really well. She happily tucked into a chicken foot for a tasty treat, although she couldn't resist pinching her kennel friend's one too! She was very curious about everything going on around her and enjoyed exploring her surroundings.

A member of staff she hadn't met before came into the office, and although Lassie was a little wary at first, she quickly settled and went back to happily enjoying her chicken foot. It was lovely to see her take the new experience in her stride and continue growing in confidence.

Beautiful beautiful Lassie! What a collie she is, she is so bright and has really come on so much with her basic training. Now knowing "sit" and "lie down". When we're out walking and a car is approaching, all I have to do is ask her to the verge and she sits and waits beautifully until we're able to start walking again. She really doesn't seem to be fazed by much traffic, but is very fixated with sheep if she comes across any. She passes horses lovely and will happily walk past other dogs no issue, but isn't keen on them approaching her. She's a big fan of food which helps with her training.She just needs people that understand that the girl she is when they first meet her is very worried but once she trusts you, her true personality really shines through.

Lassie is such a pretty girl who would do anything for you when she trusts you but is still wary of people initially. She did take treats off of a new person yesterday which was amazing to see. She needs to be in a collie experienced home with someone who will allow her the time to trust them at her own pace. She does love having a playfriend and she seems to be happiest when amongst other collies and collie crosses. She doesn't like sharing her food but is happy to share toys, beds and treats.

Lassie absolutely loves going out for her walks and gets very excited when she knows it’s time to head out. She really enjoys having a good sniff along the way and is always very interested in watching what’s going on in the fields as she passes by. Once she gets to know you, she is incredibly loving and affectionate, although she can take a little bit of time to warm up to new people. Lassie would still need a home with a resident dog, and she would truly thrive in a collie-experienced home where her needs and personality can be well understood.

Lassie is such a beautiful girl and once she knows you, she is the most affectionate, loyal and loving companion. She now walks well on a lead and absolutely loves her walks.

She will need a home with at least one other kind and confident resident dog who is a similar size to her and who loves to have a good play sometimes.

She doesn't like to share her food bowl, so he will need to be fed separately and this is something that her adopters will need to manage. In the right home she'll be someone's absolute best friend.

Lassie is a worried little lady who has come to us from a breeder. She has not had much experience with people, so everything feels very new and overwhelming to her. She can be quite skittish when approached, so she will need a calm, experienced home where she can be given the time and patience she needs to learn to trust.

At the moment, Lassie is scared of being touched, but she has already started to build a bond with her kennel mum and now allows gentle strokes from her. This shows that with consistency and kindness, shecanlearn to trust, and once she feels safe, she will begin to show her sweet side.

Lassie does walk on a lead, though she finds new experiences worrying, so she will need gentle introductions to the big wide world. She will also need to learn about house training and all the basics of living in a home, as these are things she has never known before.

Because Lassie is such a sensitive soul, she will need an adult-only home with no visiting children, and adopters who are happy to take things slowly at her pace. She will need at least one other kind and confident resident dog in her new home who can help her feel secure and show her the ropes. She gets on brilliantly with all dogs here and socialises well with a variety of breeds and sizes. She cannot be homed with cats or small furries.

These wonderful souls may be older, have medical needs, or come with a few unique quirks—but they have endless love to give. Full of personality and deserving of a second chance, they come with a reduced adoption fee to help them find the perfect home. Could you be the one to give them the love they’ve been waiting for?

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Archie
description:
I'd like a quiet, adult-only home to help me relax and feel safe.

I’d like to be the only dog in the home. I haven’t met cats

I would like a secure garden to spend time in.

I'm new to lead walking and need some practice!

I have lived in a home so I know the basics.

I have been neutered and have had 21 teeth removed.

07-07-26 Archie is such a sweet little man who came into our care from a breeder. He was adopted but has sadly been returned after his adopters felt they were not the right match for him.

Archie formed a very strong bond with his female owner, but found it much harder to build a relationship with her husband, who was frequently away from home and therefore had very little opportunity to gain Archie's trust. When Archie becomes closely bonded to his favourite person, he can become protective of them. In his previous home, he nipped when he was sitting with his owner and her husband approached, so his new adopters will need to understand this behaviour and be prepared to continue building his confidence in a positive, patient way.

Archie has settled back into rescue life well and is currently sharing his kennel with a lovely female dog, who he gets on very well with. He is a cuddly, affectionate little dog once he feels comfortable, although he isn't quite brave enough to take treats from our hands just yet.

Although Archie gets on well with other dogs, he can become protective of his favourite person around them, so we think he would be happiest as the only dog in the home, where he can relax and build a close bond with his new family.

As Archie has lived in a home before, he will already be familiar with the sights and sounds of home life. However, he may need a little reminding of his house training in a new environment, and he would benefit from some more practice walking on a lead.

Archie is looking for a quiet, adult-only home with adopters who have experience of the breed and of rescue dogs. He will need people who are happy to give him the time and space he needs to settle and understand that he is safe.

Archie is doing really well since returning to us and his confidence is growing all the time. He is such a loving little dog, and in the right home, we hope he will continue to thrive and become a devoted companion.

name: Emrys
description:
I am looking for an adult only home with experienced adopters.

I could live with other dogs or be the only dog. I cannot be homed with cats.

I’m active and would love a large, secure garden to let off steam.

I have lots of energy and need an active home that loves adventures.

I am in a foster home now so am learning all about home life and house training.

Emrys is such a wonderful boy. He’s currently living in a foster home where has been for a year now and has absolutely flourished with them. He originally came to us from a farm so had very little in the way of training and socialising. This meant he could find new people worrying and during a short adoption of just a few months, he struggled to get used to a male visitor and would react towards him and redirected onto another person while stressed. He was returned to his foster home following this and hasn’t put a paw out of place since.

In foster, Emrys does very well, he absolutely adores his three collie best friends and we think he would thrive in a home with a resident dog to keep him company. We would consider an experienced home adopting him as an only dog however he loves having doggy friends so would need regular playdates. Unfortunately Emrys cannot live with cats. He is looking for a collie and rescue experienced home to help him thrive. He absolutely loves his walks and as he is such a clever lad, he will need brain games to wear out his mind too! If an active home is looking for a dog to join in on a sport like agility, we are sure he would do well!

Once Emrys trusts you, he is a wonderful companion and after an exciting walk where he gets to play with his friends, he does really enjoy a cuddle and a snooze on the couch. He is looking for a quiet, adult only home who understands collie behaviour, especially collies bred to work. When introduced to people with plenty of space to move away if he gets worried, he makes friends quickly. His amazing foster home have done so well in bringing out his silly, confident side and absolutely love his quirks. He is such a brilliant boy and has been waiting very patiently for his forever family.

Emrys has a fatty lump on the right flank that does not pose as an issue and is being monitored.

Emrys has been in foster with us for the past 10 months and has proven to be a confident, affectionate collie. He loves his walks just as much as he enjoys relaxing on the sofa afterwards.

He gets along well with both dogs and people, is clean in the house, and travels happily in the car. During his time with us, Emrys has shown no signs of reactivity and has greeted all visitors kindly.

He is a truly lovely dog and would make a fantastic, loyal companion.

Emrys has been in foster with us for seven months now, and he’s a real character. He gets on well with all dogs and, since being back with us, has shown no reactive or guarding behaviours. In the right home, he will truly thrive and make a wonderful companion.

Emrys can occasionally feel nervous around new people, and when this happens he takes himself off to his safe space, which is in the garden. From there, he’ll assess the situation at his own pace before coming back inside when he’s ready to meet someone new. It’s important that he’s given the time and space to do this calmly.

Once he knows you, Emrys is an absolute joy to be around. He loves his walks and secure field runs, is calm and settled in the evenings, and is incredibly affectionate.

With his wicked personality and loving nature, Emrys will make a fantastic companion for the right person.

Emrys has been back in foster for 11 weeks now and has settled in beautifully with no problems at all. He gets on very well with the three resident dogs as well as visiting dogs, and he is friendly with visitors and strangers outside the home. Emrys is totally house clean, travels well in the car, and is very treat-orientated, which makes him easy to train. After his walks, he loves nothing more than relaxing with his foster family.

Emrys has settled back in beautifully with his foster family and hasn’t shown any issues at all. He loves racing around the garden with the resident dogs and is starting to enjoy playing with toys and bringing a ball back.

He’s now completely house trained, walks very nicely on the lead, and is happy to travel in the car.

Emrys is such a lovable boy and is coming along wonderfully. He’ll make a fantastic companion for the right home.

Emrys is a striking, high-energy Collie who came to us from a farm where his owner could no longer give him the time he needed. After arriving he quickly found himself in foster, where he received plenty of exercise, mental stimulation, and affection, everything a clever Collie thrives on.He was adopted for a short time, but sadly returned to us after displaying behaviours in the home that hadn’t been seen while in foster. His adopters told us that Emrys exhibited some guarding tendencies and could react negatively when he felt backed into a corner or overwhelmed. Because of this, he’s looking for a calm, adult-only home with experienced adopters who understand the needs of a sensitive, intelligent dog and can support him with firm but kind guidance.Emrys will need a secure garden with space to run, and a home that can provide him with plenty of structured physical and mental activity to keep him happy and settled. He’s a bright boy who’s eager to please and loves learning. With patience, consistency, and the right support, Emrys has every potential to thrive.Emrys is now back with his original foster where he has shown many lovely qualities, he is clean in the house, travels well in the car, walks nicely on the lead, and is affectionate with those he trusts. He’s also food-motivated and already learning basic commands. We believe that Emrys thrives on activity and as he has not displayed these behaviours in foster it may be because he is provided with so much stimulation and exercises. His adopters will need to keep this in mind, and ideally be very active and willing to provide him with different games and activities to keep his mind busy.Emrys could live with another confident resident dog or be the only dog in the right home. He cannot live with cats, and due to his sensitivity, he will need a home without children or visiting children.This boy has so much potential and is just waiting for the right people to help him blossom.

These wonderful souls may be older, have medical needs, or come with a few unique quirks—but they have endless love to give. Full of personality and deserving of a second chance, they come with a reduced adoption fee to help them find the perfect home. Could you be the one to give them the love they’ve been waiting for?

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Snowy
description:
I would like a home filled with love and kindness!

I need a laid back dog who is slightly larger than me in my new home.

I will need a secure garden while I get used to my new home.

I am learning to walk on a lead and harness, but it's still very new for me.

I have never lived in a home so I will need help to learn house training.

I have had 1 tooth extracted and am being treated for a ear infection.

Snowy is a very sweet little lady who has come to us from a breeder to find her forever home. When she first arrived she was quite worried and overwhelmed, but was trying so hard to be brave! She is still feeling quite timid, but once she gets to know she really enjoys having a fuss and is happy to be handled. She is an inquisitive and loving girl and once she's in a home we don’t think it will take long to come out of her shell and show her true personality.

Snowy would love to find a calm and quiet home with adopters who have experience with ex-breeding dogs and where she can settle in at her own pace and realise what it means to be a loved family member. We would consider homing her with gentle dog savvy older teenagers.

She takes great comfort from the company of other dogs, so she will need at least one kind, calm and confident resident dog in her new home to help her feel safe and settled. We have found that she is best suited to living with laid back dogs who are slightly larger than her. She can become a little jealous when other dogs are receiving attention and is more laid back with dogs who are a little bigger than her.

As Snowy has never lived in a home before, her adopters will need to be patient and understanding while she learns about home life and house training. She has started to learn all about walking on a lead and harness and while this is still very for her, she is doing so well.

With her sweet nature and growing confidence, we are sure it won’t take her long to settle into family life and flourish in a loving home.

Snowy has had 1 tooth removed at the time of her spay and has prevously been treated for an ear infection.

Snowy has been given the Gift of Life by Raychel Mount

This week Snowy has met a few new friends and has been out for her first walk! She took it all in her stride. We were very impressed with how well she has done.

She is a timid lady but really enjoys having a fuss when she gets to know you. She would love a home where she has at least one confident resident dog to help her to settle in. She is currently sharing her kennel with a laid back Scottie dog and gets along well with her, although she ideally needs a friend who is larger than her, she can be quite shy if they are too much larger than her.

She really is a sweetheart who is just looking for her chance at a new and happy life.

Snowy is a wonderful lady who has come to us from a breeder. She is happy to be handled and will shyly approach for fuss and attention, showing that she is trying so hard to be brave and really wants to make friends. Snowy is an inquisitive and loving girl and once she's in a home we don’t think it will take long to come out of her shell and show her true personality.Snowy would love to find and calm and quiet home with adopters who have experience with ex-breeding dogs and where she can settle in at her own pace and realise what it means to be a loved family member. We would consider homing her with gentle dog savvy older teenagers.She takes great comfort from the company of other dogs, so she will need at least one kind, calm and confident resident dog in her new home to help her feel safe and settled. We have found that she is best suited to living with laid back dogs who are slightly larger than her. She can become a little jealous when other dogs are receiving attention and is more laid back with dogs who are a little bigger than her.As Snowy has never lived in a home before, her adopters will need to be patient and understanding while she learns about house training and walking on a lead. With her sweet nature and growing confidence, we are sure it won’t take her long to settle into family life and flourish in a loving home.Snowy has had 1 tooth removed at the time of her spay and is being treated for infected ears.

Through the generosity of a Gift of Life sponsor, this dog was able to leave behind an uncertain future in Ireland and find safety and care at Many Tears. Every sponsorship helps us rescue and save more dogs in desperate need. If you would like to become part of a rescue story, please consider donating to our Gift of Life fund.

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Max
description:
I need an active, breed experienced home that loves playtime!

I’m active and would love a large, secure garden to let off steam.

I walk on the lead but I can pull when excited.

I am house trained but I need to learn some manners.

I will need to be neutered when I am of age! It's important!

Max is a superstar Lurcher who has been returned to us from his adoptive family. They made the heartbreaking decision to bring him back as he was becoming dominant over the other dog in the home.

He is a very excitable, happy boy - essentially an overgrown puppy in a big body. Max is full of fun and enthusiasm and will need an active home that can meet his exercise and stimulation needs. He already knows some basic commands such as “sit”, “paw”, “lay down”, and “wait”, and he is eager to interact with his people. He is very food oriented, which makes training enjoyable and rewarding, and he already shows lovely food manners, waiting patiently when asked.
