We would love to see him get the second chance that he deserves in a more suitable home where he can get the attention, focus and training that he thrives on.

name: Aspen
description:
Aspen is a 10 month old Beagle mix who’s life got off to a very rocky start. He was abandoned as a young puppy in Cyprus and found seriously unwell with parvovirus.

After spending time in the vets and making a full recovery, he joined the shelter and eventually travelled to the UK as a foster.

Since arriving, it’s been a bit of a journey for him, and it’s taken time to really understand what he needs to feel settled and secure.

After struggling to find the right fit in a home environment, Aspen has spent the last couple of months in kennels, where he has surprised everyone with just how well he’s done.

With clear routines, patient handling and consistent care, he has really come into himself. He’s responded brilliantly to basic training, built very strong relationships with the staff and grown hugely in confidence.

Aspen is a clever, energetic young dog, but new and unfamiliar situations can still feel overwhelming for him, and are often made more challenging by the fact that he is deaf.

Aspen is great with people and has been incredibly sociable, affectionate and engaged with those caring for him. Everyone who has been involved with him at the kennels has been really charmed by his personality.

He’s a cheeky, busy puppy who loves cuddles, fuss and being the centre of attention. Once he feels safe, his personality really shines and he shows just how much he enjoys human company.

When it comes to other dogs, Aspen is more selective. While he has previously lived alongside other dogs, is kennelled parallel to other dogs, and can pass dogs calmly on walks, we think that overall he would be happiest as the only dog in the home.

We suspect that earlier experiences may have left him feeling automatically defensive, and giving him a space where he doesn’t have to share attention or resources will help him relax and feel secure.

We are looking for an adult only home without other pets. As Aspen is deaf, it’s important that his environment is predictable and calm, and we don’t feel it would be fair to place him in a home with children or cats.

He would thrive in a quieter, more rural setting with access to a garden and plenty of opportunity to burn off energy in a safe way.

Aspen is clever and has learned hand signals for certain commands, so he would love to continue with further training as he really likes to learn and engage.

Aspen needs a confident, committed adopter who understands that he’s a young dog who has had a lot of upheaval in a short space of time.

With the right people, he lights up and shows what a sweet, silly and loving boy he really is.

Experience with training or deaf dogs would be helpful but isn’t essential, as long as his new family are patient, open to learning and ready to give him the structure and stability he’s been missing.

name: Lykos
description:
Lykos is a 7-8 year old mixed breed who sadly came into the shelter after his elderly owner passed away and none of his family members were able to take his dog in.

It’s always particularly sad to see dogs that are accustomed to living in a home try and adjust to the shelter, as it’s so overwhelming, confusing and scary for them.

Despite his slightly older age, Lykos has slowly but surely adapted well, and now he’s ready to find a forever home for his later years.

Lykos is around eight years old and is a sweet, calm boy with a gentle nature. Right now he’s sharing his kennel with a female dog who he enjoys hanging out with.

He was previously in a kennel on his own, so we think he’s quite liked having a friend come to join him.

Although he’s a little older than most of our other shelter residents, Lykos still really likes going out for walks and is well mannered on the lead.

He loves sniffing through the long grass, peeing on anything that’s already been peed on, and he also likes to just stand and observe, taking in his surroundings.

He enjoys basking in the sun on a warm day, and he’s also quite a nosey neighbour so he loves watching what everyone else is up to.

Lykos finds the close capacity of the kennels quite confronting, and he really doesn’t like being barked at by other dogs in surrounding kennels.

He would definitely prefer a calm household with a laidback lifestyle, and we don’t think he would be too fussed either way if he was the only dog, or if he shared with a resident dog.

He can be quite independent, but if he was homed with another dog we think he’d be happiest with a female simply based on his experience in the shelter.

Being an older dog, Lykos is looking for a patient family. Just because he’s been in a home before, it doesn’t mean he will adapt seamlessly, and he still requires understanding and time to settle in properly.

A UK companion home is still very different to a Cyprus companion home, and there will be a lot of change for him to navigate.

The perfect home for Lykos would be based in a calm, residential area, or semi-rurally, with direct access to a secure garden. He would suit an older family given his calmer nature, or a younger family just wanting to give a senior dog a happy life.

He’d enjoy leisurely walks in the woods or along the beach, but he won’t be too demanding in his exercise needs. As long as he has a comfortable bed and lots of attention, we think he’ll be very happy.

name: Eraty
description:
Hi, I’m Eraty, a small size 3 year old kokoni cross girl.

I don’t remember much kindness from my early life.

I was one of 27 dogs rescued from a hoarding situation last October. We had never really been socialised, and human contact wasn’t something we looked forward to.

Like the others, I was especially worried about anyone touching around my neck. It brought back memories I’d rather forget.

When I arrived in rescue, I was scared of almost everything. People were confusing, the world felt big, and trusting anyone seemed impossible.

The dogs there became my best friends straight away. They showed me how to play, how to relax, and most importantly, how to feel safe. My foster mum and dad had to work a little harder.

For weeks, I watched them from a distance, trying to understand why they kept being so gentle with me.

They never expected more than I could give.

One day I realised I wanted to be a little closer. Then a little closer again. Before I knew it, I was sitting beside them on the sofa, enjoying head scratches and asking for cuddles all by myself.

These days, I’m a happy girl who loves countryside walks, playing with my doggy friends, and relaxing at home. I also live with cats and get along with them very well.

I’m still a sensitive soul and I’ll need a family who understands that trust is something I’ve had to learn. But every step of my journey has shown that underneath the fear was a loving dog just waiting for someone to believe in her.

A friendly, playful resident dog is a must to build my confidence.

If you’re patient, kind, and willing to let me go at my own pace, I think we could be something very special together.

name: Betsy
description:
*** BETSY WILL COME TO FOSTER IN BANSTEAD, SURREY, ON 13TH SEPTEMBER 2026 ***

Our extra special girl Betsy is approximately five years old and a fabulous cross breed just off the bottom end of medium. Betsy weighs sixteen kilos and measures fourteen inches from shoulder to paw.

Having been found abandoned on the streets, Betsy has been in our Cyprus shelter for three months.

Our shelter manager describes Betsy as calm and loving. She so enjoys her human interactions and cuddles.

With her gentle and caring persona Betsy brings out the best in people who respond so positively to this delightful little dog.

Her manners are impeccable, and Betsy knows how to turn on the charm and win the hearts of those around her.

Betsy is incredibly pretty with her solid black coat, white flash to her chest and four white tips. And of course, the most beautiful eyes.

Betsy walks really nicely on her lead and enjoys her time in the car. She appears to mix perfectly well with other dogs, cats and children.

name: Cassie
description:
Cassie is a one year old Shepherd mix and was found running through farmland when a local rescuer was able to coax her into her car and take her to safety.

Surprisingly, during her check up at the vets they discovered that she actually had a registered microchip, so they were able to track down her owners and asked them to come and collect her.

But of course, had the story ended positively, then Cassie wouldn’t be on our website. Sadly, her family said they didn’t want her anymore as she was too energetic, and that the rescuer could rehome her.

There was no sense of remorse or accountability, they just gave her away and moved on with their lives.

Cassie is a shepherd mix and she is indeed an energetic, active and highly intelligent young dog. She’s estimated to be just under a year old, and she is always raring to get outside and go on an adventure.

Right now, she’s in private kennels where she’s safe and sound until she finds her forever home, but she needs more exercise and enrichment than they can offer her, and as a result whenever she goes out for a run in the yard she is like a coiled spring.

Cassie is looking for a family who have had high energy dogs before and can give her the physical and mental outlets that she needs to thrive. She is so, so loyal and bonds with people very quickly.

She’s extremely keen to please and eager to learn, and as she’s so food motivated this makes the perfect reward!

Cassie would be best suited to a home outside of the city with an active family who enjoy running, exploring and just generally being outdoors.

When her needs are met, like any dog, Cassie is a bundle of love and loves hanging out with people. She’s also great with other dogs, especially those of a similar energy level to her, and she loves racing around in the grass with them.

She would also be best suited to a home with a reasonable size garden, as she really likes being outdoors.

Cassie may be young and bouncy, but she also has lots of love to give. She is the perfect dog for someone seeking a best friend, adventure partner and a companion that they can train.

name: Cece
description:
Cece is an 18 month old Shepherd mix who arrived at the shelter as an unclaimed stray, a story we’ve heard many a time. No one came looking for her, and she had no microchip or collar, so who knows where she came from.

She was however in good health, and happy to meet new people, so we’re hopeful that she’s not had a difficult start in life.

Cece is around a year and a half old and she is a beautiful girl with a very happy, friendly and sociable personality. She loves being around people and is very eager to please, quietly confident, and reads other dogs well.

It took a little while for her to initially calm down when she first arrived in the shelter as she had some nervous energy to burn off, but once we’d found her the ideal kennel mates and she’d had some time to decompress, her true nature started to shine through.

As mentioned, Cece now shares her kennel with multiple other dogs of various sizes, ages and energy levels. She’s loving having the opportunity to make new friends and enjoys playing with them and just being in their company.

While we don’t feel she’s dependant on other dogs, she definitely likes hanging out with them, and she’s more than happy to share her bed, which is so cute.

18 months old is still young, so Cece would benefit from some basic training to help refine her manners a little. She learnt to sit in no time, despite all the distractions around her in the shelter.

As she is food motivated, this works really well as a reward. When she’s excited to see you Cece is prone to jumping up a bit, but it doesn’t take long for this to pass and with some routine and a proper home setting, we’re confident that is very trainable.

The ideal home for Cece would be with a family who enjoy time outdoors, have direct access to a secure garden, and who are keen to invest some time into a young dog learning about a whole new world.

We think the outskirts of the city would be preferable for Cece, or of course a more rural setting would suit her just fine too.

She’d enjoy a fairly active lifestyle, and with some time and patience we think she will make a brilliant addition to a family home.

name: Matilda
description:
Matilda is a huge Romanian Shepherd aged around seven years old. Matilda fostered Norwich Norfolk.

We met Matilda on our trip to the Botosani public shelter in Romania back in March. Generally we don’t willingly take Rommie shepherds as they are a specialist breed that require experienced adopters.

Because she was a middle aged dog we just couldn’t leave her there to live out her retirement years.

Matilda is now in the UK and looking for that experienced adopter. She is the most beautiful, calm, quiet of dogs who requires only gentle exercise and a garden to potter in.

However her genetic program to “protect her flock” is still lingering and she will bark protectively when strangers approach her family.

Of course barking is not going to physically hurt anyone, but she is a big dog with a big booming bark and that can of course be intimidating.

She needs a home where visitors are not a common occurrence and she needs to be put in her own safe space when they do come to prevent any situations where people feel threatened.

Matilda is the most loving and sweet girl with her family. Despite her size her movement is slow and seldom so it’s kind of like having a large rug that gets up and moves itself to another room sometimes.

She is fine with cats and dogs and is very steady around goats and horses….. again her descendency from protecting sheep in the mountains serves her well. Shes just an all round great dog who takes her family duties quite seriously. She will need an adult home environment.

name: Lucy
description:
Lucy is a 3.5 month old chihuahua cross girl who is expected to be smaller medium size when fully grown. Lucy’s had a difficult start in life, she was found with her mum and sister in a deprived area where her mum had been badly treated.

Her past could have made her fearful, but luckily it didn’t, and Lucy is full of trust and love.

This beautiful girl is a happy, friendly puppy who loves cuddles and being close to people, she is a true lapdog. She is a true puppy, full of life, loves to play and explore. She is jolly happy girl with a fun character.

Lucy is a sweet affectionate puppy, she loves dogs and could happily live with a doggie mate or be the only dog in the home.

She is also happy around children and could live with older dog savvy kids.

We can also cat test her for the right applicant.

Lucy dreams of cuddles in bed, fun adventures, belly rubs and a family to love for the rest of her life.

Lucy is in Hungary and will travel to the UK once a home has been secured for her.

name: Czarek
description:
Please meet Charming Czarek! Czarek is a beautiful German Shepherd that is 3 years old. He joined us when his owners were evicted and had nowhere for him to go, sadly he was very underweight and had an untreated skin issue, he was a very confused little lad.

He is a slight German shepherd, he now weighs 30kg so is on the smaller size, but what he lacks in size he more than makes up for in personality.

Since joining the pack this guy has absolutely blossomed, mentally he is still young and has alot to learn but would make a fantastic dog for someone that knows the breed and has experience with them.

On arrival he had no social skills and just barked in their faces, but now he has learnt how to play and is a good little lad.

He hasn’t had the training or socialisation he needs so is basically a blank canvas for his adopters to make into the perfect little lad we know is in there!

Since joining us Czarek has already come on in leaps and bounds and has much better manners around other dogs, he is an absolute gift for someone that wants to train a dog to do a job, as he is so eager to learn. He isnt the smartest shepherd I have met, but he is willing!

Czarek is crate trained and walks well on a lead, he has fab recall and loves going for a run with the other dogs. Sometimes he likes a ball, sometimes food wins! He travels well in the car and loves a routine!

Czarek would do well with an older similar breed role model, but equally could be the only dog if someone is around a lot. he can live with children age 8 years plus that are used to large breed dogs, he lived with children in his previous home and is very gentle.

Czarek will make a fantastic dog for someone prepared to put in the time and training.

Czarek is non destructive, house trained and happy to be left for short periods, like most shepherd, he is fond of a bark although he is much better now he has a routine and only barks to tell us he needs something.

Czarek mixes with dogs of all sizes but he can be a bit boisterous with the small dogs, he doesnt realise his size and is basically a big dopey puppy at heart!

Czarek is a gorgeous dog that so desperately needs a forever home and family to grow up with.

Czarek is neutered, he is microchipped de flead, fully vaccinated and wormed.

name: Lacey
description:
Lacey is a 1-year-old female Labrador Retriever. She is looking for an active family home where she can continue to enjoy the happy and energetic lifestyle that suits her best. Lacey has a wonderful character and loves everyone she meets.

She enjoys being out on walks and is always happy to explore with her family. One of her favourite things is spending time around water, and she especially loves lakes. She is also house trained, making her ready to settle into a home.

Lacey was rescued from the meat trade in China alongside her mum and siblings. Her mum and siblings have already found their homes in the UK, and now it is Lacey’s turn to find a loving family of her own.

She has come a long way and is ready to begin the next chapter of her life.

Lacey can live with all dogs, making her a great companion for a home with canine friends. She has not been cat tested, so it is not known whether she could live with cats. She can also live with children aged 10 years and over.

Lacey is currently living in foster care in Buckinghamshire while she waits for her forever home. She is fully vetted and ready to join the right family.

With her affectionate nature, love of people and enjoyment of outdoor adventures, Lacey is hoping to find a home where she can continue to thrive.

name: Batu
description:
Batu is a 3.5-year-old male Cross-Breed. Weighing 30kg and classed as a large dog, Batu currently lives in a shelter in Cyprus after being heartbreakingly surrendered by his previous owner.

Once living in an apartment, he’s now learning how to trust again in the noisy, unpredictable environment of a municipal shelter.

Though understandably wary at first, Batu has a heart of gold and a gentle nature that shines through once he starts to feel safe. He’s calm during vet visits, enjoys car journeys, and shows polite behaviour even when surrounded by barking, stressed-out dogs.

His composed reaction to chaotic situations is impressive and says a lot about his potential in a stable, loving home.

Batu can live with other dogs, particularly those of a similar size, or thrive as an only pet. In the shelter, he’s always curious and keen to say hello to the others, which shows his social side.

He’s already house trained from his previous home, and possibly has some basic training under his belt. Still, like all rescue dogs, he’ll benefit from ongoing support, routine, and positive reinforcement.

He would be happiest in a peaceful home that offers regular walks, space to rest, and most importantly, consistent affection. Batu deserves a second chance—not just at life, but at experiencing the kind of unconditional love and loyalty he’s more than ready to return.

name: Ntina
description:
Ntina is a 3.5 year old female Kangal Shepherd Dog. She would prefer to be the only dog in the home and can live with children over the age of 12. She is currently located in Cyprus.

Kangal Shepherd Dogs often find themselves in shelters, abandoned due to their size. We made a promise to help find them all loving homes. Here are two Kangal Shepherd Dogs who ended up in the same shelter.

Next up is Ntina. She adores people, cuddles, and attention. She is slightly bigger than Keith, weighing approximately 50 kilograms. Ntina is easy to get along with and is much loved by the shelter staff and volunteers.

She was found as a stray, and it is hard to understand how anyone could abandon a dog and leave her to fend for herself, especially a dog of her size and with her thick coat.

Temperatures of 40 degrees on the streets of Cyprus in summer could not have been easy for her.

Ntina is friendly and placid with people and children, but due to her size she will need an experienced handler, and any children in the home must be over 12 years old. She needs a home where she can enjoy plenty of cuddles as well as long walks.

Ntina needs to be the only dog, as she can be selective around other dogs. Given her temperament, she would likely be happiest as the only dog in the home.

name: Lady
description:
*** LADY WILL COME TO FOSTER IN BANSTEAD, SURREY, ON 13TH SEPTEMBER 2026 ***

Our extra special girl Lady is approximately nine years old and a cross breed at the bottom end of medium albeit with a Labrador appearance.

Lady weighs just twelve kilos and measures ten inches from shoulder to paw. Very sadly Lady’s owner passed very recently, and Lady has been in our Cyprus shelter for two weeks.

Although Lady is a senior citizen, she is sprightly , alert and attentive. Such a sweet lady who finds herself in a shelter through no fault of her own, but she is coping incredibly well.

Lady has a near perpetual smile and staggering attractive appearance. Her white coat runs into cream with soft tan patches which reflect her adorable and loving personality.

Such a pretty girl who will make the most delightful readymade companion. Lady’s adopters will be blessed taking this dear old lady into her dotage.

Lady walks so nicely on her lead and enjoys her time in the car. She also seems to mix perfectly well with other dogs, cats and children.

name: Kane
description:
Welcome the handsome and friendly gentleman, Kane. Kane is a 6 year old male Black Labrador Retriever with a medium build, weighing around 20 kilograms.

Kane has been in the shelter for over a year now. His best friend, Kahu, was finally adopted last month and now Kane is left behind. They had been together for the past year so now, more than ever, Kane needs to find his happy ever after.

Kane is a good, well-rounded boy and can already respond to basic commands. He is good with dogs of all shapes and sizes and relishes the opportunity for attention and a way to burn off energy.

He is super cuddly and will be a wonderful sofa dog, but will also require decent walks and new adventures.

Kane will need ongoing basic training and some patience to help him settle into home life. He is your typical Labrador Retriever – super friendly with everyone he meets, excitable, and food-oriented.

This lovely boy will suit most home settings – all he asks for is affection, nice walks, a roof over his head, and regular meals.

We have no doubt that he will be the most wonderful companion.

Kane is located in Cyprus and, if adopted in the UK, will travel with a passport and be tested for infectious diseases. He is fully vaccinated and castrated.

name: Hettie
description:
*** HETTIE WILL COME TO FOSTER IN BANSTEAD, SURREY, ON 13TH SEPTEMBER 2026 ***

Our extra special girl Hettie is approximately seven years old and a medium sized hound cross with absolutely stunning ears.

Hettie weighs twenty kilos and measures fourteen inches from shoulder to paw. Very sadly Hettie was surrendered to our Cyprus shelter by her extremely distressed elderly owner who was unable to keep her.

Hettie is the perfect readymade family dog with perfect manners. Her calm and pleasant demeanour make Hettie an exceptionally low maintenance girl.

Having spent her whole life in a loving family home, Hettie is finding shelter life overwhelming and she longs for her new forever home soonest.

Hettie’s greatest joy is human company and so enjoys a fuss, hug and cuddle.

With such a wonderful appearance, a very pretty face and near perpetual smile Hettie will be a huge prize for her most fortunate adopters. And just look at her eyes.

Hettie walks nicely on her lead and enjoys her time in the car. She seems to mix perfectly well with other dogs, cats and children.

name: Scamp
description:
Scamp is a 3 year old male West Highland White Terrier currently in foster care in Buckinghamshire with other dogs.

Scamp was rescued from the meat trade in China last year.

He is a happy, confident boy who loves walks and enjoys spending time with people.

Scamp can live with other dogs and cats. He is also suitable to live with adults and children aged 10 years and over.

name: Harper
description:
Harper is a 3 year old female Chocolate Labrador Retriever currently living in Spain and looking for her forever home due to her current owner’s ill health.

She currently lives with three cats but no children, so a home with older children would be best for her.

Harper is very boisterous and would be better suited to live with a non-dominant male dog or as the only dog in the home.

Harper was born in Spain and can travel wherever her wonderful forever home may be.

She is spayed, fully vaccinated, microchipped, has an EU passport, and has a clean bill of health.

Harper is submissive and cautious around other dogs and needs an experienced home where introductions to other dogs are carefully managed.

name: Toffee
description:
*** TOFFEE WILL COME TO FOSTER IN BANSTEAD, SURREY, ON 13TH SEPTEMBER ***

Our extra special boy Toffee is approximately two-and-a-half-year-old cross breed with a significant lurcher appearance. Toffee weighs a perfect twenty-one kilos and measures seventeen inches from shoulder to paw.

Having been found abandoned on the streets, Toffee has been in our Cyprus shelter for one month.

For the trained eye, Toffee is canine perfection. He is stunningly good looking with his light tan coat with hints of darker brown. The most lovable face and soft demeanour make Toffee a world champion.

Toffee’s marvellous face is enhanced by gorgeous whiskers, puppy dog eyes and beautiful ears.

The shelter manager reports that Toffee is bubbly fun and has perfect manners. Toffee will bring years of joy and happiness to a kind and caring adoptive family.

Toffee walks nicely on his lead and enjoys his time in the car. He seems to mix perfectly well with other dogs, cats and children. Just sublime.

name: Rocky
description:
Rocky is a 2 year old male Akita and is looking for his new forever home.

What can we say about Rocky? Isn’t he just gorgeous! He is also a bit of a doofus. Rocky is only a youngster at 2 years of age and hasn’t had much training in his short life. This has meant that he’s ended up like a giant puppy.

Rocky is very strong on the lead, especially around other dogs, bobbing and weaving left and right. Once he has calmed down, he does walk better, but a new owner will need to be prepared to spend time teaching Rocky how to walk nicely so that everyone has an enjoyable walk.

Rocky is only just learning his basic commands like sit. He also gets super excited when he sees his lead and knows he’s going out for a walk.

He likes to jump up to say hello and express his gratitude for getting attention. This is something that will be worked on in our care and needs continued training in a new home.

This lad really does not know his size and will happily walk into you without even flinching; he also came to us as a stray with no history, so we are looking for a home without any children or with children over the age of 12 years who are used to being around large breeds.

We are ideally looking for a home where he can be the only dog in his home. We feel at this stage he will need all of his adopters’ attention whilst he learns his basics.

We cannot place Rocky in a home where there are cats. With the correct training, Rocky is going to make some lucky adopter a fantastic companion; he is affectionate and really enjoys the company of people and making new friends.

name: Captain
description:
Captain is a 4.5 year old, medium-sized, male, lab cross who was abandoned in Cyprus by his owner 9 months ago. We will never know his full story—maybe his family moved away, or maybe they just decided they no longer wanted him.

Whatever the reason, Captain was left behind: alone, confused, and heartbroken. The fact that this boy is so loving and affectionate towards humans is a testament to him.

Perhaps it’s because he knows exactly what he has lost and he longs to find that again. He adores being around humans and is just such a happy boy. He doesn’t deserve to be in a kennel.

Captain has shown to be selective with other dogs so needs to be the only dog. He can be fostered, fostered with a view to adopt, or simply adopted.

Everyone involved in Captain’s plight is desperate to see him happy in a home. He is one of those dogs that it is quite heartbreaking to see him stuck in a kennel for close to 24 hours a day.

Captain will of course need patience and time to settle into a home, as with any rescue dog. We think that he may be house trained, given his past, and he also walks well on the leash.

Overall, we have no doubt this boy will be the best companion and anyone who is lucky enough to have him in their lives will just adore him.

Knowing what he has lost, Captain will be one of those dogs that is eternally grateful and will give back any love you give 10-fold.

Captain is located in Cyprus and, if adopted in the UK, will travel with a passport and be tested for infectious diseases. He is fully vaccinated and castrated.

name: Sully
description:
Sully is a 5 year old medium size male cross breed. He looks like someone accidentally shrunk a Rottweiler in the wash. He’s handsome, compact, and has mastered the art of looking at you as if he’s contemplating the meaning of life.

Sully’s story didn’t begin so happily, he was surrendered in Hungary by a British family along with several other dogs.

They arrived frightened of people, fearful of new experiences, and clearly hadn’t been shown the love, kindness and guidance that every dog deserves.

For Sully the world was a scary place initially. But then he saw people who were kind to him, spoke gently, and gave him time. And it didn’t take long for him to realise that not all humans let you down.

Today Sully is a completely different dog, he enjoys life and likes human company, and although he still has his moments of insecurity, he is now curious and not fearful of the world around him.

He is a loyal, kind natured boy, quite placid in temperament. He isn’t a high energy dog either who would need endless adventures and entertainment all through the day, but he will happily join you on your walks and then enjoy home time sharing the sofa with you.

What he really wants though is a family who won’t give up on him.

Sully is friendly with most dogs with sensible introductions, very excitable dogs are probably not the best match for him but he could happily live with a friendly placid dog.

He is friendly around children, too, but he would probably be best suited to an adult-only home as he is not used to the noise and chaos that can come with young children.

We can cat test him for the right applicant.

He would really love to leave his shelter days behind and start a new life.

And he comes with a special bonus in that he understands English!

Sully is in Hungary and will travel to the UK once the right family is found for him.

He comes neutered, microchipped, fully vaccinated, with an EU pet passport.

name: Lana
description:
Lana is a 3 month old chihuahua cross girl from Hungary who is expected to be smaller medium size when fully grown.

Lana has already had a difficult start in life, she was found with her mum and sister in a deprived area where her mum had been badly treated. Despite everything they have been through, Lana has chosen love over fear.

This beautiful girl is a happy, friendly puppy who loves cuddles and being close to people, she is a true lapdog. She has a slightly calmer nature than her sister, but still enjoys playing, exploring and having fun.

She is a sweet affectionate puppy who could happily live with a doggie mate or be the only dog in the home.

She is also happy around children and can live with older dog savvy kids.

We can also cat test her for the right applicant.

Lana has never known what it’s like to belong to a family of her own, but she’s ready to learn. She dreams of cuddles in bed, fun adventures, belly rubs and people to love for the rest of her life.

Lana is in Hungary and will travel to the UK once a home has been secured for her.

name: Pille
description:
Pille is a 2 year old female Cross-Breed, a tiny and gentle girl with so much love in her heart.

Life hasn’t always been kind to Pille. She was found abandoned with two other little dogs and, because of her past, she can still be a little timid initially.

She is looking for a patient and understanding family and a calm, peaceful home where she can feel completely safe and loved.

Pille is a very quiet, sweet-natured little dog who adores affection. In fact, she’s the kind of little angel who will happily fall asleep in your arms.

She gets along well with other dogs, but she does not pay too much attention to them and would also be perfectly happy as an only dog, soaking up all the love and attention for herself.

Being so small, she would barely take up any room at all and she’d fit perfectly in someone’s bed.

We hope Pille won’t have to wake up in the shelter for much longer.

Pille is in Hungary, ready to travel once a home has been secured for her.

She comes fully vaccinated, microchipped, spayed, and with an EU pet passport.

name: Loki
description:
Loki, a beautiful 2 year old Siberian Husky needs a new home through no fault of his own.

The owner has serious health problems and is unable to give him the care and attention he needs and deserves. He is spending more and more time in kennels which is far from ideal.

Loki is a lovely boy, he loves his toys and he has a kind nature. He is currently the only dog in the household, but he is friendly with dogs and wants to play on walks so he has the potential to live with another friendly dog.

He is also used to living with older children.

He is fully vaccinated and microchipped and up to date with flea and wormingtreatments.

Loki is in Chorley, Lancashire where he is visitable upon arrangement.

name: Ariel
description:
Ariel is a 6-year-old small-medium sized girl with a big personality and an even bigger heart. This feisty little terrier has been waiting in the shelter for over 3 years… far longer than any dog should have to wait.

Ariel is full of character – a true terrier spirit. She’s lively, expressive and not afraid to let you know when she’s excited – it’s all part of her charm!

She is affectionate with people and loves children, happily soaking up attention and enjoying being part of the action. Ariel forms lovely bonds and will be a loyal and loving companion to her family.

With other dogs, she is friendly with most and enjoys their company, though like many terriers, she appreciates respectful introductions and a good match in energy.

After spending such a long time in the shelter, Ariel truly deserves a home where she can finally relax, feel safe, and be loved every day.

She would love a family who will embrace her fun personality, include her in their lives, and give her the attention she’s been missing for so long.

Ariel is in Hungary, ready to travel to the UK once a home has been secured for her.

name: Mandy
description:
Mandy is a small crossbreed aged around six years old. Mandy fostered Dereham Norfolk.

Mandy initially came into our care a few months ago from the Botosani public shelter in Romania. She was quite shy and scared of new things on arrival as it is hard for small dogs in there.

A few weeks on and she’s still evolving but she’s ready to find her forever home to continue her story.

Mandy is now progressing well in a foster home with three other dogs. She needs the connection if at least one other dog in her forever home and will play with them once settled. A calmish and confident dog that likes to play at times would be her ideal.

Mandy likes her walks but can get spooked by unexpected events so must have an adopter with patience and experience. She can live with respectful calm teenagers or older.

Mandy’s favourite thing is her food and she loves to get cosy for nap time. She likes to pick things up and take to her bed or crate. She is able to be left comfortably for reasonable periods of time with canine company.

Mandy is the sweetest little dog with an angelic face who simply needs the opportunity to continue to shine.

name: Peter
description:
Peter, 7 months old, pointer cross, medium-large boy.

Our boy Peter has been in the shelter since he was just 4 months old. He is super loving and friendly and adores people and other dogs.

He shares a yard with both male and female dogs of various sizes without issues. Equally, with children, he is keen to approach them, play, and lap up some attention – just like most puppies.

Peter is just a pup so will of course need some ongoing training, but he is already used to walking on the leash. He takes treats gently and is eager to please so we think he will be a joy to train.

Although he is a puppy, he has quite a calming energy. He will still require an active home dedicated to showing him the way in life.

Peter would be best suited to live with another doggy friend.

Overall, Peter is a joy to be around and will make the most wonderful companion for anyone lucky enough to adopt him.

Peter can be adopted in Cyprus or in the UK. He is ready to fly. He is vaccinated and has had a full health check and blood tests for infectious diseases.

name: Smokey
description:
I’m a nearly 3-year-old Pointer cross with a handsome black and white coat, a permanent waggy tail, and a personality that can only be described as one thing: lovable goofball.

I’m a confident, happy boy who approaches life with enthusiasm and a sense of fun. Whether I’m out exploring, playing games, learning something new, or simply making my people laugh with my silly antics, I love being involved in everything that’s going on.

I’m incredibly affectionate and thrive on human company. I love spending time with my people and am never happier than when I’m included in family life.

Behind my playful, clownish side is a loyal dog who forms strong bonds and loves nothing more than being close to those he cares about.

As an active and intelligent boy, I’m looking for a family who can provide plenty of exercise, adventure, and mental stimulation.

I enjoy learning and would love adopters who are keen to continue my training and help me become the very best version of myself.

Because of my size and enthusiasm, I’m looking for a home with children aged 12 and over. I’ll also need a secure garden with 6-foot fencing where I can safely run, play, and enjoy life to the fullest.

If you’re looking for a confident, affectionate companion who will keep you laughing, join you on every adventure, and shower you with love, Smokey might just be the perfect addition to your family.

-Nearly 3 years old– Friendly and confident– Affectionate and people-oriented– Intelligent and eager to learn– Active and adventurous– Full of character and fun– Secure garden with 6ft fencing required

Smokey is the kind of dog who turns ordinary days into adventures and quickly becomes the heart of the home.

name: Dahlia
description:
I’m a beautiful two-year-old Pointer cross with a joyful spirit, and a heart full of love. If you’re looking for a dog who wants to be involved in everything you do, I might just be your perfect match!

I’m an active, intelligent girl who loves being around people. Whether it’s heading out for an adventure, exploring new places, joining you on a walk, or simply spending time with my family, I’m happiest when I’m included.

I thrive on companionship and enjoy building strong bonds with my humans.

I’m also a very clever girl who learns quickly and genuinely enjoys training. I love having a job to do, learning new skills, and using my brain as well as my energy.

With the right guidance and continued training, I’ll make an incredibly rewarding companion.

Because I’m young, enthusiastic, and full of zest for life, I’m looking for an active family who can provide plenty of exercise, enrichment, and opportunities to be part of everyday life.

In return, you’ll get a loyal best friend who will fill your home with love, fun, and laughter.

Due to my size and enthusiasm, I’m looking for a home with children aged 12 and over. I’ll also need a secure garden with 6-foot fencing where I can safely enjoy some outdoor time and stretch my long legs.

-2 years old– Affectionate and people-oriented– Intelligent and eager to learn– Active and adventurous– Loves companionship and attention– Looking for an active family– Secure garden with 6ft fencing required

If you’re searching for a loving, clever, and fun companion who is ready to share life’s adventures with you, Dahlia is waiting to meet you.

name: Apollo
description:
Meet Apollo – a gentle soul ready for a new life.

Apollo, a 3.5-year-old medium size cross-breed boy, whose life until now has been far from kind, has finally been freed from a life lived on a chain. Yet somehow, despite everything, he has held onto his sweetness, his trust, and his huge heart.

Apollo is incredibly human-centred, affectionate, and loving — the kind of dog who just wants to be close to people and soak up kindness.

He is wonderfully sociable with other dogs, big and small, and plays beautifully and politely with them. He’s also an absolute dream at the vets, calm and cooperative — everyone who meets him falls for his sweet nature.

He even has the potential to live with cats; in his previous life, he used to sleep in his dog house with a cat companion, sharing warmth and comfort.

He travels nicely in the car, walks well on the lead, and is more than ready to start discovering what family life means.

Currently, Apollo lives in boarding kennels, hoping that one day someone will finally choose him.

Apollo has so much love to give — he just needs someone who will finally give him the life he has always deserved. ?

Apollo comes neutered, fully vaccinated, microchipped, and with an EU pet passport.

He is ready to travel from Hungary as soon as his family is found.

name: Jessica
description:
Jessica, pointer cross, 4.5 years old, medium/large sized girl.

Jessica is a beautiful 4.5-year-old girl who came to us as a stray from Cyprus, and she’s been winning hearts ever since.

Despite her uncertain start, she has a gentle, trusting nature and a warm, affectionate personality that shines through in everything she does.

She’s a large dog with a balanced, medium energy level—she enjoys her daily walks and exploring the world around her, but she’s just as happy to settle down and relax with her people.

Jessica has a calm and easygoing temperament, which makes her a lovely companion both indoors and out.

Jessica is wonderful with children, showing patience and kindness, and she gets along well with other dogs, happily socialising and sharing her space.

Her friendly and adaptable nature means she could fit into a variety of home environments, whether as part of a busy family or a quieter household.

After her journey, Jessica is ready for a fresh start with a loving family who can offer her stability, care, and plenty of affection. In return, she promises to be a loyal, loving companion who will bring warmth and joy to her forever home.

Jessica is located in Cyprus and, if adopted in the UK, will travel with a passport and be tested for infectious diseases. She is fully vaccinated and spayed.

name: Valentina
description:
Valentina is a 1 year old female Podenco cross Alsation.

Currently in a foster home in Suffolk, UK.

Some dogs leave a mark on your heart from the moment you hear their story.

On a cold winter day, Valentina was found lying beside her brother’s lifeless body. She had stayed with him, waiting for help to arrive, but sadly help came too late for her brother. Valentina, however, was given a second chance—and what an incredible girl she has become.

Now around one year old, Valentina is living in a foster home in Ipswich, where she has shown just how loving and devoted she can be.

She formed a beautiful bond with her foster mum remarkably quickly and has proven that beneath her cautious exterior is a dog with so much love to give.

Valentina is a sensitive soul who is still learning that the world can be a safe place. She thrives when given kindness, patience, and encouragement, and she needs a family who will allow her to build confidence at her own pace.

There is no rush with Valentina—she just needs people willing to meet her where she is and celebrate every small victory along the way.

She can be nervous around men, though she has never shown any aggression.

Her fear simply means she needs understanding and gentle guidance from people willing to earn her trust. For this reason, she may be happiest in a female-only household or with a family that understands fearful dogs and is committed to helping her continue her journey.

Valentina gets along great with other dogs with proper introductions. She also finds being left alone difficult. However, thanks to the dedication of her foster mum, she has already made remarkable progress and continues to improve.

She is a clever girl who enjoys learning and benefits greatly from mental stimulation, enrichment activities, and positive reinforcement training that keeps both her mind and her confidence growing.

What makes Valentina so special is watching her blossom. Every time she chooses to trust, every time she tries something new, every time her tail wags a little more freely, you can see the wonderful dog she is becoming.

Valentina is looking for a patient, loving home that will give her the security, understanding, and guidance she needs to continue shining. In return, you’ll gain the privilege of watching an extraordinary dog discover just how loved she truly is.

– ?Around 1 year old– Fostered in Suffolk– Loving and affectionate once she trusts– ?Intelligent and eager to learn– ?Making excellent progress with confidence building– ?Looking for a patient and understanding family

Are you the special person Valentina has been waiting for?

Because somewhere out there is the family who will look at this gentle girl and see not her fears, but her incredible potential. And when that happens, Valentina’s happiest chapter will finally begin.

name: Nancy
description:
Nancy, pointer, 5.5 years old, medium/large sized girl.

Nancy, our sweet 5.5 year old pointer girl, is ready to find her forever home

Judging by Nancy’s breed and appearance, it is quite likely that she belonged to a hunter, bred for puppies, then dumped once she got too ‘old’.

She is the most gentle girl that gets on with everyone—dogs, people, children. She has a kind soul that doesn’t deserve the life she has led so far. Now it’s our chance to turn that around and make sure the rest of her life is worth living.

Since arriving at the shelter she has been loving and friendly, although, of course, cautious at first. She walks really well on the leash and does not pull.

We don’t think she will need an overly active home but will for sure enjoy her walks. Overall she is a well rounded girl and has shown everyone just how lovely she is and how deserving of a loving home she is.

Unfortunately for Nancy, she has leishmania. We are really hoping this doesn’t mean she is overlooked. Please don’t let this disease define her.

It is easily managed with cheap tablets, doesn’t affect how long she will live, doesn’t affect insurance premiums, and cannot be transmitted to other pets or humans.

Many dogs live long and happy lives with leishmania. Nancy is otherwise a healthy girl. We just hope someone sees how wonderful she is regardless of leishmania.

Nancy is located in Cyprus and, if adopted in the UK, will travel with a passport. She will arrive with 6 months supply of leishmania treatment. She is fully vaccinated and spayed.

name: Kabo
description:
Kabo was brought into the shelter as a stray and has been your typical pointer since the day he arrived.

Super people oriented, very loving and cuddly, likely to be a velcro dog in typical pointer fashion!

Overall, Kabo has a lovely friendly nature and will be a wonderful companion for anyone lucky enough to adopt him.

He is a gentle boy so could live with children. He also walks well on a leash. He shares his space with both male and female dogs with no issues.

Kabo is not good with cats due to his prey drive, so a home with no small furries is a must.

We expect he will have medium-high energy levels once in a home and will need decent physical and mental stimulation.

Kabo is located in Cyprus and, if adopted in the UK, will travel with a passport and has been tested for infectious diseases.

name: Junior
description:
Junior is a 3-year-old male Dachshund Cross. He was rescued from a deprived area of Hungary in very poor condition and has since been given a second chance at life. Despite everything he has been through, he has remained a sweet and affectionate little dog.

He has been waiting patiently while other dogs around him have found homes, yet he has somehow been overlooked. This is especially sad because he is described as a lovable and funny boy who truly enjoys human company.

More than anything, he is simply longing to be part of a family of his own.

Junior walks nicely on the lead and enjoys getting out and about. He loves people and shows a gentle and friendly nature in his interactions. His kind temperament also means he is patient when around children.

When it comes to other dogs, Junior’s preference is for female dogs. He would be happiest living with a spayed female companion or as the only dog in the home where he can soak up all the attention.

This flexibility means he could suit different home setups, as long as his social preferences are respected.

He comes neutered, fully vaccinated, and microchipped, which gives his future family reassurance about his health care. He also has an EU pet passport, making his travel arrangements clear and straightforward.

A home check and adoption fee will apply, and full rescue back-up is provided for added support.

Junior is a small dog at approximately 10 kg, making him a manageable size for many households. His journey has not been easy, but his gentle nature and cheerful personality have stayed intact.

He is ready to leave his difficult past behind and finally enjoy the comfort and security of a loving home.

attributes.Breed: Staffordshire bull terrier cross labrador retriever

name: Zelda
description:
Meet Zelda, a 2 year old female Staffordshire Bull Terrier cross Labrador Retriever — a brave and beautiful girl who has survived unimaginable cruelty yet still greets the world with love.

She came from hell. Zelda spent her early life chained so short she couldn’t even stand, the chain cutting deep into her neck. Despite such cruelty, she never lost her spark. Once freed, she greeted her rescuers with a wagging tail and a forgiving heart.

Now safe, she’s blossomed into an energetic, happy-natured girl. She’s medium-sized, affectionate, and adores people.

Zelda gets along well with other dogs — though she can be a bit boisterous in her play — and would thrive either as an only dog or with a friendly, tolerant companion.

In the kennel, she doesn’t get the exercise or freedom she craves, and she’s longing for a home where she can run, play, and finally just be a dog. She loves her walks, though she does pull on the lead, simply excited to be out exploring the world she missed for so long.

Zelda would suit an active home that can give her the exercise, structure, and affection she craves, so her joyful spirit can shine at long last. Older children will be considered.

Zelda has endured pain no animal should ever know. She deserves a life full of love, freedom, and fun.

Fully vaccinated, spayed, microchipped, with an EU pet passport. Ready to travel.

name: Suzy
description:
Suzy is a 1.5 year old female Cross-Breed, medium in size. She was rescued exactly a year ago from horrid circumstances, having been thrown into a yard with her brother, Tommy, at just a few weeks of age.

They lived on piles of rubbish, frequently escaping due to inadequate fencing and roaming the streets among busy traffic, constantly in danger. When rescued, they were emaciated and full of parasites.

Suzy has grown to be a kind-natured, healthy girl who is active and playful according to her age. She is medium-sized and has a lovely cheeky character. She loves people and is very friendly around kids, too.

She is also playful and happy around other dogs.

Suzy is perhaps slightly more reserved than her brother and enjoys doggie company a lot. She loves to play and run with her mates.

Suzy would be happiest in a family where she is not left alone for extended periods and there is time for her training. We want her to be treated as a treasured member of the family and be taken on nice adventures. She will need time to settle into her new home and get used to her environment.

Suzy could happily live with another friendly and playful dog or dogs but may also be happy being the only dog and the centre of attention in the family.

As with all rescue dogs, she will need time, patience, love, and commitment from her adopting family to become a happy, well-balanced dog.

Experience with rescue dogs is preferred.

Suzy is currently in Hungary and can travel to the UK as soon as the right family is found for her.

She comes spayed, fully vaccinated, microchipped, dewormed, flea and tick treated, with an EU passport and DEFRA-approved paperwork.

Adoption is subject to a home check and adoption fee. Full RBU provided.

name: Tommy
description:
Tommy is a 1.5 year old male Cross-Breed, medium in size. He was rescued exactly a year ago from horrid circumstances, having been thrown into a yard with his sister, Suzy, at just a few weeks of age.

They lived on piles of rubbish, frequently escaping due to inadequate fencing and roaming the streets among busy traffic, constantly in danger. When rescued, they were emaciated and full of parasites.

Tommy and Suzy can be rehomed together or separately.

Tommy has grown to be a kind-natured, healthy dog who is active and playful according to his age. He is medium-sized and has a lovely happy character. He is very affectionate with people and friendly around kids, too.

Tommy is the more human-centred of the siblings. He loves giving kisses and is a lapdog by nature.

He is also playful and happy around other dogs.

Tommy would be happiest in a family where he is not left alone for extended periods and there is time for his training. We want him to be treated as a treasured member of the family and be taken on nice adventures. He will need time to settle into his new home and get used to his environment.

Tommy could happily live with another friendly and playful dog or dogs but may also be happy as the only dog and the centre of attention in the family.

As with all rescue dogs, he will need time, patience, love, and commitment from his adopting family to become a happy, well-balanced dog.

Experience with rescue dogs is preferred.

Tommy is currently in Hungary and can travel to the UK as soon as the right family is found for him.

He comes neutered, fully vaccinated, microchipped, dewormed, flea and tick treated, with an EU passport and DEFRA-approved paperwork.

Adoption is subject to a home check and adoption fee. Full RBU provided.

name: Gesztenye
description:
Gesztenye is a beautiful 2-year-old medium-sized brindle girl with the sweetest soul. She adores people, loves playing with other dogs and greets everyone she meets with a wagging tail and a hopeful smile.

For over two years, Gesztenye and her sister Zserbo have shared everything. They were rescued together from a kill shelter when they were only a few weeks old. They grew up side by side, slept in the same kennel, and waited together for someone to finally choose them.

Now Zserbo’s dream has come true. She has found a wonderful family in the UK.

But little Gesztenye will soon watch her sister leave… and for the first time in her life, she will be alone.

She has so much love to give and asks for so little in return – just a family to call her own.

Gesztenye is friendly with dogs and would happily live with another canine companion. She is affectionate, playful and kind natured, and can also live with older dog-savvy children.

She’s looking for a home where she will be part of family life and not left alone for long periods.

Like all rescue dogs, Gesztenye will need time, patience and love, but in return she will give you her whole heart.

Gesztenye is currently in Hungary and is ready to travel to the UK as soon as her forever family finds her.

She is spayed, fully vaccinated, treated for parasites, comes with and EU pet passport.

name: Tango
description:
Tango is a 2 year old male Cross-Breed. He was saved from the streets with his 4 siblings and their mum, who is a Dachshund cross.

Tango is a very kind-natured boy—friendly, affectionate, and playful. He has a beautiful, friendly look and a well-proportioned build. He loves people and enjoys a lively, playful lifestyle.

He is very social with his doggie mates and plays well with them.

Tango would be best suited for a family where he is not left alone for extended periods and is treated as a family member. He would love to go on nice adventures! He will need time to settle into his new home, and his training will need to be continued.

He could live happily with another friendly, playful dog or as the only dog in the home, being the centre of attention.

As with all rescue dogs, he will need time, patience, love, and commitment from his adopting family to become a happy, well-balanced dog. Experience with rescue dogs is preferred.

Tango is currently in Hungary and can travel to the UK once the right family is found for him.

He comes fully vaccinated, microchipped, dewormed, flea and tick treated, with an EU passport and DEFRA-approved paperwork.

Adoption is subject to a home check and adoption fee. Full RBU provided.

name: Connie
description:
Connie is a 2 year old female Puli Cross. Her story began in a public pound, where she grew up in harsh conditions. A year ago, she was rescued in a heartbreaking state – skin and bone, frightened and frail.

With care, love, and patience, Connie has blossomed into a happy, affectionate girl. Today she’s full of life – her waggy tail never stops, she adores treats and cuddles, and she has even learned to smile when she’s with the people she loves. This beautiful girl has learned to trust again!

Connie loves the company of other dogs and is especially playful – wrestling and mingling are her favourite games! She would be just as happy snuggling up on the sofa as she is joining in on adventures.

Connie is still waiting in a small shelter in Hungary, dreaming of her forever family. She deserves a home where she will be cherished, spoiled, and loved for the sweet, resilient girl she is.

For Connie, an experienced rescue dog home would be ideal – someone patient and understanding, ready to continue guiding her on her journey.

She could live happily with another friendly and playful dog or as the only dog in the home.

2 years oldPlayful, affectionate, sociableSpayed, vaccinated, microchipped, with EU passportCan travel to the UK once her family is found

If you can give Connie the love-filled home she has been waiting for, please get in touch.

Adoption subject to home check & fee. Full RBU provided.

name: Floki
description:
I’m a 5 year old medium size cross breed boy.

For 3.5 years, I’ve been living in a shelter in Hungary, that’s more than half my life spent waiting behind a fence, hoping someone might stop… might notice me… might choose me. But no one ever has. Sometimes I feel invisible. I’m only 5 years old.

I’m not loud or demanding. I’m patient and quiet. I’m the kind of dog who waits in silence and hopes that one day someone will see the good boy I truly am and will not walk past me.

I love my walks, and I walk beautifully on the lead. I enjoy sniffing the world and feeling the sun on my back. I love hoomans—every scratch behind the ears and every cuddle means the world to me, because I get so little affection here.

But I treasure every moment. I never take kindness for granted.

I’m calm and peaceful around other dogs. I don’t want trouble; I just want love.

And although my muzzle is beginning to grey, please don’t think I’m old. I’m still young, playful in my soft and gentle way, and I dream—every single day—of having a home, a bed of my own, and a family who finally calls me theirs.

I have spent so long waiting, but I haven’t given up.I still believe my person is out there.

If you’re reading this… maybe it’s you.Maybe you’re the one who will make sure I’m invisible no more.

Floki is ready to travel from Hungary once the right family is found.

He comes neutered, fully vaccinated, microchipped, with an EU passport.

name: Folti
description:
Meet Folti – the smart, playful boy ready for his forever home.

Folti is a 4-year-old Border Collie cross, medium-sized and a little smaller than a typical collie — but every bit as bright. This lovely boy is intelligent and learns quickly, always eager to understand what’s being asked of him.

His world changed suddenly when his owner passed away, and Folti found himself in a shelter, confused and heartbroken. This sensitive boy does not cope well with kennel life.

He misses having someone to bond with, someone to follow around, someone to curl up next to.

Folti is full of joy and loves everything that makes life fun: running freely, playing with a ball, splashing and paddling in water, and soaking up affection. One of his sweetest habits? He happily rolls onto his back to show his tummy, just hoping for a belly rub.

He loves his walks and would be an amazing companion for adventures and hikes. He walks nicely on the lead and travels well in the car.

Folti is good with other dogs given proper introductions, and he’s affectionate and people-oriented, he loves children, too.

He just needs a patient, kind owner who will give him the time he needs to settle into a new environment and show his true, loving personality.

He is ready to travel from Hungary and start a new life once the right family is found.

He comes fully vaccinated, microchipped, neutered, with an EU pet passport.

name: Maggie
description:
Maggie is a 1 year old female Cross-Breed, expected to be medium-sized when fully grown.

She is currently in Hungary and is adorable and playful, as expected for her age.

Seven siblings were born in an abandoned property with their mum struggling to feed them when she was emaciated herself.

They were taken in by the local shelter at three weeks of age.

The whole litter was stunningly beautiful, and only two pups are still waiting for their families.

Maggie is fully vaccinated, free from parasites, and ready to travel. She comes with an EU passport.

She can live with dogs and older children. The right owner will need to agree to have her spayed once fully grown.

name: Beigli
description:
Beigli is a 1.5 year-old medium-sized crossbreed boy with a beautiful soul. Born in an abandoned property alongside his six siblings, little Beigli’s life began in heartbreaking circumstances.

His mum was desperately trying to keep her babies alive while she herself was painfully thin and starving.

At just three weeks old, the family was rescued and brought to safety.

The puppies grew up, one by one they found their forever homes, and today only two remain: Beigli and his sister Maggie.

At just 1.5 years old, Beigli has already spent almost his entire life waiting.

He adores people, loves playing with other dogs, and has the kind, gentle nature that makes everyone fall in love with him.

All Beigli dreams of is a family to call his own. A warm bed. Daily walks. Someone to greet with a wagging tail and endless devotion.

He can live with older dog-savvy children and can be cat tested for the right applicant.

Beigli is in Hungary and is ready to travel to the UK as soon as the right family finds him.

He comes neutered, fully vaccinated, treated for parasites, with an EU pet passport.

name: Jenny
description:
Jenny is a 10 year old female Airedale Terrier Cross with a heartbreaking past and a heart full of hope. For seven long years, she was left chained outside without shelter or kindness, while the world looked away.

Her suffering only came to light by chance — her rescuers were returning another dog when they saw her standing there, neglected and desperate.

Jenny’s eyes lit up as if she knew this was her moment, and when her owner was asked to surrender her, he did so without hesitation.

Since that day, Jenny has been safe, but her home has been a kennel. A whole year has passed, and she is still waiting for the love she has never truly known.

Jenny is gentle and people-oriented. She loves her walks and is good on the lead, though she can be a little strong at times. She enjoys chasing a ball and, while she isn’t interested in making doggy friends, she’s content as long as her space is respected.

Jenny would be happiest as the only dog in the home so she doesn’t have to share attention or space.

She is spayed, fully vaccinated, and travels with her EU pet passport — ready to start the new chapter she so deserves.

Jenny has lost so many years, but it’s not too late for her to find happiness! All she wants now is to spend her golden years knowing comfort, kindness, and love.

Could you give her the forever home she has waited a lifetime for?

Fully vaccinated, spayed, microchipped, with an EU pet passport. Ready to travel.

name: Malna
description:
Malna is a 3 year old crossbreed girl, she was dumped on the roadside along with her sister at about 2.5 months of age. She is medium size, weight: 15 kg, shoulder height: 44 cm.

Malna is a lovely girl, she is friendly and kind and loves everyone. She is energetic and playful and sociable around her doggie mates.

She enjoys nothing more than running around in the fields with other dogs, playing a chase. She cooperates nicely with humans too and is very kind with humans.

She enjoys car rides and is good around children too. She also walks nicely on the lead.

Malna would be best suited for a family where she is not left alone for extended periods and she is included in her family’s activities. She would be happiest in an active family, she would happily be your company on long walks, hikes and holidays.

Older dog savvy children will be considered.

Malna would probably be happiest living with another playful dog but she could also live happily as the only dog and the centre of attention in her home.

As all rescue dogs, she will need time, patience, love and commitment from her adopting family to become a happy, well-balanced dog.

Malna is currently in Hungary and can travel to the UK as soon as the right family is found for her.

She comes spayed, fully vaccinated, microchipped, dewormed, flea and tick treated, with an EU passport and DEFRA approved paperwork.

Adoption is subject to Home Check and Adoption fee. Full RBU provided.

name: Nino
description:
This loveable, gentle boy is Nino – a large lad standing at 71cm and aged just 4 and half years old. Nino is a Spanish Mastin and he has a lush, light tan, soft coat fostered in Weymouth.

As you can see by the photos, he’s a good looking boy, such a beautiful dog, inside and out with an outstanding, lovely personality. Nino is friendly with other dogs.

He loves people and is very gentle and good with children. He is very noble, he walks well on lead and he likes to walk very close so you can stroke him all the time.

Nino has mild Leish, which is managed by a daily inexpensive tablet costing £4 for a months supply, and it is non transferable to other dogs. This tablet enables him to have a full natural life expectancy.

Nino is great with male and female dogs, and loves all people, he loves his cuddles. Children we think over 10 years due to his size. Can be homed on his own or to homes with other dogs. Nino has been neutered, blood tested, chipped, vaccinated, and has his passport.

Foster Report:Nino is a very gentle, steady dog with a naturally peaceful energy. He spends most of his day just lounging around and keeping a quiet eye on his people.

Life with Nino:Other Dogs: Completely relaxed. He has shown zero aggression and really seems to enjoy the company of other dogs. He’d do very well in a home with an existing “pack.”

On the Lead: Surprisingly easy to handle for his size. He doesn’t pull and is very responsive to where you’re going.

The Home: He’s fully house-trained and lets you know when he needs to go out. He sleeps through the night without any issues.

The Crate: He’s a bit hesitant to go in at first, but once he’s settled, he’s quiet and calm.

Nino is a versatile boy. While he’d love a quiet setting, he’s adaptable enough for most homes as long as he gets plenty of attention. He’s just a very easy, soulful companion who brings a lot of peace to the house.

name: Sophie
description:
Sophie is a beautiful Portuguese Podengo girl, with a long, rough coat in light tan and white. Poor girl is just 4 years old, and has spent a lot of her life living on the streets and giving birth.

Sophie is a very sweet natured and calm girl, but also a little shy (she takes time to blossom and get to trust you, but once she does she’s a super sweet natured and affectionate girl).

Sophie loves her doggy pals, is good on the lead and a clean girl in her pen. She’s learning that people are kind and now enjoys the love and affection she’s shown but needs someone with time and patience to let her blossom.

Sophie is spayed, up to date with vaccinations and had clear blood tests, and tested negative for Brucella.

We are looking for a family with another friendly dog for Sophie to play with and learn from and older calm children no younger than 11yrs to suit her level of confidence. Sophie is ok with cats.

Sophie mixes well with other dogs, not bothered by cats, very calm and relaxed girl, walks well on her lead, needs someone to give her the time and patience to blossom

name: Cookie
description:
Cookie – 6-year-old gentleman (around 20kg) – Cross-Breed.

Currently being fostered in Gloucestershire in a busy adult home with five other dogs.

Please read Cookie’s full profile before applying.

Cookie is an incredibly handsome boy and we know he is likely to receive a great deal of interest. However, we are looking far beyond appearances when choosing his forever family.

Cookie has had an unsettled life and deserves people who understand that rescue dogs sometimes need a little time, patience and reassurance while they learn that they are finally safe.

We feel he would be happiest with adopters who have previous experience of rescue dogs or experience of helping an anxious dog build confidence.

They will recognise that the little moments of uncertainty we have seen are simply part of his journey, not faults to be fixed.

We were originally told Cookie was a Cockapoo… although we’re not entirely convinced! His looks have sparked plenty of debate, with guesses including Poodle crossed with German Wirehaired Pointer, Schnauzer and Labrador.

The truth is, nobody really knows – so he can be whatever you would like him to be! Whatever his mix may be, one thing is certain – he is an absolutely lovely boy.

Cookie came into our care due to very complex family circumstances. Before that, he had already experienced neglect, and sadly his life has been far from straightforward.

Through no fault of his own he has been passed from one situation to another, leaving him understandably anxious when faced with change he has also been subject to being hurt.

Despite everything he has experienced, Cookie has never shown any aggression towards people. When he has felt frightened, his instinct has simply been to move away.

In his previous home he often ended up being shut away to protect him from the children to feel safe, which has only added to his worries.

What has amazed us most is just how forgiving he is. He met one of our foster home’s three-year-old little boy. Cookie’s response? He happily covered him in kisses. That is Cookie all over. His biggest wish is simply to be loved.

We know he previously lived with another dog, although we suspect that relationship wasn’t always been the easiest for him.

When Cookie first arrived, he was quite wary of the resident dogs and would occasionally give a little growl when he felt unsure.

It was never aggressive; it was simply his way of saying, “I’m worried.”

Fast forward just a week, and that uncertainty has almost completely disappeared. He has formed lovely relationships with the other dogs and is even enjoying playing with the younger, larger dog who initially worried him the most.

Once Cookie realises another dog is kind, he settles beautifully.

One of Cookie’s greatest joys is getting out into the countryside.

He absolutely adores his walks and you don’t even need to call him back he checks in and his recall is excellent.

We know this was something he sadly missed out on before coming into rescue, so watching him run, explore and simply enjoy being a dog has been incredibly rewarding. He walks beautifully on the lead, travels well in the car and is polite when meeting other dogs.

Occasionally, he can still be a little unsure, but his confidence continues to grow every day.

At home, Cookie is a wonderfully easy companion. He is clean indoors, sleeps peacefully through the night and happily settles in his crate with the other dogs in our kitchen.

We introduced the crate when he first arrived because he found everything so overwhelming, but it quickly became his safe place. He has also been left for short periods without any problems.

He has recently enjoyed a much-needed haircut and nail trim, taking both completely in his stride.

As the days have gone by, we have watched the anxious little soul who arrived begin to relax. The more secure Cookie feels, the more his true personality shines through. He is affectionate, gentle, incredibly loyal and simply wants someone he can trust.

If Cookie could tell us what he is looking for, we think he would say this:

“I’d love a peaceful home in the countryside with people who enjoy walking as much as I do. I don’t want to be left for long hours because I really enjoy being with my people.

I’d be happy living with another kind dog, although I may need a little time to realise they’re going to be my friend. I don’t mind meeting visiting grandchildren – I actually like children very much – but because of my past I’d rather not live in a busy family home.

I’d much prefer an adult-only home where life is calm, predictable and full of kindness. I’ve had enough uncertainty now. I just want somewhere to finally belong.”

Cookie has so much love waiting to give the right family. We know saying goodbye to him will be difficult, but we also know there is a wonderful home out there where he can finally put his past behind him and simply enjoy the life every dog deserves.

He is a bit of a Velcro dog and wants to be where you are.

Cookie has been fully health checked by our vet, restarted his vaccinations and has been flea and worm treated.

When applying, please tell us why you feel your home would suit Cookie’s needs, rather than simply why you love his appearance.

Below you’ll also find videos showing Cookie settling into foster care and enjoying his new life.

His first proper play with his foster sister Lucy

Meeting new friends lovely couple who donated blankets to us

With foster daddy who hates being video lol

Trying to video new dogs and Cookie wanted in on the action

name: Marly
description:
This lovely, gentle, large lad is Marley- a 3 year old, male Spanish Mastin. He has a beautiful, brown, textured thick coat, with unusual facial markings.

This handsome boy came to our attention because he is being left in a tiny apartment and a tinier balcony for 23 hours per day!

Marly is a very special and well-loved dog since puppyhood that, due to family circumstances, needs a new home.

He is beautifully trained, used to living inside, and a striking looking dog with a beautiful coat, affectionate with people and social with other dogs.

Due to Marley’s size, we think he is better homed with another female or as an only dog. He is used to the lead he will thrive in a rural environment, maybe a farm, small holding or large gardens, and a family used to walking or other outside activities.

He ignores cats and can live with children over 8 years.

He is very clean in his home, very loving and affectionate, and a nice social demeanor. Used to living with a family.

name: Levi
description:
This little, cheeky chappie is Levi: 9 month old male Podenco Cross, a handsome and very friendly cross breed youngster, with a short, tan and white coat and attractive facial markings and a lovely social demeanour.

Just a little dot and he’s a bit of a whirlwind! But what a lovely little boy he is, very happy, friendly and confident little fella. Levi is only young, about 9 months old and about 12kg so just a smaller puppy boy.

Levi recently came into the rescue from a local vet found abandoned along with Raven, he’s very used to people so we think he has previously lived with a family.

Levi has recently been neutered, got his passport and vaccinations and clear blood tests so he’s ready to travel for the right family.

Levi would make a wonderful companion for a young family and equally he would do very well with any outdoor activities.We are looking for a home with or without another dog and children over 8 years old. Levi hasn’t been tested for cats yet

He is very clean in his pen, very loving and affectionate, cheeky and loves his kennel mates and playing.

name: Lawrence
description:
Lawrence is a 6 month old male Galgo cross Podenco, super-friendly youngster, with a trusting and very nice nature considering he was abandoned to fend for himself on a dried up piece of land all on his own.

We have no idea what happened to his mum or why someone would just want to throw him away.

This very attractive, friendly six month old pup is a possible Galgo cross with a Podenco, which accounts for his attractive markings, with a beautiful high stepping gait super social and positive, loves the whole world.

We are looking for a family with or without another friendly dog for Lawrence to play with and children no younger than 8 yrs. Would suit an active family. Lawrence hasnt been tested for cats yet.
