Loves to socialise, gets on very well with all dogs, loves `people, has a happy, friendly manner about him, affectionate with children, healthy condition.

name: Topi
description:
Topi is a 10-month-old medium sized cross breed boy. He is a real bundle of joy, very people-centred, affectionate and kind natured. He loves cuddles, attention, and being involved in whatever his humans are doing.

Topi is lovely friendly pup who loves to run, explore and play. He learns quickly, especially when there are treats and praise involved. His eagerness to please makes him a lovely dog to train and grow with.

He would thrive with an active family who enjoys daily walks, adventures in nature or even hiking. Topi would happily join in on family outings and would love to be part of everyday life rather than left on the sidelines.

Topi is brilliant with other dogs, and enjoys playing with them. He is very sociable and makes friends easily.

He would probably be happiest living with another friendly, playful dog, but may as well enjoy being the only dog in the home, soaking up all the attention.

Because he is still young, Topi will need time and patience to settle into a new environment and learn the routines of his new home. With kindness and guidance, he will quickly blossom into a truly wonderful companion.

He could live with older, dog-savvy children who understand how to interact respectfully with a puppy. He can also be tested with cats for the right applicant.

Topi is currently in Hungary and will travel to the UK once a home has been secured for him.

He comes fully vaccinated, microchipped, with an EU pet passport.

name: Trixy
description:
Trixy is a 1.5-year-old, smaller medium crossbreed girl with a heart full of love. She was rescued from the streets alongside her mum (a Dachshund cross) and four siblings.

Three of the pups, including Trixy, are still waiting for their happy ending in a shelter in Hungary.

Trixy is a lively, playful young girl who sometimes shows a little shyness at first. But once she feels safe, her sweet, cuddly side shines through — she only needs love, patience, and kindness to truly thrive.

She adores playing with her doggie friends and gets along well with them.

She is friendly with people, including children, and enjoys her walks, trotting happily on the lead. Trixy would be happiest in a family where she isn’t left alone for long hours and is treated as a true member of the family — joining in on adventures, cuddles, and daily life.

She could easily share her home with another playful, gentle dog, or be the only pet, soaking up all the love and attention. Like all rescue dogs, she will need time, patience, and commitment from her new family to settle and blossom into the wonderful companion she is meant to be.

Trixy is currently in Hungary and can travel to the UK once her perfect family is found. She will come spayed, fully vaccinated, microchipped, and with an EU pet passport.

Adoption is subject to a home check and adoption fee. Full RBU provided.

name: Oliver
description:
Oliver is a 0 month old male Mastin located in Weymouth.

Gorgeous cuddly puppy Oliver is a beautiful-looking Spanish Mastin boy, with a lush, cream coat and darker face.

He has recently been admitted to his foster home and he is proving to be very calm, friendly and affectionate and, as you can see in the videos, he gets on well with his new kennel mates.

He is learning fast how good it is to have human company, after he was abandoned for so long as a puppy on his own.

At the moment he stands at medium height and is approximately 10 months old. He weighs 20kg and he needs to put some weight on, which is looking very good now as he fills out. He is healthy and has a confident personality.

It’s early days in his foster home and as we get more information we can update his page.

Oliver is going to be a big boy once fully grown, and he is large now and playful, so for that reason we need to look for a home with other young dogs (or no dogs) and older children over 12 years old. He hasn’t been tested for cats

Calm and friendly with people and dogs. Still learning to walk on the lead but improving daily. So much potential!

name: Lucia
description:
Lucia is a 10 month old cross breed fostered in Derbyshire .

Lucia is an very pretty, medium-sized puppy girl Cross breed, with a fluffy off white soft coat She is a lovely gentle girl, and at times quite nervous but very affectionate and loves attention from the volunteers and enjoys being cuddled and groomed.

She gets on well with her kennel mate, but definitely prefers to be with people.

Lucia has just started her lead training which is going well, as we don’t think she is used to being walked because she came with chain round her neck and black stains on her fur so we think she’s just been chained up all her life.

Poor girl, but we are happy she is gaining confidence on a daily rate. Lucia is not cat tested yet. We really want a loving home for this girlie with another friendly dog and children over 12 years.

Very friendly with people and dogs. gentle nature, and is obedient, enjoys learning new things and a laid back.

name: Charlie
description:
Charlie is a 6 years old male cross breed located in Ipswich.

This handsome lad is Charlie, he has a glossy, black short, soft coat and he is a medium sized boy standing at only 20 inches from shoulder to paw.

Charlie is castrated and he is outgoing and loves to play. He loves cuddles and praise. Charlie is 2 years

Charlie is coming out of a home and is well trained, used to children and other dogs plus he has been with cats. He is only being rehomed for owners health reasons.

He loves toys and is playful, clean in the house, good on harness….

He needs to live with another dog preferably a quiet rural environment and needs an experienced home. He can live with children over 6 years.

name: Mora
description:
Mora is a medium English Setter, 7 years old.

Mora is one of four setters who was given up by their hunter when he became unwell and was no longer able to look after them.

She is a very energetic, confident and happy girl.

She lives with four other setters and they all get on really well together, sharing their kennel and all eating together.

Mora is very playful and is always happy play with the other dogs.

She is very affectionate and always eager for a fuss and a cuddle.

She comes running when she’s called around the shelter, although this will no doubt be different on an exciting walk!

She can live with older children and can be cat tested for the right home.

name: Simba
description:
Simba is a medium mixed breed, 8 years old.

Simba turned up in a village on his own, nobody knew where he was come from but the rescue that took him in say he was in such a state he ‘must have lived through hell’.

He arrived very skinny but is putting on weight but sadly had to have half his tail amputated due to a bad infection.

Despite whatever his past may hold, Simba is learning to enjoy life again, rolling in the grass and living with other dogs.

He gets on well with all the male and female dogs he lives with and he’s also passed his cat test, meaning he could live in a home with a resident cat as well.

name: Sky
description:
Sky is a medium English Setter, 5 years old.

Sky’s story, as with so many of our dogs, didn’t begin in the easiest way. Rescued from a hunter in Spain, she arrived at the shelter frightened, overwhelmed and unsure whether people could be trusted.

After spending some time there, one of our wonderful foster homes in Essex offered to foster her in November 2025 and that’s when her new life truly began.

While she is still naturally shy and a little sensitive, every month has brought new milestones. Watching her confidence blossom has been incredibly rewarding, and she’s now ready to find the forever home that will help her continue her journey.

Sky shares her foster home with several resident dogs, and they have been her greatest teachers. She absolutely thrives in their company and has learned that life can be fun.

One of her favourite games is playing tag with the resident English Setter – where she has gone from always being the one chased to proudly becoming the chaser herself! It’s a lovely reminder of just how far she has come.

Although Sky remains nervous around new people and unfamiliar situations, once she feels safe her affectionate nature shines through.

She loves gentle fuss and will often quietly inch closer for a cuddle. When she’s completely relaxed, she’ll happily flop onto her back, inviting you to rub her tummy, a privilege reserved for those who have earned her trust.

Like many sensitive dogs, Sky appreciates having the choice to retreat to her own safe space when she needs a little quiet time.

Giving her the freedom to move at her own pace has been key to her progress, and her future family will need to continue offering her that patience and understanding.

Sky is looking for a calm, quiet and kind home with people who won’t expect too much too soon. A confident, friendly resident dog is essential, as they will give her reassurance and confidence.

She is a truly beautiful girl, inside and out, and while she may never be the dog who greets strangers with instant enthusiasm, she will form an incredibly special bond with the people lucky enough to earn her trust.

Sky will celebrate her sixth birthday this September, and nothing would make us happier than seeing her spend it in the forever home she has waited so patiently to find.

name: Benji
description:
Benji is a small to medium sized crossbreed aged around nine years old. Benji fostered Fakenham Norfolk.

Benji was originally adopted from us a few years ago and has been very well loved and cared for. Sadly he has recently had to be very reluctantly returned due to insurmountable personal reasons.

Benji is a very sweet natured and a quiet dog. he’s no trouble whatsoever. His owners will need to be with him most of the time, as he can be anxious when being left on his own for more than a couple of hours.

He can live with respectful children aged eight plus.

Benji gets on with most dogs out and about but there are a few he takes exception to. He has lived happily as the only dog. Benji loves people, he walks well on the lead enjoy his walks.

name: Gertie
description:
Gertie is a large crossbreed aged around five years old. Gertie fostered Norwich Norfolk.

Gertie came to the UK from a squalid shelter in Romania. She is a big girl but calm and placid around the home. Gertie gets on well with other dogs and ignores the safe rescue cat. She walks nicely on a lead.

She would prefer an adult family – but visiting children is fine. Large breed experience preferred.

name: Lyric
description:
Lyric is a medium sized crossbreed aged around four years old. Lyric fostered Norwich Norfolk.

We discovered this absolute beauty queen in the Botosani public shelter when we visited in March. It’s taken a few weeks for her turn to come to arrive in the UK, she’s here now and ready for a new life.

Lyric is a sweet and gentle girl with a cheeky side that’s just beginning to emerge.

Once settled she does tend to love her human a little too much and can become possessive/protective over them if allowed to and will therefore need an adopter who is experienced with shepherd types or similar natured dogs.

Lyric is living very happily with multiple other dogs in her foster home but could become bossy if allowed to “rule the roost”.

She can live with dog savvy, confident cats but would be likely to chase more timid or skittish cats. She needs an adult home environment and would be best suited to a single person or couple.

name: Coco
description:
Coco is a 2 year old female Cross-Breed weighing 10.6kg. She is currently located in Swindon and is looking for a calm, understanding home.

Coco is an absolute sweetheart with so much love to give. She adores her people and is happiest when she’s close to them, enjoying cuddles, affection, and simply being part of family life.

She loves spending time in the garden soaking up the sunshine, enjoys her walks, and has a gentle, loving nature with those she knows and trusts.

Coco shares her home with a confident Golden Retriever, and she loves him, though her love is not always returned as he is more of an independent dog.

She takes a lot of comfort from having another dog around, so we’re looking for a home with a confident, playful resident dog who can continue to help her feel secure.

She can be left for a few hours with another dog for company but finds being completely alone much harder.

As Coco has got older, she has become more worried by strangers coming into the home and finds a busy household difficult to cope with.

With the children in her current home now growing up and friends visiting more often, it has become clear that this environment is no longer the right fit for her.

She would thrive in a calm, adult-only home with a predictable routine and few visitors, where she can feel safe and relaxed.

Out on walks, Coco can be a little unsure of unfamiliar people and dogs when on the lead, but she enjoys exploring quieter areas and has good recall in secure spaces.

Coco is looking for kind, patient adopters who will appreciate what a loving companion she is. In the right home, with another friendly dog by her side and plenty of affection from her humans, she will make a wonderfully loyal and devoted best friend.

name: Raggie
description:
Raggie is a medium sized crossbreed aged around ten years old. Raggie fostered Norwich.

Raggie was rescued from a kill shelter in Romania when he was a youngster and has lived in a private shelter since then.

Despite having a nice place to stay all these years it was not a proper home and we really want his retirement to be something more comfortable.

Raggie is a sweet and inquisitive boy who enjoys the company of other dogs and is absolutely fine with the safe rescue cat. He would like a quietish home environment with at least one other calm dog in residence.

He is not really used to a lot of human contact but is adapting well to human interaction and beginning to really seek it out. He has surpassed all expectations as we expected him to be much more independent.

He’s just a dear old chap that doesn’t really ask much out of life at all.

name: Leah
description:
Leah is a two year old female Terrier Cross who is affectionate, intelligent, and full of curiosity.

Leah didn’t have an easy start in life. As a puppy, she was kept on a balcony by an addict and only survived because kind neighbours managed to throw her food. Soon after, she was abandoned on the streets and quickly became pregnant.

Thankfully, caring neighbours stepped in and had her spayed immediately, but Leah was still struggling to cope. She would follow children around the playground, hoping someone might take her home… but no one ever did.

After a short stay in a public shelter, we took her into kennels and soon afterwards she was adopted by a lovely family. Unfortunately, we have now received the sad news that Leah needs to be rehomed as the family’s other dog is not coping with her and the current dynamics are no longer suitable.

Leah missed out on vital love and care in her early years and had to learn how to survive on the streets. Adjusting to life in a home was a big challenge, but she has come a long way. She has learned how to settle, share, play nicely and how to manage her excitement.

Leah is a funny, affectionate girl full of curiosity and intelligence. She loves mental stimulation, training games and one-to-one attention. She already knows many commands and is patient around food, waiting for instruction before eating or going through a doorway.

Walks are still very exciting for her and she can become overwhelmed in certain environments, especially around other dogs. She does have a few specific triggers that can make her reactive, but with continued support, structure and patience she has already made amazing progress and will continue to do so.

She will thrive in a calm, consistent environment where she feels safe and secure – ideally with one person or a couple who enjoy an active lifestyle and are committed to continuing her training.

Leah adores cuddles and loves to snuggle under a blanket (and occasionally dry herself off in the bedsheets!). She is an energetic, loving girl who is ready for a forever home where she can continue to grow into the best version of herself.

Leah is spayed, vaccinated, healthy and comes with full Rescue Back Up.

To apply for adoption, please complete the form on our website: https://strayswithoutborders.org.uk/adoption-process/.When contacting us, please include a phone number or a non-iCloud email address, as iCloud emails are currently bouncing and we cannot contact applicants using them.

name: Malia
description:
Malia is a medium sized crossbreed aged around two years old. Malia fostered Norwich Norfolk.

Malia and her mother were rescued from a crowded shelter in Romania. Her mother came to the UK before her and has since been rehomed.

Malia was very shy and distrustful on arrival due to lack of social interaction in her early life. She has since gotten used to her foster carers and is gentle and attentive with them though still a little hand shy.

She will continue to grow in confidence in the coming weeks and months with kindness and patience.

Malia mixes well with other dogs and will need another steady dog in full time residence in her forever home. She walks nicely on a lead but needs more practise and experience as she’s still playing a little catch up.

She doesn’t like cats and would like a home with no children under the age of twelve.

name: Cooper
description:
Cooper is a handsome 8-month-old male cross-breed who is expected to remain a small to medium-sized adult.

Cooper’s story began when he was found hiding underneath a car outside a veterinary practice. We can only imagine that someone hoped he would be found by kind-hearted people, and thankfully, that’s exactly what happened.

Since arriving at our cage-free home rescue, Cooper has shown us what a truly wonderful young dog he is.

He has a calm, gentle and affectionate nature and loves everyone he meets. He is excellent with other dogs, enjoys human company and has a lovely, easy-going personality that makes him a pleasure to have around.

Raised in a loving home environment rather than kennels, Cooper has been able to socialise naturally, build his confidence and simply enjoy being a puppy. He is healthy, happy and ready to become a much-loved member of his own family.

Cooper will travel to the UK fully prepared for adoption with his pet passport, microchip, vaccinations, blood tests, veterinary health checks and DEFRA-approved transport.

He would suit a wide variety of homes, whether as an only dog or with a friendly canine companion, and would love a family who will continue his adventures and give him the love he deserves.

We consider all applications and are always happy to discuss whether Cooper could be the right match for your home.

name: Freya
description:
Meet Freya, a 3 year old female Kokoni Cross who is looking for a calm and loving home.

She was rescued as a puppy from the streets of Macedonia and is now seeking a new home due to her family experiencing a very difficult period following a serious illness, meaning they can no longer provide the stable environment she needs.

Freya is a sensitive and intelligent dog with a big personality. She is quirky, funny and very loyal once she learns to trust someone, becoming a sweet and affectionate companion who loves being close to her people.

She enjoys playing and short bursts of running, and her charming circus paws are guaranteed to make you smile.

Due to anxiety and her difficult early life, Freya needs an experienced and patient adopter who can provide calm guidance and consistency.

She can be reactive towards unfamiliar people and some dogs, and would do best in a quieter home without cats, where her confidence can continue to grow at her own pace.

Freya manages well with a mild hip condition and lives a happy, active life with sensible exercise.

She is looking for a committed home that understands sensitive dogs and is willing to give her the time and space she needs to truly flourish.

If you feel you could offer Freya the safe and loving home she deserves, please apply using the organisation’s adoption process.

When contacting them, please include a phone number or a non-icloud email address, as icloud emails are currently bouncing and they cannot contact applicants using them.

name: Toby
description:
Toby is a 4 year old Cross-Breed boy with the look of a white teddy bear and a loving heart. Toby adores women and children, though he can be cautious with men until he learns to trust.

Life hasn’t always been easy, and busy places, traffic, and car journeys can overwhelm him. But with training and patience, he’s making real progress and enjoying calm local walks.

He can be scared of people and dogs he doesn’t know, but with gentle introduction, he can come over his fears.

At home, Toby is affectionate, loves back scratches, can be left for short periods, and relaxes well. He would thrive in a quiet, patient home—ideally countryside—with older children and the companionship of a friendly female dog.

Toby is still young, playful, and full of love, waiting for someone to help him blossom into the wonderful companion he’s meant to be.

We are looking for an experienced family, who won’t mind taking it slow with him, and continuing to work on his progress.

He would love nothing more than to chill in a calm environment and have a confident canine and human friends to help him feel more secure and to play with him.

name: Luna
description:
Meet Luna, a 5-year-old female Border Collie who was adopted in the UK but was later returned due to anxiety and reactivity issues.

After spending time in rehabilitation, she now thrives at Sakima’s Sanctuary in Blackpool, where she has made remarkable progress.

Smart, healthy, and eager to learn, Luna loves agility, obedience training, and long walks.

She needs a calm, understanding adopter who can respect her space, manage her reactivity, and continue her training.

Though affectionate on her terms, she’s not a cuddle bug and prefers gentle, minimal attention.

Luna has made great strides with other dogs and now enjoys quiet walks and car rides with her trusted people.

Her ideal home is a quiet, adult-only environment, ideally as an only dog, with adopters who enjoy rural walks and mental exercises.

Luna is spayed, vaccinated, and microchipped, ready for her forever home.

name: Willow
description:
She only has a few weeks to find a home, and we are a bit desperate. Thank you.

Willow is a sweet and affectionate 6 year old female Cross-Breed dog who thrives in a calm and quiet environment. She prefers a peaceful home with minimal visitors and enjoys a steady routine.

While she can be vocal with new people, she settles well with those she trusts. If unfamiliar guests move too quickly, she may nip at their legs, so a patient and understanding adopter is ideal.

When out on walks, Willow dislikes heavy traffic and crowded areas but does well in open spaces where she can see an exit. She’s indifferent to other dogs but does not appreciate overly excitable or bouncy ones.

At home, she is a loving companion who enjoys her early bedtime at 7:30 PM and sleeps soundly through the night. She is house-trained with very few accidents, knows basic commands like “sit” and “wait,” and has a decent recall. She has become more food-motivated, which helps with training, and she absolutely loves her outdoor walks!

Willow was diagnosed with a thyroid condition about 18 months ago, for which she requires medication (we have a 2-3 months’ supply). She has also been on calming pills due to recent home renovations but is expected to manage fine without them now that things have settled.

Recently, she surprised us by doing exceptionally well in kennels, despite initial concerns. The staff adored her, which was a big win!

name: Hazel
description:
Meet Hazel, a 3 year old female Shepherd Cross who is ready for her forever home.

Hazel has made significant progress in her behaviour thanks to a dedicated foster home, but she still requires a calm, adult-only environment to continue her journey.

Hazel is a playful dog who loves her toys and enjoys cuddles. While she takes time to warm up to new people, she is loyal and intelligent, eager to show off her tricks.

As a large, active dog, she needs around two hours of exercise each day and would thrive with a confident resident dog.

The ideal home for Hazel is one with experienced owners who can provide the ongoing training she needs. A secure garden and access to quiet walking areas are essential, as she may feel anxious in busy environments.

Additionally, her new family should be prepared to help her adjust to new surroundings, as she can be nervous at first.

Hazel is spayed, microchipped, and vaccinated, with lifelong rescue backup and support available.

If you can offer her the understanding and love she deserves, please consider giving her the forever home she’s been waiting for.

To apply for adoption, please complete the form on our website: https://strayswithoutborders.org.uk/adoption-process/.When contacting us, please include a phone number or a non-iCloud email address, as iCloud emails are currently bouncing and we cannot contact applicants using them.

name: Bodie
description:
Bodie is a 2 year old male Labrador Cross currently located in Leicestershire, UK. He is urgently seeking a very special and experienced home after being unable to settle in his current environment.

Bodie is a Labrador-sized dog, weighing approximately 27kg, with a long body and legs.

Bodie had a lively start in a Macedonian rescue, playing freely with his siblings, but he has struggled to adapt to life in his current home, particularly around children and other pets.

He is a nervous boy, which can lead to reactivity toward other dogs on walks, although he improves when on a longer lead. Bodie is also nervous around visitors to the house, sometimes snapping when he feels threatened.

He is being rehomed because his adopters found him too strong and because he is not cat-friendly. Bodie would do best in a semi-rural home with a physically fit adopter who has experience working with large reactive dogs.

He should not be rehomed with cats or children but may benefit from living with a confident resident dog.

name: Dora
description:
Dora is a 3 year old female Labrador Cross. She is looking for a forever home. She is neutered, chipped, and vaccinated. She is now moved to kennels in Preesall.

Dora is a wonderful dog and has brought her owners lots of happiness. She is incredibly clever, learns commands very quickly and loves to play!

Dora is such a playful and happy girl and has her owners laughing every day with her cheeky personality.

Dora travels well in the car and is happy just sleeping when she is left at home alone. She shows can be reactive to humans when out on walks, although has made lots of progress with this recently and is becoming much more relaxed around new people and dogs.

Visitors can be managed once she knows people well, and she does form close bonds with others outside of her family who she adores!

Dora cannot be homed with young children due to her reactivity and general bounciness. She is also very fearful of cats.

Dora can sometimes guard food and spaces but again has shown much improvement with this over recent months.

Dora has excellent recall and always wants to be near her humans. She loves being outside, playing fetch and running about.

Dora’s an absolute joy, and anyone would be very lucky to have her. We are looking for a home with experienced dog owners who can manage fear reactivity and continue on her positive journey with this.

Dora likes to say hello to other dogs but would be happier being the only dog in the house. We would also prefer a calmer household in a rural/semi-rural environment for her.

name: Chill
description:
Chill is a 1-year-old smaller medium-sized Puli girl with the softest eyes and the gentlest soul.

This beautiful girl was found abandoned alongside another Puli cross and taken to a kill shelter, where her future looked uncertain. Thankfully, she was given a second chance and came into our care.

When Chill first arrived, she was timid and reserved, unsure of the world around her. But once she settled into boarding and realised she was safe, her true personality slowly began to shine.

Today, her tail is up, she greets us with a smile, and every day she grows a little braver.

True to her name, Chill is exactly that – calm, quiet, easy-going and wonderfully relaxed. She is relaxed around other dogs, too and mingles beautifully with them, although she can feel overwhelmed by noisy or boisterous dogs.

She walks nicely on the lead and has a peaceful, gentle nature.

After being abandoned once, we would love her next chapter to be the one she truly deserves — a home where she will never be left behind again, where she is included in everyday life and can become a treasured member of the family.

Currently in Hungary and ready to travel to the UK once the right family is found.

name: Camila
description:
Camila is an approximately 5 year old female Cross-Breed of medium size, currently in kennels in Macedonia and hoping to find a home in the UK.

Camila is an affectionate, friendly, and social little dog with a very easy-going and loving temperament. She enjoys human company and forms bonds quickly, often seeking closeness and affection from people she meets.

She gets along very well with other dogs and is happy in their company. She has also been assessed as being comfortable around cats. Camila is suitable to live with children and is generally a playful and sociable girl.

Despite spending time in kennel conditions, Camila is still a cheerful girl, always happy to greet people warmly. She shows a bright, affectionate personality even in a stressful environment.

Camila is the type of dog who enjoys companionship and will likely thrive in a home where she can be included in daily life. She enjoys being near her people and would suit a home where she can be part of the family routine.

Camila has tested positive for leishmania but is not displaying any symptoms. Dogs with leishmania can often live long, happy lives, and the condition can usually be managed with appropriate treatment and monitoring.

She is ready to travel to the UK for adoption with full rescue support.

When contacting us, please include a phone number or a non-iCloud email address, as iCloud emails are currently bouncing and we cannot contact applicants using them.

All dogs rehomed via this rescue will be vaccinated, neutered/spayed, and microchipped. Home checks apply, and full post-adoption support is offered.

name: Molly
description:
Molly is an approximately 2 year old female Cross-Breed of medium size, currently in Blackpool and looking for either a foster placement or a permanent home.

Molly originally travelled to the UK and spent time in a foster home where she settled well and learned the basics of home life.

She lived with another dog, children, cats, and small animals, and was affectionate and well bonded with her family during this time.

When she later moved into an adoptive home, Molly struggled with the change in environment and the lack of consistent structure.

She became increasingly unsettled within the home, including behaviours such as patrolling and guarding areas of the house.

On walks, she also began to show the early development of reactivity. As the family felt unable to continue supporting her through these challenges, Molly was returned to the rescue and moved into kennels.

Since arriving in kennels, Molly has settled well and has shown a much more stable and relaxed temperament. She is friendly with everyone she meets and has shown no reactivity or redirected behaviours in the kennel environment.

She is a firm favourite with staff and is described as affectionate, sociable, and easy to handle.

It is expected that Molly will need a structured, consistent home environment going forward, with continued training, clear boundaries, and patient guidance to help her feel secure and supported. With this in place, she has the potential to thrive.

Molly is an affectionate and loving young dog who enjoys human company and forms strong bonds once settled.

She may need time to build confidence with new people due to her previous experiences, but she is willing to engage and responds well to calm handling.

She is an active dog who will require regular exercise and mental stimulation. She has good recall and walks nicely on the lead when settled.

Molly could potentially live with other dogs and has previously lived with cats and small animals, with appropriate introductions. A secure garden and a stable, predictable home environment will be important for her.

Molly is now ready for a committed home that can offer her structure, patience, and ongoing support so she can move forward confidently and safely.

When contacting us, please include a phone number or a non-iCloud email address, as iCloud emails are currently bouncing and we cannot contact applicants using them.

All dogs rehomed via this rescue will be vaccinated, neutered/spayed, and microchipped. Home checks apply, and full post-adoption support is offered.

name: Goldie
description:
Goldie is a 5 year old female Cross-Breed weighing approximately 48kg and is looking for her forever home from Blackpool, UK.

Goldie spent most of her life in a multi-dog household where she was settled and familiar with her routine.

When her family’s circumstances changed significantly, including long working hours and health issues, they were no longer able to meet her needs. Her world changed quickly, and she began a difficult period of instability.

She moved through a couple of foster homes but found the changes and time alone overwhelming and struggled to settle. Because of this, she eventually moved into kennels, where she has now been for several months.

Kennel life has taken time for her to adjust to, but she is gradually learning to feel safe again. With people she knows and trusts, her personality shines through.

She forms strong bonds, seeks affection, and shows a deeply loyal and loving nature once she feels secure.

She enjoys the company of familiar kennel staff and, in those moments of trust, she becomes playful, affectionate, and eager for closeness and reassurance.

She walks well on the lead and enjoys her walks once she has built a bond with her handler.

She can be wary of unfamiliar people and needs time to build trust at her own pace.

She also shows guarding instincts around her home and people, particularly when meeting new individuals, so she will need an experienced, understanding home that can manage this calmly and safely.

Because of this, Goldie is looking for an adult-only home with a quiet, predictable environment and limited visitors, ideally in a rural or low-traffic setting.

She could potentially live with calm, friendly dogs following careful introductions, as they may help support her confidence.

Goldie is not suited to a busy household, but in the right stable and experienced home, she has every chance to build a strong, trusting bond and thrive.

Meets and greets can be arranged following a successful home check.

When contacting us, please include a phone number or a non-iCloud email address, as iCloud emails are currently bouncing and we cannot contact applicants using them.

All dogs rehomed via this rescue will be vaccinated, neutered/spayed, and microchipped. Home checks apply, and full post-adoption support is offered.

name: Rocco
description:
Rocco is a medium sized crossbreed aged around two years old. Rocco fostered North Walsham Norfolk.

Rocco is from, what followers of safe rescue know to be, the barking Bush in Crivatu Romania. It is an area of shrub land known to be used to dump rubbish, furniture, and unwanted dogs.

We have a history of busting a gut to catch dumped dogs from this area before anything bad happens to them. In Rocco’s case he and one sibling escaped death by only twenty four hours.

The day after volunteers managed to catch them and get them safe, the rest of the litter were mercilessly slaughtered and left in the rubbish.

Whilst their siblings were being given a respectful burial, Rocco and his sister were being taken to a private shelter where they remained safe and cared for until we could take them here in the UK.

Rocco arrived in the UK when he was seven months old and his luck has continued to be against him since then.

Rocco has been adopted twice and returned twice for completely unavoidable reasons. The first return was due to unforeseen health issues of his single owner so there was nobody to care for him.

The second reluctant return recently was due to the other dog in the household refusing to accept him even though his loving owners tried so hard for months to make it work.

Rocco is a delightful and friendly boy who is a delightful foster. He plays beautifully within the pack but could also be the only dog in the home. He could live with respectful children aged ten plus.

name: Agika
description:
Agika is a 1 year old female Cross-Breed. She is a small mixed-breed girl looking for her forever family.

This little girl has suffered so much in her short life; still, she is a little miracle. She was rescued from a heartbreaking situation where she had been kept on a short chain that had grown deep into her neck.

Once she was released, the chain had to be surgically removed.

Despite a truly rough start, Agika is a real sweetheart—loving, affectionate, and full of life. When she was rescued, she was timid and afraid of the world.

However, she soon left her old self behind, learned that some people can be trusted, and has grown into a joyful dog who loves life, loves other dogs, and now loves people too.

She walks nicely on the lead and looks forward to her walks, which she does not get many of.

She loves attention and will happily accept any amount of cuddles or affection. Agika is ready to start a new life in a safe, loving home where she will be treated the way she has always deserved.

She wants to be part of the family and discover all the things she has not yet had the chance to see or sniff.

Experience with rescue dogs is preferred but is not essential, as all applications are assessed individually.

Agika is in Hungary and will travel to the UK once a home has been secured for her. She comes spayed and fully vaccinated, with an EU pet passport.

Adoption is subject to a home check and adoption fee. Full rescue backup is provided.

name: Cleo
description:
Cleo is a 3-month-old sweetheart, expected to grow into a small-medium or medium-sized girl.

She and her three siblings were found abandoned, all in very poor condition, terrified and unsure of the world around them.

With their parents unknown and their past full of uncertainty, all they had was each other.

But this brave little girl has already come so far. She has started to trust, to step forward instead of hide, and to show the world her sweet, gentle personality.

Cleo is timid by nature, but also playful, kind, and full of quiet charm.

Cleo loves playing with her siblings and is starting to enjoy gentle affection. She will thrive in a patient, loving home that can help her continue learning that the world is safe and people can be trusted.

Cleo comes fully vaccinated, microchipped, with an EU pet passport. Ready to travel in January.

name: Dana
description:
Dana is a 5 year old female Cross-Breed currently in Kent and looking for her forever home after spending much of her early life in kennel environments before moving into a UK foster home in 2024.

Since arriving in foster care, Dana has begun to show real progress and her confidence has grown steadily as she learns what it feels like to live in a stable home environment. Day by day, her personality has started to shine through more clearly.

Dana is an affectionate and gentle dog who enjoys close contact with people she trusts. She loves cuddles, will happily settle beside you on the sofa, and enjoys quiet companionship as well as time spent relaxing in the sun.

She also has a playful side and enjoys interacting with other friendly dogs.

She can be unsure of new situations and may find some everyday experiences a little overwhelming at times, particularly things she has not encountered before. With patience and gentle guidance, she is eager to learn and responds well to calm, positive support.

Dana can also be initially wary of men, but once she builds trust, she forms strong attachments and becomes very loving and loyal to her people.

She could live with other friendly dogs and would likely benefit from having a calm canine companion in the home. She is best suited to a home with older, dog-savvy children who understand when to give her space and allow her to settle.

Dana will thrive in a calm, patient home where she can continue building her confidence at her own pace. She will need ongoing gentle training, reassurance, and stability as she continues to adjust to home life after her early experiences.

She is now ready for a committed family who can offer her the time, understanding, and love she needs to continue her journey and finally enjoy the security of a forever home.

When contacting us, please include a phone number or a non-iCloud email address, as iCloud emails are currently bouncing and we cannot contact applicants using them.

All dogs rehomed via this rescue will be vaccinated, neutered/spayed, and microchipped. Home checks apply, and full post-adoption support is offered.

name: Kayan
description:
Kayan is a very handsome boy of 7 years young who is a large size Mastin cross.

He is very loving and affectionate dog, a real gentle soul in a large body. He is very good with other dogs and would be suitable to live with dog savvy children 12 years and older.

Kayan is a very happy dog who is very obedient and quick to learn. He is kennel clean and well behaved on the lead and is not reactive.

He loves his walks and is desperate for a family of his own, he has waited a long time.

He is neutered, inoculated, microchipped, etc and is available for meet and greets from our premises in Wistaston, Cheshire by appointment only.

name: Frida
description:
Frida is a medium size very pretty crossbreed girl of just 4 years.

She is very loving, intelligent, affectionate and obedient, a real people dog. She can live alongside other dogs but would be happier I think as an only dog.

She is very well behaved on the lead, not reactive and is confident, well behaved and responsive on her walks. Frida is kennel clean and would make a great family friend for adventures and fun.

Frida would be okay to live with dog savvy children 12 years and over. Would consider families with younger children too.

She is neutered, inoculated, microchipped, etc and is available for meet and greets from our premises in Wistaston, Cheshire by appointment only.

name: Bam Bam
description:
BAM BAM is a 9-month-old male Chow Chow Cross. He is a friendly, social young dog who weighs around 25 kg and enjoys spending time with people. He is affectionate and has a happy nature despite having a difficult start in life.

He gets along well with other dogs and is also good with children over the age of 10. His compatibility with cats is not mentioned, so this would need to be assessed in his new home.

BAM BAM would thrive in a family that can give him plenty of attention and companionship.

Before his rescue, BAM BAM was kept in a very small enclosure with pigs and chickens. His previous owner claimed that he had gone after the chickens, but since being rescued and spending time around them, he has shown no interest in harming them. This suggests that he has settled well into a more positive environment.

Despite the neglect he experienced, BAM BAM has remained loving, affectionate and eager to be around people. He enjoys human company and is looking for a family who will provide him with the love, care and security that he deserves.

A home where someone has plenty of time to spend with him would suit him best.

BAM BAM has been neutered and is vaccinated. His Brucellosis test will be sent to the UK before he travels. His adoption fee is £525, a home check is required, and rescue back-up will be provided following his adoption.

name: Tina
description:
Tina is a 5 month old female Cross-Breed.

Tina was abandoned outside a rescuer’s shelter in Romania together with her 6 siblings. They are now nearly 5 months old.

Tina can travel to the UK if she has an offer of a home. A delightful, friendly girl, she will be large size when fully grown.

She loves to play. Not cat tested. Loves other dogs. Vaccinated. She will also be tested for Brucellosis. The test is sent to the UK prior to travel.

Tina will need some lead training. Tina would suit a home with someone who is there most of the day and who can offer her a full and active life.

No small children because of her size. She would benefit from a good-sized garden. Someone who will walk with her. Tina is not spayed because of her young age.

Looking for someone with experience and patience. Adoption fee £550, a home check is requested and there is rescue back up.

name: Bruno
description:
Bruno is a 12–15 month old male Pointer currently in Spain and is expected to arrive in the UK at the end of July.

For the past 15 years, Starfish has worked alongside a wonderful shelter in Spain called Noah’s ARC.

Twice a year I travel there with my own dogs and spend several weeks helping at the shelter, and whenever possible I choose one or two dogs who I feel would really struggle to find homes in Spain and offer them a lifeline here in the UK.

Bruno was a bit of a last-minute addition to Kyle and Jackson, but sometimes a dog just quietly works his way into your heart and makes the decision for you.

I first noticed Bruno while we were carrying out an assessment on a new dog at the shelter, using parallel walking with Bruno alongside him.

The other dog was quite reactive towards Bruno, but Bruno’s response was honestly so impressive. He stayed calm, friendly and completely non-confrontational throughout, showing such a lovely, steady nature.

Earlier that same day, I had watched him being introduced into a new group of dogs. They were all a little bolshie with him at first, but again Bruno was so gentle, calm and sensible.

He took it all in his stride, read the situation beautifully and settled himself almost immediately. In a busy shelter environment, where dogs are often under pressure and overwhelmed, that sort of temperament really stands out.

Bruno came into the shelter as a stray along with his brother, who has already been lucky enough to find a home in Spain. Sadly, Bruno’s chances there are much slimmer.

Large black dogs are so often overlooked, and in Spain, they can wait a very long time for someone to notice them. He is also a slightly shy boy when he first meets new people, but there is nothing difficult about him at all.

He simply needs a moment to take things in and realise he is safe. Once he does, you can see what a lovely, sweet-natured boy he really is.

Like many of our Spanish dogs, Bruno probably has not had a great deal done with him in his short life, but what he does have is a beautiful nature and a willingness to trust.

Between now and when he travels, the volunteers in Spain will continue getting him out and about, helping him experience different situations and build his confidence, but I honestly think Bruno is the sort of dog who will adjust very quickly once he is in a home environment with kindness, patience and guidance.

Bruno is also a Pointer, so any potential home will need to understand the breed and be able to meet the needs of an active young dog.

He will need plenty of exercise, stimulation and companionship and would be best suited to adopters who appreciate that young Pointer types are not couch potatoes. They are clever, energetic dogs who thrive when given the right outlet and guidance.

From everything we have seen of Bruno so far, I do feel he would really benefit from a home with another friendly, well-balanced dog to help him settle and continue to grow in confidence.

He takes great comfort from the company of other dogs and seems to read them beautifully, so I think a canine companion in the home would help him flourish.

Bruno has been neutered, fully vaccinated and blood tested, so he will be ready to travel at the end of July once the right home is found.

He is such a lovely, gentle lad and far too nice a dog to stay unseen in kennels, waiting for prospective adopters to walk past without noticing him.

He deserves so much more than that. Bruno may not be the one shouting the loudest for attention, but sometimes it is the quiet ones with the softest souls who deserve to be noticed the most.

We will update Bruno’s profile as we get more feedback from his adventures in Spain, but for now I wanted to introduce this very special boy and show you exactly what I see in him.

Videos below so you can see Bruno for yourselves and why he stole my heart.

With Betsy, who is sharing a kennel with him

name: Darci
description:
Darci – 5 year old female Cross-Breed. She is a small to medium-sized dog currently in rescue in Ayr.

Meet Darci – the little lady ready for her chance at happiness.

Darci has spent far too long waiting for a family of her own.

Originally taken from the streets after having multiple litters of puppies, Darci was brought to safety and spayed by our rescue partners.

What should have been the start of a new life sadly turned into more waiting, as she then spent over three years living in a shelter. Now, at last, Darci is in Ayr and ready to find the forever home she deserves.

Darci is a delightful little dog with a gentle, loving nature. She can be a little shy when meeting new people, but once she realises she’s safe, all she wants is affection and companionship.

She has a sweetness about her that is impossible not to fall in love with.

She’s lovely with both people and other dogs and has already started learning about life beyond the shelter.

Darci has done a little lead walking and is making good progress, but she will need a family willing to continue her training and help her build confidence in the world around her.

As Darci has never lived in a home before, her new family will need to be patient and understanding while she learns all the things most dogs take for granted, such as house training, household routines, and settling into family life.

* A calm, patient family willing to help her adjust to home life* A secure, enclosed garden where she can safely build confidence* Owners who understand that she may take a little time to settle* A home with or without another friendly dog* Older, sensible children only, as younger children may find her shyness overwhelming

Darci is currently carrying a few extra pounds, so she would also benefit from a family who can help her enjoy gentle exercise and a healthy lifestyle.

What Darci lacks in confidence, she more than makes up for in kindness. She has waited years for someone to choose her, and we know that with a little love, time, and patience, she will become the most wonderful family companion.

A successful home check and adoption donation will apply. Potential adopters must be willing to travel to Ayr to meet and collect Darci.

Let’s make sure Darci never has to wait for a family again.

name: Woody
description:
Woody – 1 year old male Golden Retriever Cross-Breed.

Meet Woody – a golden boy with a heart full of love.

If you’re looking for a dog who wakes up every morning excited to see what adventures the day will bring, then Woody might just be your perfect match.

This handsome young lad is an absolute delight. Full of love, joy, and enthusiasm for life, Woody has quickly become a favourite with everyone who meets him.

He approaches life with a wagging tail, a happy heart, and a firm belief that every person he meets is a potential new best friend. Woody currently lives alongside our famous “Wonky Zone” dogs, including some rather colourful canine personalities, and he gets along beautifully with every single one of them.

He has a wonderful nature and seems to have a natural ability to fit in wherever he goes. At night, Woody sleeps happily in the house and has proven himself to be a very good house guest.

He is clean overnight, has not been destructive, hasn’t chewed anything he shouldn’t, and has shown no interest in escaping. For such a young dog, he’s been remarkably sensible.

* Friendly and affectionate with everyone he meets* Loves other dogs* Sleeps well in the house* Clean overnight* Non-destructive* Full of fun and enthusiasm* Loves cuddles and human company* A happy, confident youngster

Woody has started learning to walk on a lead and is making good progress.

At the moment, he can get a little overexcited, occasionally spinning around because he’d rather stop for cuddles than continue walking.

He’s still a young dog and will need ongoing training and guidance, but he is eager to learn and loves spending time with people.

Woody could fit into many different types of homes.

He could live with another friendly dog or be equally happy as the centre of attention.

He could also live with children, although due to his age and exuberance he can be quite bouncy and jumpy, so interactions should always be supervised and children should be dog-savvy.

His new family will need to be committed to continuing his training, helping him develop good manners, and showing him all the wonderful things life has to offer.

Woody is one of those dogs who simply makes people smile.

He has a joyful outlook on life, a gentle heart, and so much love to give.

He’s the kind of dog who will happily join you on adventures during the day and then curl up beside you for cuddles in the evening.

With a little guidance and continued training, he is going to make an incredible family companion.

If you think you could offer Woody the loving home he deserves, please visit the rescue website.

We can’t wait to see this wonderful boy find the family he’s been waiting for.

name: Zack
description:
Zack – 8 year old male Staffordshire Bull Terrier Cross Bulldog.

Meet Zack – the gentleman looking for his next chapter.

Weight: 29kg (though he wouldn’t mind losing a few pounds!)

Status: Neutered, flea and worm treated, vaccinations to be restarted.

Sometimes the very best dogs find themselves needing a second chance. Sweet Zack recently lost the only home he had ever known when his beloved owner sadly passed away after a spell in hospital.

Through no fault of his own, Zack suddenly found himself alone, wondering where his person had gone and why life had changed so dramatically.

For a month, his owner’s son visited twice a day to walk and care for him, but Zack spent the rest of his time waiting patiently at home.

When it became clear that the family could not take him on and no rescue spaces were available elsewhere, we knew we had to find a way to help. Thankfully, Zack’s luck changed when a wonderful foster home stepped forward.

Since arriving in foster, he has shown everyone exactly what an incredible dog he is. Zack is quite simply an angel.

He has settled beautifully with the resident dogs, slept through the night, asked to go outside for the toilet, enjoyed a pampering bath, and even celebrated his fresh start with his first-ever pup cup at the café.

Everywhere he goes, people fall in love with him.

* Loves absolutely everyone he meets* Friendly and affectionate* Generous with kisses and cuddles* Great with other dogs* Clean in the house* Walks beautifully on a lead* Has reliable recall off lead* Loves trips to the beach and park* Happy to accompany you on adventures* A calm, well-mannered companion

Although Zack is now entering his senior years, he still enjoys getting out and about and being involved in family life. He’s not ready for retirement in front of the television just yet!

The one thing Zack misses most is companionship.

Having always been part of a family, we’d love to find him a home where someone is around for much of the day. Whether that’s a retired couple, someone who works from home, or a family with a flexible lifestyle, Zack will thrive where he can enjoy company, affection, and plenty of adventures.

While Zack is friendly around children, due to his age we would prefer a home without very young children. Older, sensible children who understand how to respect a dog’s space would be absolutely fine.

Zack has settled beautifully alongside other dogs in foster care, and we think he would love the companionship of a friendly canine companion in his new home.

Equally, he would be happy as an only dog provided he receives plenty of love and attention.

He could happily fit into many different households and would make a wonderful addition to a lucky family.

After losing everything he knew, all Zack wants is a sofa to snooze on, people to love, and a place to call home once again.

A successful home check and adoption donation will apply. Potential adopters must be willing to travel to Ayr to meet and collect Zack.

Let’s find this special boy the happy ending he so deserves.

name: Archie
description:
Archie is a very small traditional 10-12 year old male Staffordshire Bull Terrier that just wants to snuggle with you!

Archie was taken in by us when he was left unclaimed in the pound, we later tracked down the owner and it was decided he was best finding a new home to live out the rest of his days in.

Archie is a lovely gentle old boy that doesn’t want for much at all, food in his belly, a soft bed to sleep, a small potter around the block or garden, and cuddles.

Archie has torn both cruciate ligaments at some point in his life and this doesn’t appear to have been treated so he is arthritic, which is to be expected given he is an older boy.

Archie is without a doubt the sweetest little old boy,he is not giddy or bouncy, he is very happy to plod around and loves sleeping on his bed, he loves mine even more and with a face and smile like his, it’s very hard to say no!!

He is perfectly clean in the house, is not destructive and can be left for short periods without any problems. He walks well on the lead and is an absolute pleasure in every way.

He is fine with other dogs, he is currently being fostered with other dogs of all shapes and sizes and loves them all. He is also fine with children. Archie travels perfectly, in fact he just wants to curl up in comfort and sleep!

Archie is a very undemanding little guy that would make a fantastic addition to any home, he is the sweetest most adorable little snuggle bug you will ever meet.

All he wants is to curl up with you for cuddles! He can be a bit whingey as is typical of the staffy, when he wants something or is excited, but he does stop when you tell him to, and its usually dinner or wee time!

Do you have room in your home and heart for our beautiful Archie? Whatever love you give will be returned ten fold by this little old gent.

He is castrated and had a lump removed from his chin, he is flea, wormed, and microchipped, he also had his teeth cleaned and didn’t need any extractions.

name: Rio
description:
Rio – 11 month old male Labrador Retriever Cross Akita. He is a gorgeous young dog looking for his forever home where he can truly be part of the family.

Rio loves people and other dogs and is currently in foster with multiple dogs, which he is really enjoying.

Therefore, we will be searching for a home with another canine companion that he can play with.

Although he is only 11 months old, he is very well-mannered. He is house-trained but, as usual, may have a few accidents at first until he settles into his new home.

Due to his age, he would benefit from an active family.

He can live with dog-savvy children. Due to being playful and enthusiastic, he may accidentally knock over small children, but this is never malicious.

Rio is described as a medium-sized dog and weighs around 23kg.

If Rio sounds like the pup for you, please get in touch.

name: Tony
description:
Tony is a 1-year-old medium-sized crossbreed boy with a glossy black coat, a permanently happy smile, and a personality to match.

This charming young boy lives in a shelter in Hungary, where he patiently waits for the day someone chooses him to be part of their family.

Tony has recently come to the conclusion that he might just be the complete package.

Handsome, young, friendly with everyone he meets, wonderful with children, great with other dogs, and blessed with the sweetest, most affectionate nature — he really does tick all the boxes.

In fact, Tony’s reasons for wanting a home are surprisingly selfless. He’s become such a favourite with the shelter’s dog walkers that everyone wants to take him out, leaving some of his kennel mates with fewer opportunities for walks and attention.

Being the kind-hearted soul that he is, Tony feels it’s only fair to solve the problem by finding a forever home of his own. That way, he gets the family he deserves, and his friends get a turn in the spotlight too.

Whether it’s family adventures, lazy sofa days, playtime in the garden, or simply being your loyal shadow, Tony is ready for it all. He’s adaptable, sociable, and has the sweetest nature you could wish for.

Tony comes neutered, fully vaccinated, treated for parasites and with an EU pet passport.

Home Check and Adoption Fee applies. Full RBU provided.

name: Toffee
description:
Toffee is a medium sized crossbreed aged around eighteen months old. Toffee fostered Norwich Norfolk.

Toffee came into our care a few months ago from another rescue who we regularly work with. Sadly they had no foster place for him and he was not progressing in a kennel environment.

He arrived, like many have before him, scared of humans because his last experiences with them hadn’t been good ones. It took many weeks to get him to walk on a lead and even longer to accept human touch.

Finally we feel he is ready to find his forever home.

He is now a fun loving and cheeky chap who enjoys the company of the humans he’s learnt to trust. He will need an experienced adopter who will be willing to follow advice and wait patiently for that trust to extend to them too.

Believe me he is worth the wait! He is playful and friendly with other dogs and will need at least one full time canine companion in his forever home.

He could live with respectful children aged twelve plus.

If your dog pulls on the lead, then we can advise you on training methods that avoid harm.

name: Tilly
description:
Tilly – 2-year-old Cockapoo girl (approx. 10kg)

Currently being fostered in Gloucestershire in an adult family home with multiple dogs.

Tilly was originally homed by Starfish as a puppy and has recently come back into our care due to a change in her owner’s circumstances.

This is absolutely no reflection on lovely little Tilly, who has done nothing wrong. We are heartbroken for her, as returns are something that very rarely happen within our rescue.

When Tilly first arrived, our hearts broke for her. For several days she sat by the door waiting, barely eating and seeming very sad and withdrawn.

It was clear she missed everything she had known and was struggling to understand why her world had suddenly changed.

Her coat was also in poor condition and our wonderful groomer spent nearly three hours carefully clipping it all off. Throughout the entire process, Tilly was incredibly patient and good.

Fast forward a couple of weeks and we now have a very different Tilly – a happy, confident little girl who has settled beautifully into a home with six resident dogs of different ages, sizes and personalities without a single issue.

She has even attended agility classes and proved to be a natural!

There is honestly very little negative to say about this lovely dog.

“Tilly is the easiest dog I’ve fostered to date. She met my six dogs all at once, which can be intimidating, but took it all in her stride. She is affectionate and enjoys human company, but has also learnt to settle happily when a person is not in the room.

She loves routine, enjoys her two walks a day and is a bright little dog who would thrive in a home where she can continue to use her brain. She is inquisitive, confident and wants to say hello to every person and dog she meets.

She settles well at night and sleeps through without any problems.

She walks nicely on the lead and although interested in wildlife, she has not been overly fixated on it. She has not yet been let off lead, so we cannot comment on her recall.

One thing she does go deaf for, however, is a particularly interesting smell she wants to investigate or roll in!

She is pretty much the perfect dog. I feel she would be happiest in a home with another dog and ideally more than one person around, ensuring she receives plenty of companionship and attention.”

We know Tilly will be extremely popular. However, as you can imagine, we are very protective of this special little girl.

We watched her arrive feeling lost and confused, and we have seen her blossom back into the happy, joyful puppy we remember from two years ago.

Because of this, we will not compromise when it comes to finding her the right forever home.

Tilly has been spayed, vaccinated, microchipped, wormed and flea treated.

Below are various videos showing Tilly’s journey with us so far. ??

name: Sombra
description:
Sombra is a 5-year-old female Podenco Maneto. She is a small-sized dog with a friendly and sociable nature. While she enjoys spending time with people, she also has an independent side that allows her to be content in her own company.

She loves going for walks and enjoys exploring her surroundings. Sombra is inquisitive when out and about and likes meeting new people. Her curious personality means she takes an interest in the world around her without being overwhelming.

Sombra is not reactive and is well behaved on the lead. She walks nicely and remains calm when encountering different situations.

Her good manners make her a pleasant companion for daily outings and adventures.

She is good with other dogs and could happily share her home with canine companions. Sombra could also live with dog-savvy children aged 10 years and over.

Her friendly temperament and balanced nature help make her a wonderful family dog.

Sombra is kennel clean and has all the qualities of a loving family companion. She is neutered, inoculated and microchipped. She is available for meet and greets by appointment from the rescue premises in Wistaston, Cheshire.

Sombra is ready to find a caring home where she can enjoy companionship, walks and family life.

name: Dante
description:
Dante is a 6-year-old male Cross-Breed. He is a medium-sized dog who is described as handsome, loving and affectionate. Dante is also obedient and quick to learn, making him a wonderful companion for a family looking for a friendly and sensible dog.

He is very good on the lead and has no known fears. Dante is sociable with all dogs and enjoys being around other canine companions.

His happy nature and confident personality help him settle well into different situations.

Dante is kennel clean and has no health issues reported. He is a cheerful and well-balanced dog who appears to take life in his stride.

His affectionate temperament means he enjoys spending time with people and forming strong bonds with those around him.

He could live with other dogs in his new home and would be suitable for families with dog-savvy children aged 12 years and over. Consideration may also be given to families with younger children.

His loving and sensible nature suggests he would enjoy being part of an active and caring household.

Dante has been neutered, inoculated and microchipped. He is available for meet and greets by appointment from the rescue premises in Wistaston, Cheshire. He is a happy, affectionate and sociable dog who is ready to find a loving forever home.

name: Dora
description:
Dora is a medium English Setter, 11 years old.

Dora is a beautiful older girl who has found herself looking for a new home after being surrendered simply because she is now considered too old to hunt.

But don’t let her age fool you – Dora still has plenty of love, energy, and joy to share.

She is sociable and gets along wonderfully with other dogs. Dora currently shares her kennel with Boss, a fellow ‘senior’ setter, and the two have formed a lovely companionship. They enjoy spending time together and love playing together in the enclosed field.

With people, Dora can be a little shy at first as she takes a moment to feel safe with new faces.

However, once she gets to know you, she quickly blossoms into a cheerful, affectionate, and loving companion.

Dora is still quite active but would love to find a home where she can enjoy gentle exercise, companionship, and a comfortable sofa.

Currently in Spain, transport available to the UK.

name: Boss
description:
Boss is a medium English Setter, 10 years old

Boss is a charming older gentleman who is looking for a loving home after being given up due to his age.

For his age, Boss is still active and enjoys stretching his legs and running in the enclosure, especially alongside his friend Dora. The two share a lovely bond and make a delightful pair.

Boss loves his food and has a great appetite. He has a calm, balanced nature and is incredibly affectionate with people, always seeking out attention.

He absolutely adores cuddles making him a perfect companion for anyone looking for a loving, loyal friend.

Boss would thrive in a home where he can enjoy both relaxed companionship snuggled on a sofa, and some gentle walks.

Currently in Spain, transport available to the UK.

name: Zeus
description:
Zeus is a medium English Setter, 6 years old.

Zeus is a gentle, loving boy who is now thriving in one of our amazing foster homes in Kent.

Since arriving in the UK at the end of May, he has settled in beautifully. He has done really well with toilet training with no accidents in the home.

He is learning to walk well on the lead without pulling, and loves a bit more freedom on a long line in the park. However he is likely to take a step or two backwards with training when moving home.

Zeus is sociable with other dogs and currently lives with two resident dogs. He particularly enjoys the company of the female setter and loves to play in the garden with her followed by a snuggle on the sofa.

We think he would be fine as an only dog or happy to have a playmate in his new home.

His foster family describe him as an absolute gem. A sweet natured and incredibly affectionate boy, who is eating well and adapting steadily to family life.

He instantly fell in love with the foster family’s daughter when she returned home from university and has shown what a wonderful family dog he can be. He is especially gentle and affectionate with her.

Zeus may be six, but he’s got loads of energy for running and playing and then, in true setter style, settles beautifully at home.

name: Reyna
description:
Reyna is a medium Border Collie, 9 years old.

Reyna is a beautiful Border Collie in foster in Cambridgeshire, her passport says she is 9 years old, although her vet believes she may actually be younger.

Reyna came into the shelter with her three puppies, Ares, Sena and Zeus, after being surrendered by a family who had kept her outdoors for her entire life.

We believe she may have spent much of that time chained up, with very little opportunity to interact with people, other dogs or the world around her.

Her puppies are all in wonderful homes now, and although Reyna is going from strength to strength, we would love to find her a home of her own.

When Reyna first arrived at the shelter, she was extremely frightened and overwhelmed by everything around her.

Reyna was at the shelter for a few months before one of our wonderful foster homes offered to foster her in June 2025 and Reyna has been there since.

She shares her home with several resident dogs and, despite likely having very limited socialisation in her previous life, she has learned so much from them. She had many fears and insecurities and found it difficult to trust people.

Since then, she has made wonderful progress and now enjoys gentle attention and affection from those she knows.

However, she is still a sensitive girl who will need a patient, understanding home where she can continue to build her confidence at her own pace.

Once she feels safe and familiar with someone, she is incredibly sweet, affectionate and eager to be close to them.

Although she was very nervous when she first arrived in foster care, Reyna has slowly but surely started to come out of her shell.

With the support of her foster family and input from a behaviourist, she has even enjoyed some small adventures, including trips to a local café.

While she can still be shy around new people, she is gentle and affectionate once she feels comfortable. Her foster mum is undoubtedly her favourite person, especially as she’s the one who takes her on all her walks!

As her confidence grows, Reyna’s personality is beginning to shine through. She has a playful, cheeky side and can be quite the little character.

Watching her discover new experiences and learn that the world can be a safe and enjoyable place has been incredibly rewarding.

She gets along with all of the resident dogs, although the other resident Collie is a particular favourite and the two have formed a lovely bond.

Reyna is an extremely special girl looking for a kind, patient and understanding family who can continue the fantastic work started by her foster home.

With time, reassurance and gentle encouragement, we believe she will continue to flourish and be an incredible loving member of the family to someone.

Reyna currently lives with a cat, so can do again providing they both have their own space and adopters taken introductions slowly. She would do best in a home without young children. She can live with other dogs or as an only dog.

name: Demis
description:
Demis is a 2-year-old male Pointer Cross currently waiting in Cyprus for his forever home in the UK. This gorgeous boy is bursting with love, energy, and the potential to be someone’s perfect companion.

Friendly and affectionate, Demis thrives on human connection and would do well either as the only dog or living alongside a resident dog. He’s all about that family life—he just hasn’t found his yet.

He still needs some basic training and socialisation, especially when it comes to walking nicely on the lead (yep, he’s a bit of a puller). But don’t let that fool you—he’s not destructive and can be left alone for short periods without turning your house into a war zone.

A spacious garden where he can zoom around would be his absolute dream.

Demis is suitable for homes with children aged 12 and up, and with the right guidance, he’s going to be an incredible addition to the family.

He’ll come vaccinated, travel-ready, and with full rescue back-up, because this boy isn’t just being shipped off—he’s being set up for success. If you’re up for a bit of training and a whole lot of love, Demis could be your guy.

name: Russett
description:
Russett – 4 year old male Cross-Breed is medium in size weighing twenty-two kilograms.

What a cool guy Russett is! From the moment he entered the shelter as a stray, he was a perfect shelter dog. Such striking and handsome markings, many comment on just how handsome a dog he is too, but there’s more to him than just a pretty face.

He has a calm personality, other dogs are drawn to him for this reason.

A gentle character, full of strength though and maturity and confidence in himself.

The shelter team say Russett thinks he’s a lapdog! Climbing onto staff and visitors’ laps to get as much love as possible. Hugely cuddly and loving, Russett struggles sometimes to recognise personal space! He adores the attention when he gets it.

The shelter team describe him as a very good all-round dog.

He’s a great boy with other dogs, he prefers those who are more calm in nature as he is.

He’s loving and hugely sociable with everyone he meets.

Russett is quite food orientated which is great for training.

He enjoys any interactions where there’s a treat involved!

Pulls a little on walks but soon calms down.

He doesn’t have high energy levels, in fact he would be happy with a nice walk then a snooze, followed by some cuddles on the sofa. His needs aren’t great or demanding.

Russett would enjoy a household that had a chilled feel about it like him. He’d be happy to share his space with another dog of a similar energy level, maybe even living with older dogs would suit him.

Russett would be the happiest boy if he had humans around him who realised his greatest need in life is to be loved by his people. Tactile, loving and affectionate adopters are a must for this boy.

Only what he deserves now after patiently waiting for the right people to find him.

Rescue back up by Saving Souls Animal Rescue, a UK registered charity.Adoption and travel fees apply.Homecheck applies.Fully vaccinated and tested/neutered.He has his passport/ready to fly!

name: Dave
description:
Meet delicious Dave — the cuddly charmer from Cyprus! Dave is a 1 year old male Labrador Retriever Cross, medium-sized at around 20 kilograms. A firm favourite of the shelter team, this little fella hasn’t put a paw wrong since he arrived as a stray.

Hugely loving, immensely cuddly, and incredibly affectionate — Dave’s capacity to love knows no bounds! This doesn’t just extend to his human companions but to his furry friends too.

Dave seeks out kisses and cuddles from anyone willing to share them, nuzzling into people’s shoulders for a proper full-body hug.

With his doggy companions, he’s equally sociable and kind — welcoming new dogs to the shelter with open paws, initiating play with those who want to, or sitting calmly with the ones who are a bit unsure.

Dave is such a great all-round kid — balanced and happy in equal measures.

His ideal home would be an active one that understands he’s young, playful, and full of life. He’ll thrive with physical and mental stimulation, and a large secure garden is a must — he’s known to be a bit of an escape artist! In a loving home, that urge may fade, but high fencing will still be important just in case Dave decides to test his skills.

Dave would be happy living with another dog or as an only dog. A companion dog would be lovely for him, but he’d also enjoy meeting other dogs for playtime.

He could live with children over 10 years old and hasn’t been cat tested.

Dave is young, with medium energy levels, and like most shelter rescues, will benefit from further training. The rewards, though, will be plentiful — Dave will give back a lifetime of love. Seems like a fair exchange to us!

Rescue back-up by Saving Soula Animal Rescue, a UK-registered charity.Adoption and travel fees apply. Home check required. Fully vaccinated, neutered, passport ready, and all set to fly!

name: Seiko
description:
